news_summary: Investopedia reports Freeport-McMoRan shares jumped as copper and gold prices rose. Copper futures climbed about 4% after China unveiled stimulus aimed at supporting sluggish housing and financial markets, boosting expected demand. The piece also notes Fed rate cuts weaken the dollar, further supporting copper and gold prices. | A London-listed ACG Metals executive says he is buying the Gediktepe mine in Turkey as a first step to build a copper-focused portfolio. The mine is set to become largely copper, supported by a $145m expansion. The move reinforces EV-driven copper demand optimism, boosting copper sentiment. | Western Metallica announced the start of a first drilling program in October at its Luz Maria copper (Cu-Mo) porphyry prospect within the Caña Brava project in Peru, following permits. The news is an exploration sentiment driver with no immediate supply or demand change; nickel and aluminium are not addressed. | Indonesia inaugurated two major copper smelters: PT Amman Mineral’s West Sumbawa refinery (900,000 tonnes of copper concentrate capacity per year) and a PT Freeport smelter in Gresik (processing 1.7 million tonnes from Papua). The move supports Indonesia’s downstreaming push and strengthens near-term copper processing capacity expectations. | GGL Resources announced Teck’s option agreement for GGL’s Le Champ copper-molybdenum-gold porphyry target in Nevada, including cash option payments and a production-decision milestone. The release supports potential future copper/molybdenum development but provides no near-term output, so any immediate copper price impact is likely limited. | Copper Fox reported its Q3 2024 operating and financial results, including continued geotechnical and exploration progress at several copper projects (Van Dyke, Mineral Mountain, Sombrero Butte, Eaglehead) and geotechnical drilling at Schaft Creek. The company highlighted ongoing metallurgical testwork and permitting timelines; no production-impact events were noted. | Koryx Copper announced a CAD$10 million non-brokered private placement to fund advancement of its Haib copper project in Namibia and Luanshya West/Mpongwe projects in Zambia, plus working capital. Shares are subject to a four-month hold and TSX-V approval. It is not an immediate supply shock. | Bayhorse Silver Inc. announced an underground diamond drilling program targeting a low-resistivity anomaly under its Oregon Bayhorse Silver Mine for a potential porphyry copper deposit. The company links the anomaly to a low-resistivity porphyry model and plans downhole geophysics after drilling to refine mineralization profiles.

South Africa is facing an epidemic of armed copper theft, stripping copper from power stations, cables and rail infrastructure and damaging public assets. Authorities have struggled to contain the problem. At the same time, expanding green technologies such as electric vehicles, wind and solar is cited as boosting overseas copper demand. | Kyrgyzstan’s exports of copper ore and concentrates to China fell 37% in the January–August 2024 period, according to Tazabek. The reduced shipment flow suggests less copper concentrate supply into the Chinese market, which can support expectations of tighter copper availability. | Cobre and a BHP subsidiary have agreed to exclusively negotiate an earn-in joint venture to explore Cobre’s Kitlanya West and East copper-silver projects in Botswana. The deal follows BHP’s Xplor-funded seismic survey, with results expected end of the quarter. It targets potential Tier 1 discoveries but remains subject to approvals and paperwork. | Marula Mining updated its Kinusi Copper Mine plan to use a two-phase processing route, aiming for LME-grade copper cathode. Open-pit activity starts in Q4 2024, with high-grade concentrate production planned for Q1 2025 and Phase 2 test work continuing into commissioning. The Board approved the plan. | Aurubis said its European refined copper premium stays unchanged at $228/t for 2025, despite record low concentrate treatment charges and expectations of a larger premium increase. Sources cited tight European supply but also weak local demand. The article also notes falling Grade A cathode premiums and sharply lower concentrate TCs. | Pacific Ridge Exploration completed its first drill program at the Chuchi copper-gold project in British Columbia, reporting copper mineralization (chalcopyrite-pyrite) in every diamond drill hole across a 750 m strike length at the BP Zone. Assays are pending, with potential for expansion at depth. | Londian Wason, an electrolytic copper foil maker, signed an MOU to develop a high-end copper foil project in Malaysia’s Kuantan Industrial Park. The facility will produce copper foil for EV battery applications and copper clad laminates, supporting growing demand tied to EV and energy storage. | Copper rallied above US$10,000 on the LME, driven by China’s Politburo pledging real-estate stabilization and “forceful” rate cuts, plus a potential large capital injection into state banks. U.S. growth data also improved risk appetite. Aluminium rose about 2% alongside copper strength. | The only available information is the headline reporting that Comex copper futures settled up 3.46% at $4.5810. This points to bullish near-term price pressure in copper. However, the page content containing the underlying causes is missing, limiting confidence about specific drivers. | Producers of raw materials rallied as markets priced in potential further Chinese stimulus. Copper futures jumped 3.5% on Comex, the biggest gain since 2022, with strategists expecting follow-up fiscal measures in 2–4 weeks. A weaker U.S. dollar also supported broad risk appetite and commodity prices.

Pan Asia Metals launched an induced-polarisation (IP) geophysical survey at its Rosario copper-silver project in Chile to map “manto-style” copper mineralisation ahead of maiden drilling. It also starts geochemical exploration, with about 27 line kilometres of DCIP planned before RC drilling later in the year. | Carnaby Resources reported results from a VTEM magnetic survey at the Greater Duchess copper-gold JV, identifying multiple new undrilled conductive targets and highlighting the Mohawk corridor with prior 21m at 2% copper. Management expects immediate drilling focus and possible resource expansion, but no near-term output change is provided. | Londian Wason plans to launch a new Malaysia facility in the Malaysia-China Kuantan Industrial Park to produce high-end electrolytic copper foils for EV batteries and copper clad laminates. The move supports Southeast Asian EV supply expansion and suggests increased copper-related production and demand activity. | Reuters reports mining companies are struggling to pay premiums for pure-play copper assets as copper demand expectations keep valuations elevated. It cites robust outlook tied to EV and data-center/AI demand, while noting benchmark copper has recently pulled back from record highs. The sentiment remains supportive for copper. | Schneider Electric partnered with Glencore to decarbonise the copper supply chain, improve procurement standards for low-emission capital equipment, and explore greater recycled copper content and closed-loop solutions. The firms also plan enhanced energy monitoring and electrification services for Glencore’s raw material supply, aiming to lower emissions rather than change near-term output. | FireFly Metals raised A$65m via a share placement to fund an expanded drilling and exploration program at its Green Bay copper-gold project in Canada. The company plans additional 100,000m of drilling, supported by underground access upgrades and new geophysical targets. The deal supports future copper supply expectations but not immediate production changes. | Panama is reportedly considering exporting the first batch of First Quantum’s copper that is already stockpiled. If exports restart or increase soon, it can improve near-term supply flow visibility and support market sentiment for copper prices over the coming days to weeks.

Pan Asia Metals reports promising early “visual” copper indications at its Rosario project in Chile, including malachite mineralisation from soil and rock-chip sampling and new induced-polarisation surveys. Results are pending lab confirmation, with follow-on drilling planned later, keeping the near-term impact on copper prices limited. | BT said it received £105m as the first upfront prepayment under a forward agreement to sell copper granules made from leftover UK copper telecom cables. The copper comes from exchange clearance as BT/Openreach replace copper with fibre, with a multi-year target of recovering up to 200,000 tonnes through the 2030s. | BHP forecasts global copper demand to increase by an additional 1 million metric tons per year on average until 2035, supported by copper-intensive technologies in energy transition and digital sectors. It also flags constrained supply due to declining ore grades and higher costs, implying tighter fundamentals. | Kincora Copper finalized an earn-in and joint venture with Woomera for its Bronze Fox copper-gold project in Mongolia, enabling up to $4m spend for an 80% interest. Drilling is set to begin with a 2,250–3,250m program and a second mining license application, targeting resource expansion. | Freeport’s Manyar copper smelter in Indonesia will not deliver initial output until November after water and steam leakage issues during startup tests. The delay may reduce near-term refined copper supply expectations. Manyar was expected to begin in September, with samples for client testing in October. | Copper surged on hopes of China stimulus and tightening Shanghai stocks, but Reuters warns the fundamentals point to ample supply. The ICSG projects large global surpluses for 2024-2025 and forecasts rising mine and refined output. Exchange stocks are high and contango persists, indicating no immediate copper shortage. | The DRC government reportedly says Kamoto Copper owes about €800m in unpaid royalties/taxes and is investigating the company’s accounts. Authorities previously froze local bank accounts and sealed Kamoto’s warehouse tied to metal storage/export, though production was reportedly uninterrupted. The dispute raises near-term copper shipment and supply risk.

Metso secured a large equipment order from Barrick Gold for expansion work at the Lumwana copper mine in Zambia, supplying equipment for grinding, flotation, thickening, feeding and filtration for a concentrator plant. Lumwana is projected to reach about 240,000 tonnes of copper per year, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Noronex started a 5,000m reverse-circulation drilling program at its Fiesta copper project in Namibia to test several mineralised shear zones. The company cites strong historical copper intercepts and will later move the rig to its Damara project, funded via a South32-linked JV/earn-in. | Power Nickel announced assay results from the first five holes of its summer 2024 drilling at the polymetallic Lion Zone discovery. Reported intervals range from about 5–12 metres with copper-equivalent grades between 2.36% and 5.93%, which may strengthen expectations for future nickel and copper resource development. | Power Nickel reported assay results from the Lion Zone discovery, including multiple copper- and nickel-bearing intercepts (e.g., several meters of %Cu and sub-% Ni, plus CuEq). While positive drill results can support sentiment for future nickel and copper supply, the impact is likely small given the exploration stage. | Australia’s copper production is expected to fall 4.7% in 2024 to 765.7 kt, driven by closures of Sandfire’s DeGrussa and Monty and lower output from several Glencore and Newmont mines. Some restarts and modest increases won’t prevent the aggregate decline. Recovery is expected in 2025 from new projects. | Global copper production is forecast to rise 3.2% in 2024 to a record 22.9 million tonnes, supported by expansions and ramp-ups across major producers including Chile (Quebrada Blanca), the DRC (Tenke Fungurume, Kamoa-Kakula), and Russia (Ak-Sug, Udokan), along with growth in China and Zambia. | QC Copper announced an all-share acquisition of Cuprum, adding the Thierry Copper Project and increasing its copper resource base. The Thierry asset historically produced copper and contains minor nickel, with references to past concentrate shipments. The deal is positioned for future development, not immediate production or supply changes. | Orecap announced QC Copper’s offer to acquire Cuprum in an all-share deal, adding the Thierry copper project and increasing QC Copper’s copper resource base. The transaction, expected to close by end-December 2024, strengthens Canada’s copper developer footprint. Thierry also historically produced concentrate with minor nickel content. | Copper Fox announced it retained Stantec to complete a hydrogeology characterization and conceptual site model for its Van Dyke ISCR oxide copper project in Arizona. The work reviews historical data, updates groundwater requirements, and supports advancement toward a preliminary feasibility study, but no immediate production or supply change is stated. | Metso was awarded an ~€70m contract to supply process equipment for Barrick Gold’s Lumwana copper project expansion in Zambia. The scope covers grinding, flotation, thickening, feeding and filtration. The expansion is positioned to boost Lumwana’s copper output, supporting sentiment for copper supply growth, despite longer construction timelines. | BHP said the energy transition should lift copper demand by about 1 million tonnes per year until 2035, with demand growth expectations rising over time. It also flags supply challenges from falling ore grades and rising production costs, implying a future supply deficit that could support copper prices. | Trivariate Research says China’s aggressive stimulus announced in late September has been incrementally bullish for US equities, citing stronger moves in US-listed copper stocks such as Freeport-McMoRan and Southern Copper. The stimulus is intended to support weak Chinese demand and property activity, potentially lifting copper expectations. | Canada plans to implement a 25% surtax on certain steel and aluminum products from China on Oct. 22, while considering tariff relief where alternative supply is in short demand. The move follows a same-day 100% surtax on Chinese-made electric vehicles. Trade barriers may tighten aluminum availability and support prices. | Canada will impose 25% tariffs on a wide range of Chinese steel and aluminum items starting Oct. 22, citing China’s overcapacity and protecting domestic producers. Goods already in transit on the start date are exempt. The policy is framed as aligning with allied trade actions against Chinese industrial supply. | Capstone Copper says its Mantoverde Development Project achieved commercial production, defined by 30 consecutive days at 75% of nameplate throughput, with in-line concentrate grade and recoveries. It expects Q4 to be the strongest quarter and shows 2024 production near the low end while C1 costs run slightly above guidance. | Capstone Copper reported results of an optimized feasibility study for its Mantoverde brownfield expansion in Chile, including debottlenecking to raise sulphide concentrator throughput and add ~20,000 tonnes per annum of incremental copper for $146 million. The plan targets low C1 cash costs and follows recent commercial production startup. | Capstone Copper said its Mantoverde Development Project reached commercial production after achieving 30 consecutive days of mill operation at 75% of nameplate throughput. The firm expects Q4 to be the strongest quarter, while 2024 output trends toward the low end of guidance and C1 costs are slightly higher.

Panama’s government is seeking an environmental assessment to determine the amount and extraction timing of copper concentrate stored at First Quantum’s dormant mine. Officials said copper can’t remain indefinitely and they may pursue export after due diligence. The mine was idle after a court ruling, affecting revenues and growth, and the company is in arbitration. | Danske Bank says Boliden’s 2025 guidance for copper grades at the Aitik open pit mine will be key at the Oct. 22 Q3 report. After grades fell from 0.29% (2018) to 0.17% recently, Danske expects a 0.18% grade in 2025, signaling an improved multi-year cycle. Bullish outlook. | China’s stimulus (lower rates, easier home purchases) sparked a strong sentiment-driven rally in iron ore, but copper reacted more modestly. Shanghai copper rose 1.8% and London copper stayed near pre-stimulus levels and below a recent four-month high, reflecting cautious expectations for global demand. | UK Export Finance guaranteed a €12.6m loan to Uzbekistan’s Almalyk to refinance automated machinery purchases from Weir, supporting ongoing operations and exports tied to Almalyk’s copper facility. The deal is the first UKEF transaction in Uzbekistan and complements Almalyk’s planned expansion to raise copper cathode output. | Ero Copper reported an initial NI 43-101 mineral resource for its Furnas copper-gold project in Brazil, including indicated and inferred copper grades and planned 28,000 meters of Phase 1 drilling after receiving permits. The news is about resource definition rather than near-term output changes. | The International Copper Association (ICA) appointed Glencore executive Stephen Rowland as its board chair and outlined a new strategic plan emphasizing sustainability, circularity, carbon footprint and market intelligence to defend copper demand. Glencore described net-zero plans by 2050. No near-term copper production, trade, or pricing actions were announced.

Castillo Copper Limited announced a conditional agreement to sell its Cangai Copper Mine tenements in northern New South Wales to Infinity Mining, receiving IMI shares and options. The deal is subject to due diligence, approvals, and royalty arrangements, mainly reallocating development ownership rather than changing copper supply quickly. | Power Nickel released new drill assay results from the Lion Zone at its Nisk property, highlighting longer and higher-grade intersections with notable nickel and copper grades. The company says the discovery appears substantially bigger than previously envisioned, improving confidence in mineral continuity and supporting expectations for future Ni-Cu supply. | Power Nickel reported assay results from three additional drill holes at its Lion Zone polymetallic discovery. Hole 70 returned 32m of 0.18% Ni plus 3.62% Cu and other metals, alongside additional Au and Ag. The update strengthens the perceived resource potential and is mildly supportive for nickel and copper sentiment. | Grid Metals outlines 4 km of copper-dominant mineralization with associated nickel at its MM copper/nickel project’s Eagle gabbro target in Manitoba. The company plans inaugural drilling and additional geophysical surveys. It provides grab-sample grades but no new resource estimate or production impacts. | Grid Metals reported surface copper/nickel mineralization along a ~4 km Eagle gabbro corridor at its MM project in Manitoba, with grades up to 1.3% Cu and associated nickel/cobalt/PGM values. The company plans geophysical work and inaugural drilling this fall after permits, but no production or supply changes were announced. | Granite Creek Copper reports discovery of a new “Gap Zone” at its Carmacks copper-gold-silver project in Yukon, with copper mineralization in 3 of 4 holes and results pending from sampled sections. The company suggests potential to expand resources and mine life, but this is exploration news without immediate supply impact. | Granite Creek Copper reports discovering a new zone during 2024 drilling at its Carmacks property. The update can be read as improving prospects for potential future copper resources. Because the release provides no detail on deposit size, grades, or timing to production, any immediate copper price impact is likely limited. | Battery Mineral Resources announced encouraging 2024 underground drill intercepts at its Punitaqui/Cinabrio copper mine in Chile, including multiple intervals grading between roughly 0.65% and 2.6% Cu (with silver). Management says results confirm copper grade and better delineate mineralization within planned production areas. | Codelco chair Maximo Pacheco says a dislocation in the copper concentrates market—lower concentrate availability and smelter capacity excess—could lead to smelter closures tied to spot market exposure. He notes spot copper concentrate treatment charges rose to very low levels/volatility and benchmark talks are ongoing for 2025. | Codelco’s chairman said no copper premium number will be issued this week as contract talks with customers continue. The story references prior Codelco premium levels and notes stable European and US cathode premium assessments. It also points to firmer physical demand signals (Chinese stimulus, falling inventories), but premiums remain largely unchanged. | Copper prices fell on geopolitical tensions, a stronger US dollar, and doubts about when China’s stimulus would translate into physical demand. LME three-month copper dropped 1.8% to about $9,906/ton, after rising the prior session, indicating near-term risk to demand sentiment. | Comex copper reportedly settled 2.05% lower at $4.5020. The move suggests immediate bearish sentiment toward copper futures, reflecting weaker price action into the settlement rather than a neutral or bullish development.

Castillo Copper announced a conditional sale of Cangai Copper Mine tenement licences in New South Wales to Infinity Mining. The transaction requires sale agreements, capital raising, and shareholder/regulatory approvals, with royalty interests transferred and Castillo receiving equity in Infinity. It supports Castillo’s shift toward its NWQ copper project. | Metal Energy Corp. announced an arm’s-length deal to acquire 100% of the Highland Valley Copper Project in British Columbia from Happy Creek. The property is copper-silver-molybdenum mineralized and permitted for drilling, with a planned exploration spend ahead of year-end, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Freeport CEO Andrea Quirk says the medium/long-term copper outlook is positive and short-term price action is sentiment-driven. She cites China stimulus and US rate cuts for improving sentiment and supporting LME copper prices. Demand drivers remain strong, mine supply growth is limited, and copper-to-aluminium substitution is expected to be small (1–2%). | Freeport says it will shift from marketing copper concentrates to marketing cathodes as its new Gresik smelter ramps up. The timing coincides with tight copper concentrate supplies, worsened by Cobre Panama’s closure. Copper concentrate treatment charges rose to about $2/tonne, signaling feed constraints that can support copper. | Northstar Gold and Novamera signed an MOU to test and develop potential copper at the Cam Copper site in Ontario using “surgical mining.” Work includes 3D modeling, calibration, permitting, and environmental assessment, with later extraction dependent on external funding. The initiative is copper-focused but exploratory, not immediate supply. | Solis Minerals applied for 27 exploration concessions (25,600Ha) for the Canyon Project in Peru’s Coastal Batholith, targeting porphyry copper mineralisation with planned reconnaissance and drilling near year end. The release focuses on early-stage exploration and potential near-surface heap-leachable copper oxides, not production or supply disruption. | Kavango Resources said Phase 1 drilling at its Karakubis Copper Project in Botswana produced promising stratigraphic results, including evidence of structural trap sites and significant hydrothermal alteration. Copper-bearing mineralisation was confirmed across five holes, prompting additional geophysical surveying and future drill targeting, but no production timelines were given.

Ero Copper reported a temporary power outage at its Tucumã copper operation in Brazil after a windstorm damaged the regional grid. Processing circuits were affected while mining/crushing continued, with industrial power expected to be fully restored within two weeks. The company expects main impact on processing ramp-up and full-year results. | Kavango Resources and ENRG report very strong early drilling results at the Karakubis copper project in Botswana’s Kalahari Copper Belt, including bornite and chalcopyrite hits with high copper readings. The JV is continuing with geophysical and electromagnetic surveys alongside further diamond drilling, indicating promising potential for a large discovery. | Trader IXM said copper demand tied to electric vehicles is intact. The message implies ongoing consumption support for copper, reducing downside fears about EV-related demand weakening in the near term. That view is typically supportive for copper sentiment as markets look for evidence of steady end-user demand. | Camino Minerals, with Nittetsu, signed a definitive agreement to acquire the Puquios copper project in Chile, a construction-ready SXEW copper heap leach asset with permits and infrastructure. The deal includes cash and share consideration plus financing for working capital, potentially supporting near-term copper development and future supply expectations. | Armenia’s copper concentrate production increased 11.4% year-on-year to 202,087.6 tonnes in January–August 2024, according to the national statistical committee. The same report notes zinc and molybdenum concentrate changes, but the copper output rise suggests improved near-term supply conditions for copper. | Aurwest Resources will sell its 100% interest in the Stars porphyry copper-molybdenum project in British Columbia to Interra Copper for C$250,000 plus Interra shares and warrants. The acquisition is subject to approvals and includes a 2% NSR. It mainly reflects ongoing exploration financing, not near-term copper supply changes. | Ero Copper Corp. announced it will release its third-quarter 2024 operating and financial results on Nov. 5, 2024 and hold a conference call on Nov. 6. The statement is a routine investor-relations update and includes company background, but no new production, guidance, or market-moving data for copper.

A study for the Recycle Your Electricals campaign estimates UK households hold hundreds of millions of unused/binned cables containing tens of thousands of tonnes of copper. Researchers and the Royal Society of Chemistry warn of potential supply constraints for copper in wind and solar, urging more recycling to boost secondary supply. | Britons are urged to recycle unwanted electricals to unlock “urban mining” copper contained in unused cables and chargers. The study estimates large hidden copper volumes in UK e-waste and argues recycling could help offset rising demand from renewables and EVs. Copper shortages are projected, but near-term effects look limited. | Las Vegas residents report prolonged streetlight outages after thieves stole copper wire from light poles. The county confirms copper-wire theft caused the failures and says nearly 1 million feet of wiring were stolen since 2022, replacing over $1.5 million of copper wiring, with about 200 repair sites still pending. | Chakana Copper Corp. relinquishes its option to acquire the Condor concessions that make up about 25% of its expanded Soledad copper-gold-silver project in Peru. The decision follows inability to agree on a buyout price amid outstanding property payments. Permits will be cancelled and Condor would need new permitting if proceeding. | Copper Fox Metals said it retained Quantec to run a deep penetrating DCIP and MT geophysical survey on its Sombrero Butte porphyry copper project in Arizona, aiming to advance toward drill-ready targets. Work is budgeted at about US$300,000 and planned to start mid-October, contingent on Phase I results. | A study estimates UK households hold about 38,449 tonnes of copper in discarded electricals, while UK copper use is ~250,000 tonnes per year and expected to rise with electrification. Bloomberg Intelligence projects a global copper shortfall by 2033 and warns new supply is slow, boosting recycling relevance for future tightness. | Grid Battery Metals announced it acquired a large copper land position in north-central British Columbia and outlined exploration plans for 2024/2025, including drilling activity in its Nevada projects. The news relates to potential future copper supply from exploration activity but provides no immediate production volumes or supply disruptions. | The RSC warns the UK may face a substantial copper supply shortfall as wind and solar targets approach, citing already-struggling production to meet demand. Ahead of International E-Waste Day, it launches a cable-recycling campaign to increase secondary copper supply, seeking government investment in recycling infrastructure. | Western Metallica Resources said it is finalizing logistics and access for a drill program in late October 2024 at its Caña Brava copper project in Peru, including targeting at the Caña Brava 1 porphyry prospect. Rock chip results indicate shallow copper mineralization and anomalies open in all directions.

Moxico Resources, via Atlantic Metals, signed an agreement to earn 75% in two copper exploration projects in Argentina, including drilling and milestone payments. Planned work includes 65,000m of drilling and feasibility studies, with an option to reach 100% later. No near-term supply disruption is indicated. | Freeport Resources appointed Practara to run a Phase 1 desktop optimisation study for the Yandera copper project in Papua New Guinea, aiming to update the 2017 pre-feasibility assumptions and improve CAPEX/OPEX, metallurgy, and logistics before a definitive feasibility study. No production change is announced. | The interview says Indonesia’s nickel industry is scaling into an EV battery-material ecosystem, citing commissioned precursor and lithium refining capacity and existing copper-foil operations near Freeport. It also notes forced-labor/environmental allegations and IRA rules limiting subsidized EV inputs from highly foreign-controlled entities, plus LFP’s nickel-free shift. | The UK campaign Recycle Your Electricals urges “urban mining” to recycle unused cables and electronics, claiming UK homes hold up to ~38,449 tonnes of copper worth about £266m. It cites a global supply-demand shortfall and argues better recycling could help meet additional copper demand. | Lara Exploration reported its maiden mineral resource for the Planalto copper-gold project in Brazil, with indicated and inferred resources graded around 0.5% copper. The pit-shell and economic parameters use assumed copper prices, but there is no production or supply timeline, implying limited short-term impact on copper markets. | A Utah history feature explains how Bingham Canyon/Kennecott Copper Mine grew with U.S. copper demand, supported company towns, and later expanded further. It notes Kennecott supplies about 13% of U.S. copper and links growing renewables and EV adoption to continued copper use, supporting demand expectations.

Patriot Lithium signed a binding option agreement to acquire an 80% stake in Zambia’s Katwaro Copper Project from Array Metals. The deal covers a 12-month option period with exploration funding and potential extra payments tied to JORC-indicated copper resources. Planned work is mainly review and surveys in Q4 2024. | NorthWest Copper announced new mineralized zone results from first-ever drilling at its Lorraine-Top Cat Road IP target, including 104.7 metres at 0.13% copper and 7.4 metres at 1.10% Cu. Assays also show anomalous copper at the Nova target. No production or supply impacts were stated. | Direct Wire opened a new oxygen-free copper rod mill using an oxygen-free copper upcasting process to improve purity and conductivity. The company says the facility will expand production of oxygen-free copper rods and stems for energy and high-tech manufacturing. The release signals additional copper output, but without volumes, near-term pricing impact is unclear.

Patriot Lithium secured a deal with Array Metals to earn an 80% interest via a 12-month due diligence/option for the Katwaro copper project in Zambia. Patriot will fund exploration and feasibility after transfer, with payments tied to resource delineation. The plan targets future copper development, not immediate supply. | Fastmarkets’ outlook expects copper prices to face upward pressure in Q4 2024. Drivers cited include Fed rate cuts and China’s large stimulus, a recovery in Chinese physical demand and Shanghai cathode premiums, potential smelter production cuts tightening supply, and positive seasonality/speculative long rebuilding. Risks include macro and geopolitical shocks. | Clark County is considering stiffer laws targeting copper wire and other scrap purchased from suspected stolen sources. Proposed rules would restrict scrap transactions (e.g., seller transport requirements, payment methods, and proof for burned copper). Violations carry fines and the city of Las Vegas already adopted a similar ordinance.

Pan Asia Metals reported encouraging early fieldwork at its Chile Rosario copper project, finding secondary copper minerals like malachite and chrysocolla and some primary copper sulphides. Results from soil/stream sediment and an IP geophysical survey are expected soon, followed by planned RC drilling. | Reuters columnist Clyde Russell says investors reacted differently to Beijing’s latest stimulus rhetoric. Shanghai copper futures opened higher while London copper fell, reflecting sentiment on possible refinancing of local government debt and renewed construction/property demand, though the author argues demand effects may be limited and later. | Woomera Mining started a dual-rig diamond core drilling program at its Bronze Fox copper-gold project in Mongolia’s Southern Gobi copper belt, targeting an extension of a previously reported high-grade zone. The work includes infill and resource expansion toward a mining license, with results expected in January. | Xanadu released a large prefeasibility study for its Kharmagtai copper-gold project in Mongolia, projecting ~60,000 tonnes of copper annually initially, low cash costs, and a long 29-year mine life. The company expects a bankable feasibility study in 2026, supporting sentiment for future copper supply. | China’s unwrought copper imports rose 15.4% in September to 479,000 tonnes versus August, driven by improving seasonal demand, restocking before Golden Week, lower inventories, and an open arbitrage window from relatively high global prices. U.S. rate cuts and China stimulus further brighten copper demand outlook. | Alma Metals acquired a 51% JV stake in Queensland’s Briggs Copper Project after meeting earn-in conditions and expanded an ongoing drilling program. Results include long intercepts with grades around 0.40–0.45% copper, prompting increased drilling metres and aiming for tighter resource estimates and potential scoping work. | The technical piece analyzes Elliott Wave patterns in copper futures (HG_F). After an impulsive move from the 3.9230 low, copper experienced a 3-wave pullback. It now argues the price has reached the buyers’ zone (4.3758-4.3069), found support near 4.3729, and could rally toward new highs or a three-wave bounce. | The piece argues that gold/mining stocks are correcting, and uses copper as a confirmation signal. It says copper’s short rally after Friday was erased, labeling the late-September attempt above key Fibonacci/July highs as invalidated. The author expects the decline to continue, citing a broader bearish technical pattern.

Freeport Indonesia stopped copper cathode production at its Manyar smelter after a Monday fire in a sulphuric acid unit. Production was halted because the gas must be captured by that plant. The firm is investigating the cause and will determine the restart timeline, with ramp-up plans to be reviewed. | Freeport Indonesia temporarily halted operations at its new copper smelter in Gresik, East Java, after a fire at the gas cleaning plant on Monday. The blaze was extinguished the same evening, with no casualties reported. An assessment will determine damage, cause, and any impact on plans to raise smelter capacity. | Los Andes highlights LME Week 2024 discussions where copper was voted the “top prospect” for base metals. It notes copper’s strong 2024 performance and analyst views that demand weakness from China has not materialized, while insufficient capex implies an impending copper supply gap, supportive of bullish prices. | York Harbour Metals says Firetail Resources has started drilling at the Skyline copper project in Newfoundland under an option agreement. A 5,000-metre maiden program and EM surveys aim to extend high-grade copper mineralization along a 16 km strike length. It signals progress, but no near-term supply impact is stated. | Xanadu Mines released a pre-feasibility study for the Kharmagtai copper-gold project in Mongolia, estimating 60–80 ktpa copper over early expansion stages and low cash costs. The study supports potential development and a future supply outlook for copper, but it does not signal imminent production, limiting near-term price pressure. | Great Western Exploration mobilised a drill rig to begin imminent diamond drilling at its Oval copper-gold target in Western Australia. The program aims to test EM and gravity anomalies to refine the model before further holes. Assays are expected weeks after drilling; funding support was also received from WA. | The article is a London-listed companies roundup. The update is copper-positive for sentiment. | SolGold began geotechnical investigations at its Cascabel copper-gold porphyry project in Ecuador, including drilling and seismic assessments to refine ground conditions for mine infrastructure. The step is incremental and not a near-term copper supply shock. | Copper and other metals reportedly gained as the U.S. dollar weakened and markets built hopes for China stimulus. The combination tends to improve risk sentiment and expected industrial demand, generally supporting prices for industrial metals like copper, aluminium, and nickel over the coming days. | North Bay Resources reported assay results of up to 9.5% copper from its Murex Copper Project in British Columbia, describing high-grade rock chip samples in the D Zone and other mapped copper-bearing areas. The release is based on Summer 2024 exploration sampling and filed assessment work, not commercial production. | Kutcho Copper Corp. closed a non-brokered private placement raising $1.29 million to advance the Kutcho copper project and for working capital. Units were sold at $0.10 with warrants exercisable at $0.16. The company paid finder fees and awaits final TSX Venture approval. | Faraday Copper reported Q3 2024 financial results and highlighted ongoing exploration drilling at its Copper Creek Project in Arizona, including multiple copper intercepts and positive metallurgical outcomes. The release also notes equity funding and future technical reporting milestones. It is largely company-specific with no clear immediate impact on overall copper supply or demand.

Teck Resources presented its strategy to grow copper production to ~800 ktpa before decade end, backed by $3.2–$3.9B investment over four years and a strong balance sheet. Projects such as Quebrada Blanca optimization and mine life extensions are expected to lift copper output in 2024-2025. | Teck Resources said it will present its strategy and plans to boost copper production to about 800,000 tonnes per year by the end of the decade. The company says the findings expand copper target zones and will be incorporated with earn-in partner Eldorado’s model, with soils pending. | Michigan’s EGLE is holding a public comment process for an air-use permit for the proposed Copperwood underground copper mine near Lake Superior. Opponents cite environmental justice and emissions concerns, while officials say the permit focuses on air standards. No decision timeline is set, leaving near-term copper supply uncertainty. | Megado Minerals agreed to acquire an 80% stake in Iberian Copper’s Spanish copper project and plans to fund development through a rights issue and share placements. The update supports company-level financing for Doré Copper’s Québec copper project, but provides no new production, cost, or demand information for copper markets.

Rio Tinto partnered with China’s SPIC to trial battery-swap electric haul trucks at its Oyu Tolgoi copper mine in Mongolia. No direct changes to copper output or inventories are indicated. | Copper is reported to be dropping as the US dollar strengthens after early US election results. The move suggests tighter USD conditions reduced demand expectations for dollar-priced industrial metals, pressuring copper prices in the near term despite election-related uncertainty. | Copper prices reportedly declined as the US dollar strengthened on expectations tied to a Trump lead in the US election. The move implies near-term bearish pressure for copper given the currency effect on dollar-denominated commodity pricing and trading sentiment. | Metals X reported an expanded mineral resource estimate for the Rentails tailings retreatment project at Tasmania’s Renison mine. Eight 91t trucks and a robotic swap/charging setup will be commissioned progressively, initially for above-ground non-production tasks. The project targets less downtime and faster electrification assessment.

Canada’s steel and aluminum producers face uncertainty after Trump’s election and his campaign talk of broad U.S. tariffs. History includes 2018–2020 tariff back-and-forth between the U.S. and Canada, plus existing USMCA monitoring. Aluminum industry leaders warn another flareup and tariff-based concessions are possible, weighing on prices. | The piece notes Trump’s US election win could bring tariff hikes on China, pressuring base metals in the short term. Offsetting that, brokers see an accelerating copper deficit from electrification demand and expect aluminium supply deficits. It also highlights RBC’s view of accelerating copper deficits from electrification and Wilsons’ forecast of an aluminium swing to deficit as China’s supply growth is constrained. Nickel remains pressured by inventories and soft demand. | Saudi Arabia’s Ma’aden/Manara Minerals is in advanced talks to acquire a 15%–20% stake in Zambian copper assets held by First Quantum Minerals, potentially valuing $1.5–$2 billion. The deal is expected to close by year-end. Aluminium is mentioned as a strategic priority but without any specific transaction. | Metal Energy completed acquisition of a 100% interest in the Highland Valley Copper project adjacent to Teck’s Highland Valley Copper mine. The property hosts copper-rich mineralization in Zones 1 and 2 and will begin new geophysical work ahead of permitted drilling, but no immediate production impact is stated. | Barrick’s quarterly earnings improved as both gold and realized copper prices rose. Realized copper was up 13% year-on-year, and copper output increased to 48,000 tons from 43,000 tons in the prior quarter. The company expects production growth into Q4 from capacity ramps at several sites. | Jubilee Metals said its copper production fell to 744 tonnes in the first quarter from 1,048 tonnes previously, citing limited run time during linkage to the Roan concentrator ramp-up and power outages in Zambia. The company raised some other output but maintained copper guidance for the half-year ahead. | China will implement from Nov 15 clarified rules that widen categories for importing recycled copper and aluminium scrap, but market participants doubt near-term volumes due to unchanged copper quality requirements. Copper inflow gains are expected limited, while aluminium scrap demand is stronger; however high import prices and LME volatility curb buying. | China’s MEE scheduled new categories for recycled copper and aluminium scrap imports to take effect Nov 15, but copper quality thresholds remain strict, limiting incremental scrap inflows and keeping copper scrap discount pressure modest. Aluminium scrap demand is stronger, though high import prices and LME futures restrain near-term buying.

Metal Energy completed the acquisition of the Highland Valley Copper Project in British Columbia, securing 100% ownership from Happy Creek Minerals. Terms include cash and share issuance plus a 2.5% net smelter royalty, with exploration and $2.5m financing requirements. The news supports future copper development but is not supply-shifting immediately. | Police in Lilongwe are investigating theft of copper wires from an ESCOM transformer at Mlale farm in Mitundu, shortly after lightning damaged the original unit. ESCOM had planned repairs and replacement, but unknown suspects removed the transformer and cut all wires. Investigations are ongoing. | China has licensed more private firms to blend and process more polluting complex copper concentrates domestically, widening import flexibility and easing concentrate grade constraints. With China a major copper smelter/importer facing concentrate shortages, the change could improve smelter feed supply and influence near-term refined copper availability and prices. | Koryx Copper closed the second tranche of an oversubscribed private placement, raising about $5.2M (at $1.10/share) and bringing total gross proceeds to about $14.9M. Net proceeds will support the Haib copper project in Namibia, Zambia projects, and working capital, which can modestly improve copper development sentiment.

Great Southern Copper PLC raised about GBP780,000 via a placing of 62.4 million shares at 1.25 pence, with warrants exercisable later. Proceeds will fund expanded drilling and exploration at its Chile Especularita copper-gold project over the next 6–12 months. No near-term production effects are indicated. | Koryx Copper raised about US$5.2 million in a second equity tranche to fund development work at Namibia’s Haib copper project, plus Zambia’s Luanshya West Project and working capital. The company reports strong investor demand and plans increased drilling ahead of a 2025 PEA update. | Canada Nickel announced assay results from drillhole BAN24-18 at its Bannockburn project, including 3.95% nickel over 4 metres and up to 4.65% nickel over 1 metre. The interval also reports minor copper (0.40% over 12 metres). The update supports bullish near-term sentiment for nickel. | G11 Resources reported significant copper-rich VMS drill intercepts at its Wilandra copper corridor in NSW, while Firetail Resources released high-grade massive copper-zinc-silver intercepts from its Skyline copper project in Newfoundland. Both pieces are exploration updates with no stated near-term mine output changes. | Hindustan Copper reported a 67.4% rise in Q2 net profit to Rs 101.67 crore, driven by higher operating revenue (Rs 518.19 crore vs Rs 381.40 crore). As India’s only integrated copper producer, improved business performance can bolster near-term expectations for copper market fundamentals. | Codelco, Chile’s state copper producer, has offered its 2025 refined copper output to Chinese clients at a premium of $89 per tonne over LME, largely unchanged from last year. Copper traded risk-off ahead of the result, then rebounded on hopes of China stimulus tied to tariff threats. China’s $1.4 trillion debt measures disappointed bulls, triggering renewed selling. The piece also cites higher U.S. duties on Chinese aluminium.

Premium Nickel Resources extended the study phase for its Selebi Mines project by one year to complete an economic study and a US$25 million milestone payment. It continues drilling and metallurgical/engineering work, and reports historic drill core data including nickel and copper to support an upcoming resource estimate. | Atlantic Copper, a major Spanish producer of high-purity refined copper, signed a long-term PPA with Alter Enersun to supply green electricity from a 30 MWp solar PV plant for self-consumption at its Huelva metallurgical complex. Electricity pricing risk should be reduced, improving cost stability, though the plant starts in 2026. | Hot Chili signed a three-year option to acquire 100% of Chile’s historical La Verde open-pit copper mine, paying $320,000 upfront with additional contingent payments and a $6.89m final exercise fee. The deal consolidates the La Verde copper system, enabling comprehensive drilling and forthcoming assay results. | Minerals Development Oman has broken ground on the Mazoon Copper Project in Oman, an integrated copper concentrate development with multiple open-pit mines, a processing plant, and planned annual output of copper concentrate. Company leaders stress mining is not imminent and depends on funding and future permitting. | Surge Copper reported assay results from infill drilling at its 100%-owned Berg project, including 320m at 0.46% CuEq (0.29% Cu) and higher-grade intervals within supergene sulfides. Results are expected to upgrade inferred resources, potentially improving outlook for future copper supply. | Coppernico Metals reports early drill results from its Sombrero (Sombrero Minerales SAC) project in Peru, including 19.15 m of 0.50% copper and other intervals with elevated copper/zinc. The company is also applying to expand permits for more drilling rigs and shares are expected to start trading on OTCQX. | Thunderbird Minerals announced a ~39% expansion of its Argo Copper-Gold Project land package near Quesnel, adding ground prospective for epithermal and porphyry-style copper-gold mineralization. The York target includes historical copper anomalies and chalcopyrite observations, but no production or resource update is provided. | Lara Exploration says mining and processing have resumed at its Celesta copper project in Brazil after reinstating permits and taking the plant out of care and maintenance. China’s base metals smelting group says using more aluminium instead of copper could help secure copper resources, given China’s higher reliance on overseas copper imports versus aluminium. The chairman cites economic advantages and calls for copper refining capacity reorganisation, but no immediate supply disruption is stated. | Ge Honglin of China’s nonferrous smelting industry group said promoting aluminium use instead of copper would help conserve China’s copper resources, where imports are more dependent. Cuprum owns the project; a related business combination is expected to close in December. No immediate production or supply impacts are described. | Sonoran Desert Copper Corporation announced the creation of a Brazilian subsidiary and filed an application with Brazil’s Agência Nacional de Mineração for seven new copper exploration concessions in Pernambuco and Bahia. The company said technical work is underway for a preliminary prospecting program. However, the announcement is exploratory and does not imply immediate production or supply changes, limiting near-term copper price impact. | Copper prices fell to a two-month low as the dollar rose and traders weighed cautious U.S. rate-cut expectations. Citi cut its short-term copper price target to $8,500/t from $9,500/t, citing concerns that Trump’s sweeping tariffs and disappointing China stimulus could hurt global manufacturing demand. Ausgrid warned copper theft endangers public safety and power infrastructure. | Police charged a man over an attempted theft of copper cabling from an Ausgrid substation in Heddon Greta. Repairs to replace stolen copper cables are expected to exceed $165,000, and authorities warn copper theft endangers public safety. | Police charged a man after he allegedly stripped copper cabling from an Ausgrid substation, triggering an explosion that injured him and causing major power-infrastructure damage. Authorities expect repairs and replacement of stolen cables to cost over $165,000, highlighting ongoing copper-theft risks and disruption. | Police charged a man after alleged copper cabling theft from an Ausgrid substation in Heddon Greta caused an explosion. Ausgrid warns that copper theft endangers power infrastructure, but the event is likely too small for broad copper price moves. | Police charged a man after alleged copper cabling theft from an Ausgrid substation caused an explosion and injuries. Repairs and replacement of stolen copper cables are expected to cost more than $165,000. Ausgrid warned that exposed power infrastructure is dangerous, but the incident is small for market pricing. | Police charged a man accused of stealing copper cabling from an Ausgrid substation near Heddon Greta, triggering an explosion and injuries. Repairs to replace stolen cables are expected to cost over $165,000. Ausgrid warned copper theft endangers safety, but the event appears localized with limited market impact. | Copper prices fell below $9,000 as the U.S. dollar extended gains following the post-election period. Police say repairs and cable replacement are expected to exceed $165,000, with authorities warning copper theft endangers public safety and disrupts emergency response. | A man was charged after allegedly stealing copper cabling from an Ausgrid substation, triggering an explosion that injured him. Police say repairs and cable replacement are expected to cost over $165,000. Authorities warn copper theft endangers public safety, but the reported quantities appear too small to move global prices. | A man was charged after allegedly stealing copper cabling from an Ausgrid substation in Heddon Greta, triggering an explosion and injuries. Police say repairs and replacement of stolen cables may exceed $165,000. Ausgrid warns copper theft endangers public safety and diverts emergency resources. | Police charged a man over an alleged attempt to steal copper cabling from an Ausgrid substation, which triggered an explosion injuring him. Police say repairs and replacement are expected to cost over $165,000. Authorities highlight copper theft risks to power infrastructure and public safety. | Police charged a man over an alleged copper cabling theft attempt from an Ausgrid substation in Heddon Greta that led to an explosion and injuries. Investigators expect repairs and replacement of stolen cables to cost over $165,000, highlighting copper theft risks to critical power infrastructure. | Police charged a man after alleged copper theft from an Ausgrid substation caused an explosion and serious burns. The utility said the stripped copper cabling and repairs/replacements are expected to cost over $165,000. Authorities warn theft leaves powerlines exposed and diverts emergency resources. | A man was charged after allegedly stripping copper cabling from an Ausgrid substation, causing an explosion that injured him. The piece notes continued pressure on Hong Kong equities and copper, arguing US-China trade tensions may jeopardize manufacturing recovery. It also highlights copper’s bearish technical acceleration and downside targets. | BHP is expected to detail plans on a Chile roadshow to spend at least $7bn to recover more copper from Escondida and maintain output as ore grades decline. The move improves financing prospects for future concentrate supply, but as it is a development milestone, the immediate effect on copper prices is likely limited. | Barksdale Resources said drill assays from its Sunnyside project in Arizona returned high-grade copper-silver and zinc-silver mineralization, including up to 4.33% copper and 99.7 g/t silver, plus broader anomalous zones. Management highlighted consistency of the near-surface chalcocite copper zone and ongoing drilling toward deeper targets. | York Harbour Metals announced progress under an option agreement for the Skyline high-grade copper-zinc-silver project in Newfoundland. Firetail reported initial drill intersections with strong copper grades, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | Highland Copper reported substantial completion of early wetlands and streams site work at its Copperwood project, progress on detailed engineering and permitting, and a pending state infrastructure grant decision. At White Pine, it updated on completed drilling, environmental baseline studies, and ongoing metallurgical test work, supporting future copper development. | Helix Resources, via Leichhardt Resources, agreed to acquire a 51% interest in seven new copper project tenements in Western Australia’s Bryah Basin, with earn-in terms to potentially reach 90% ownership. Drilling is planned with government funding, building on prospects near the DeGrussa copper mine. | Osisko Metals announced an updated mineral resource estimate for the Gaspé Copper project near Murdochville, showing a 53% rise in indicated copper-equivalent and a major increase in inferred copper-equivalent. The pit-constrained estimate contains about 4.91 billion pounds of contained copper, alongside molybdenum and silver, with further drilling planned in 2025. | Capstone Copper filed an NI 43-101 technical report and feasibility study for its Mantoverde Optimized brownfield expansion project in Chile. Tertiary Minerals announced the start of reverse circulation drilling at its Brunton Pass copper-gold project in Nevada. Two holes target skarn/porphyry copper and two target an epithermal gold-silver/geophysical/geochemical anomaly. The shift is tied to refocusing on mining rather than new energy infrastructure, potentially affecting near-term copper supply expectations. | China’s finance ministry plans to reduce or cancel export tax rebates effective Dec. 1, including scrapping rebates for aluminium and copper products. Traders expect lower Chinese exports, tightening supply abroad. Reuters reported a jump in LME aluminium prices and broader commodity moves as rebate changes affect cross-border flows. | China will reduce or cancel export tax rebates for various commodities effective Dec. 1, including scrapping rebates for aluminum and copper products. Traders expect higher export costs and lower Chinese outbound supply, which has already triggered an LME aluminium price jump and supports copper. | BHP is expected by Berenberg to face balance sheet pressure from higher capital outlay for copper projects in Chile, Argentina and Australia, with total growth capex estimated at USD21–26 billion over 10 years. The bank expects net debt to rise above targets and limits capital returns, though no near-term supply change is cited. | China’s finance ministry plans to reduce or cancel export tax rebates effective Dec. 1, including scrapping rebates for aluminium and copper products. The policy is expected to raise exporters’ costs and reduce China’s outbound metal flows, boosting near-term aluminium prices on the LME and supporting copper sentiment.

Malaysia-listed aluminum stocks rose after China said it will end export-tax rebates for aluminum starting Dec. 1. Analysts expect the removal to increase Chinese aluminum export costs, potentially reducing near-term exports and tightening supply. ING also flags trade shifts toward alternative sources. The rebate change includes copper. | China’s Ministry of Finance will end 13% export tax rebates for copper and aluminium semi-finished products from 1 Dec 2024. With weak domestic demand and tight replacement options globally, exporters’ margins fall and overseas buyers may face higher prices or limited availability. LME/SHFE initially moved differently, but shortages could support prices. | China will end a 13% export tax rebate for copper and aluminium semi products effective 1 December 2024, cutting exporter margins as domestic demand is weak. SMM expects limited ability to replace Chinese supply quickly, with potential support for LME prices, despite near-term pricing negotiations and shipping/bonded-warehouse acceleration. | U.S. GoldMining reported confirmatory drilling results at its Whistler gold-copper project in Alaska, including 120 m at 0.16% copper and 1.00 g/t gold equivalent. The update signals potential improvement in deposit grades, but no production, permitting, or shipment changes are provided, so near-term copper price impact is likely limited. | Mazoon Mining (MDO) has begun work on Oman’s Mazoon Copper Project, described as the country’s largest integrated copper concentrate venture. It includes multiple open-pit mines and a processing plant targeting 115,000 tons of copper concentrate annually. The project aims to meet rising copper demand, though it is a longer-run supply build. | Osisko Metals announced an updated mineral resource estimate for its Gaspé Copper Project in Quebec, boosting contained copper-equivalent resources substantially and planning a 70,000-metre drill program in 2025. The news improves perceived future copper project potential, supporting copper sentiment though it won’t affect near-term supply immediately. | The article reviews nickel price momentum and volatility in 2024, citing Indonesian laterite supply, nickel oversupply, and China stimulus-induced swings in demand. It also highlights Vale’s involvement in nickel and copper and an agreement for a $1.42B Indonesian nickel plant, but provides no clear short-term market disruption.

AT&T is offering rewards for information leading to arrests of people stealing copper wires in Indiana, citing outages caused by wire theft. AT&T says thefts increased over the past year as copper prices rose. Copper wiring is still used in some services, businesses, hospitals, and emergency systems. | Australian Gold and Copper and PolarX report new drill intercepts with meaningful copper grades. AGC’s South Cobar Achilles discovery includes copper-bearing intervals, while PolarX’s Caribou Dome delivers additional high-grade copper zones below 300m depth. The results may slightly support copper sentiment, though they don’t affect current supply immediately. | Kodiak Copper Corp. reported 2024 drill results at its MPD copper-gold porphyry project in southern British Columbia, including shallow high-grade copper mineralization from new Adit Zone step-out holes (up to ~0.64% Cu over 4m, 0.73% CuEq). The company says the Adit zone remains open and warrants further drilling in 2025. | Faraday Copper Corp. reported new drill intercepts at its Copper Creek Project in Arizona, including 57.73 m at 0.85% copper with an enrichment zone. The company plans continued near-surface drilling in Rum and American Eagle. The news supports exploration success but does not imply immediate production impacts. | Talon Metals’ drilling at the Boulderdash target in Michigan intersected substantial copper-nickel sulphide intervals across several drill holes, including up to 110.3 meters of mineralization with up to 40% sulphides, and indicators of possible massive sulphides. Assays are pending; results could modestly support copper and nickel sentiment. | Sterling Metals reported completion of a 3D IP survey over its Copper Road project in Ontario, identifying new copper sulphide outcrops and extending the porphyry footprint. The company collected additional surface samples and is starting a drone magnetic survey, with final IP inversion expected in December. | Vital Battery Metals reported assay results from its Sting Copper Project, including a grab sample with 9.3% Cu and soil sampling showing a 300+ meter Cu–Zn–As anomaly. The update supports exploration potential but does not indicate near-term supply changes, limiting likely immediate effects on copper prices and none on nickel or aluminium. | Marula Mining provided an update on the Kinusi copper mine in Tanzania. Export permits were received and three ore samples were shipped to South Africa for metallurgical testing and assays, with more trial shipments planned by 31 December 2024. Continued mining and stockpiling are expected to support early copper concentrate sales. | AT&T is offering up to $5,000 rewards for tips leading to arrests over copper wire thefts in several Indiana counties. The firm links rising theft activity to higher copper prices and says stolen lines caused recent phone outages and safety hazards, urging reporting to police and AT&T security. | Glencore will begin scaled on-site testing of a copper leaching process at its Lomas Bayas mine in northern Chile. The trial follows lab results and aims to improve recovery from 0.25% grade sulfide ore, potentially extending mine life. First results are expected in April/May. | Aluminum is reported to have steadied in October but turned slightly bearish mid-November as copper and other base metals weakened. Clark County approved an ordinance to curb illegal copper wire sales, requiring documentation for seven-strand copper wire, banning walk-up sellers, and prohibiting cash payments at scrap yards. Officials link the move to ongoing streetlight copper theft, which may tighten illicit copper flows. The article also cites continued aluminum substitution and replacement costs. | BHP plans to invest $10bn–$14bn to expand copper production at Chile’s Escondida mine, targeting output growth of 430,000–540,000 tonnes per annum. It aims to counter ore-grade declines and stabilize long-term output while projecting continued demand growth from renewables and EVs. | Xanadu Mines announced four new high-grade copper targets at its Sant Tolgoi project in Mongolia, with surface rock-chip assays up to 2.1% Cu and geophysical anomalies pointing to mineralised intrusions. The planned reconnaissance drilling will test for massive sulphide copper and possibly nickel, but no production is yet confirmed. | Arras Minerals reports extensive 2024 exploration in Kazakhstan’s Bozshakol-Chingis magmatic arc, identifying new undrilled copper and copper-gold targets and planning to commence drill-testing in the first half of 2025. The release focuses on exploration activities and does not indicate immediate copper supply changes, so near-term price effects appear limited. | Electra Battery Materials received a ten-year exploration permit from the US Forest Service covering 91 drill pad locations for its Idaho Cobalt Belt projects, including the Iron Creek copper-cobalt deposit. The permit adds regulatory certainty to resume exploration, but the news is more developmental than an immediate supply change. | CoTec announced Ceibo is partnering with Glencore’s Lomas Bayas to deploy leaching technology aimed at improving copper recovery from low-grade sulphides and possibly extending mine life. After two years of tests, scaling begins via an on-site lab. Results could improve operational efficiency, but current impact is uncertain. | Quartz Mountain Resources announced completion of an induced polarization survey at its Maestro property ahead of planned 2026 drilling. Technical results relate to identifying sulphide systems tied to epithermal Au-Ag and porphyry Mo-Cu/Cu-Mo mineralization, including notes of increasing Cu grades. Xanadu Mines announced strong surface copper assay results from its Sant Tolgoi Project in Mongolia and identified four drill targets. The company plans 3,000m of reconnaissance drilling to test for high-grade massive sulphide copper and nickel, with follow-up drilling expected in early 2025. | Electric Royalties signed an agreement to acquire a 0.75% gross revenue royalty on the producing Punitaqui copper mine in Chile, providing cash to the operator to support continued production and processing. It also notes a prior copper stream will not proceed. The news is copper-focused but largely financial and not a near-term supply shock.

Aldebaran announced an updated mineral resource estimate for its Altar copper-gold project in Argentina, showing a substantial increase versus 2021, including 22.01 billion pounds of copper. The company also plans the next study work (PEA in Q2 2025), adding near-term development optimism for copper.

Capstone Copper’s analysts argue the company is at an “inflection point,” citing ramp-up progress at Chile’s Mantoverde and expected operational improvements in coming quarters. The piece notes September issues from mill downtime and higher costs, but anticipates better recoveries and cashflow as payback from capex nears, reinforcing positive copper sentiment. | Minnesota’s Department of Natural Resources has delayed its decision on a key permit for a proposed copper-nickel mine. The delay may push back timelines for potential future production, improving near-term supply expectations. This is supportive for both copper and nickel prices, though magnitude is uncertain while the project remains pending. | Minnesota DNR delayed its decision on a key permit for a proposed copper-nickel mine. The delay likely postpones potential production timelines, increasing near-term uncertainty around future supply. That typically supports bearish sentiment for copper and nickel as markets recalibrate expectations for when the project could contribute output. | Copper is reported to be slipping after the U.S. dollar surges, following Trump’s threat of more tariffs. The headline links the currency move and trade-policy uncertainty to weaker near-term market sentiment for copper, implying downward price pressure in the coming days. | A Las Vegas-area recycling firm, SA Recycling, works with Metro Police on a copper theft task force to detect and prosecute stolen-copper sales, including streetlights and construction-site wire. The company supports a Valley-wide ordinance and a proposed 2025 state law making possession of stolen copper illegal to deter theft and improve traceability. | Metals Creek Resources began diamond drilling at the Tillex Copper Project in Ontario to further define the structurally complex copper mineralization. The release reiterates recent high-grade drilling intercepts and states results will be provided after compilation. The updates relate to potential future copper supply but likely won’t affect near-term pricing. | The article is focused on cobalt but repeatedly ties it to copper and nickel. Net signals for copper vs nickel are mixed. | The article says new U.S. tariffs on Canada and Mexico could raise aluminum prices because Canada is a key, low-cost aluminum supplier to the U.S. Other market notes mention copper falling amid stronger USD and tariff pledges, implying mixed near-term pressure across aluminum (upward) versus copper (downward). | Vedanta said it will invest USD 2 billion in Saudi Arabia copper projects, including a 400 KTPA greenfield smelter/refinery and a 300 KTPA copper rod project, plus an existing 125 KTPA rod mill. Production is planned for full swing in Q4 FY26 as Saudi copper demand rises and is met via imports. | True North Copper secured about A$50.3m ($32.7m) in a heavily discounted underwritten equity placement to recapitalize its Queensland copper projects. The news is company-specific and unlikely to shift broader copper prices in the next weeks. | Vedanta is reported to plan about $2bn in investments in major copper projects in Saudi Arabia. The announcement is likely to support market sentiment for copper by reinforcing expectations around future supply development and associated construction and procurement activity, even though near-term production timing remains uncertain.

MEED reports Vedanta is seeking to invest $2bn in copper projects in Saudi Arabia. If confirmed, the move would signal continued development of copper capacity and related spend in the near term. With details unavailable due to paywall, the immediate effect on spot prices is uncertain but cautiously supportive for copper. | Vedanta will invest $2 billion in Saudi Arabia to build a 400,000 t copper smelter/refinery and 300,000 t copper rod facilities, after closing an Indian smelter following an accident. Initial rod mill work starts first, with full production planned for 2026, boosting copper processing capacity visibility. | GT Resources announced a Phase I BHEM geophysical survey and reconnaissance work at its North Rock copper-nickel-PGE project in Ontario. The program targets historic holes to find net-textured to massive sulphides beneath shallow drilling, with a historic copper grade noted, but no near-term production impact. | GT Resources announced a BHEM survey and reconnaissance at its North Rock copper–nickel–PGE project in Ontario, targeting historic holes around the Beaver Pond zone. The firm also notes pre-construction work at Hod Maden. Overall, the update supports copper-focused sentiment.

China’s scrap copper imports are expected to drop as some traders stop buying from the US amid rising US–China trade tensions and promised tariff hikes. The project is awaiting an environmental permit in four to six weeks and targets large-investment incentives for tax and customs relief. Renewables energy and stakeholder support are highlighted, pointing to improved future supply prospects for copper. | McEwen Copper is raising $2.5 billion to advance the Los Azules copper mine in Argentina. Minnesota’s DNR is postponing a decision on permits for NewRange Copper Nickel’s proposed copper and nickel mine on the Iron Range. Main Roads WA says thieves stole about 15,000 metres of copper cabling from light poles in Perth, with overall losses of roughly 800,000 metres over five to six years. Patriot Lithium says it has secured an option to acquire a 90% stake in Zambia’s Kitumba copper exploration license next to the Kitumba copper deposit. Near-term supply effects appear limited. | VR Resources says drilling has begun at its Empire copper–gold project in Ontario, targeting strong IP (chargeability) anomalies identified by a recent DCIP survey and related EM conductivity features. Rio Tinto and Sumitomo Metal Mining signed a term sheet for a joint venture to develop the Winu copper-gold project in Western Australia, with SMM paying Rio Tinto $399 million for a 30% share. Copper output is expected at 780,000-850,000 tons in FY2025 versus 660,000-720,000 in FY2024, alongside broader copper growth ambitions. | Rio Tinto and Sumitomo Metal Mining signed a term sheet for a joint venture on the Winu copper-gold project in Western Australia, with SMM paying $399 million for a 30% stake. Definitive agreements and studies are targeted for 1H 2025, signaling continued copper project progress. | Rio Tinto said it expects 2025 copper output of 780,000–850,000 tonnes, up from its 2024 range, and also projected higher aluminium production. The update supports near-term supply expectations. | Rio Tinto told investors it plans to increase copper volumes by 18% next year and 40% by 2030 as it reduces reliance on Australian iron ore. The company also expects similar iron ore export volumes next year. The copper expansion is the key development for copper market sentiment. | Rio Tinto and Sumitomo Metal Mining agreed on Sumitomo acquiring a 30% stake in the Winu copper-gold project in Western Australia for $399m. The managing partner remains Rio Tinto, with JV terms targeted for mid-2025 and pre-feasibility and environmental approvals ongoing. | Barrick CEO Mark Bristow says clean-energy demand will outpace existing and planned copper production, widening a potential supply gap after 2026, with future supply likely in challenging jurisdictions. The piece highlights Argentina’s improved mining policy, a secured permit for Los Azules, and the role of recycling. | pv magazine reports that renewables and EVs are driving copper shortages faster than expected. The change already lifted LME aluminum prices as traders expect reduced Chinese export flows and tighter global supply. | The piece highlights China’s decision to cancel export tax rebates for products including aluminum and copper starting Dec. 1. It states the announcement had an immediate market impact, with LME aluminum prices surging as traders expect reduced Chinese export flows and lower global supply. | The piece highlights China’s decision to cancel export tax rebates for several products, including aluminium and copper, effective December 1. Traders expect reduced Chinese exports, tightening supply and supporting higher prices. | Insider Monkey highlights China’s decision to cancel export tax rebates for several products, including aluminium and copper products, effective Dec 1. SMM’s conference takeaway highlights copper: weak macro but improving liquidity signals, and notably tightening copper concentrate conditions (negative spot concentrate TCs, worse smelting economics) with expectations of supply constraints. The release supports longer-term project value but provides no near-term production or supply impact. | Canada Nickel reported assay results from drill BAN24-20 at its Bannockburn nickel sulphide project, including 1.11% nickel over 10.1 metres (and up to 1.32% Ni over 4.4 metres). An independent assessment says the abandoned Panguna copper mine in Bougainville still poses risks from unstable structures, landslides, and soil and water contamination. The claims extend ground and potential tailings areas for exploration north and south of the current property. The news is exploratory and not tied to near-term production changes.

India’s Ministry of Mines says expansion of Adani’s Kutch Copper smelter will add refined copper capacity, potentially reducing reliance on imports. The Mundra plant has started operations, with initial 500,000 t capacity and plans to double later. India currently imports hundreds of thousands of tonnes annually due to a supply gap. | XXIX Metal Corp. completed its all-share acquisition of Cuprum Corp. and the 100% owned Thierry Copper Project, expanding its copper resource portfolio in Quebec and Ontario alongside its Opemiska project. The company was renamed and shares begin trading under a new TSXV ticker on December 11, 2024. | Lundin Mining will sell two European mines for up to $1.52 billion to raise cash and strengthen its balance sheet. Proceeds fund its South American copper growth strategy, including the Vicuna operations and joint projects with BHP. The deal also includes copper-linked pricing thresholds for part of the payment. | Armenian copper concentrate production rose 8.9% in January–October 2024 to 258,497.5 tons year-on-year, according to the national statistics committee. The report also notes changes in zinc and molybdenum concentrates, but the primary copper signal is higher concentrate supply, likely not enough for an immediate price move. | The piece says the Chilean peso strengthens against the US dollar because commodities rise, with copper up more than 2%—its best performance since November. It attributes the move to China’s 2025 policy shift toward easier monetary conditions, while noting near-term uncertainty from upcoming US inflation data.

China’s Politburo promised more proactive fiscal policy and moderately loose monetary policy, aimed at stabilizing property and markets. The report says industrial metal prices rebounded and notes copper trading above USD9,200/t as demand prospects improved, supported by expectations of stronger growth. | Tucson says copper wire thefts from street lights have surged, with more than 445,000 feet stolen in six months and over $1.3 million spent on repairs. The city is seeking countermeasures but still needs ongoing copper replacements. | China’s November copper imports hit a one-year high at 528,000 tons, driven by solid end-demand, declining inventories, and more shipments from Africa.
