The article also cites higher copper production and 13% higher net imports, plus duty exemptions and a new smelter coming online. It expects continued demand growth. | Capstone Copper published its 2023 sustainability report, highlighting progress on “The Copper Mark,” renewable electricity coverage, and sustainability/performance metrics across its copper assets in Arizona, Mexico, and Chile. Immediate impact on copper prices appears limited. | Copper rallied about 1.76% early Monday, with price attempting to break above the 50-day EMA and viewed as near “fair value.” The piece links potential upside to inflation dynamics and hopes for China stimulus that could increase housing demand. The strategy favors buying dips above key support. | Norilsk Nickel forecasts a copper market surplus of about 200,000 metric tons in 2024, expecting rebalancing only in 2025 as demand grows. It notes copper prices have already fallen since May amid mixed industrial outlooks, pointing to near-term bearish sentiment. | GT Resources reported 2024 drill results from the Canalask Nickel-Copper Project in Yukon, including copper-rich skarn-like mineralization and nickel intersections (e.g., up to ~2.4% Ni and ~1.84% Cu in sections). The company cites historic drill intercepts and outlines planned geophysics and sampling before drilling in 2025. | Manx Telecom will begin phasing out the Isle of Man’s copper telecom network in 2025, completing the transition to fibre broadband by 2029. The company says fibre rollout is already advanced. The change affects copper usage in telecoms, but it appears small and gradual versus global markets, limiting price impact. | Manx Telecom plans to begin phasing out the Isle of Man’s copper telecom network next year, replacing it with fibre optic broadband under a £50 million investment. Support for reopening rises when job losses are highlighted. | Canadian telecom executives told a Senate committee that copper wire theft is rising, disrupting services and harming emergency operations. They urged tougher criminal penalties and rules targeting resale of stolen telecom copper, including tighter scrap/recycling controls and traceability. Officials note copper can be melted down, complicating enforcement. | Canadian officials told a Senate committee that copper wire theft is rising and hard to prevent, with telecom carriers reporting over 1,300 incidents since January 2022 and copper theft responsible for most cases. Theft often causes network outages and collateral damage to fibre lines, prompting calls for stronger enforcement and laws.

Southern Hemisphere Mining announced thick, higher-grade copper-equivalent drilling intercepts at its Chilean Llahuin copper-gold-molybdenum project, extending strike length and indicating deeper, still-untested expansion targets. The company plans further diamond/RC drilling and an MT survey, potentially boosting resource upgrade expectations in the first half of next year. | ING flags worsening 2025 copper headwinds after Trump’s election, citing potential US tariffs and a stronger dollar that could depress industrial demand. Michigan’s House Appropriations Committee backed a $50 million state grant for Highland Copper’s Copperwood underground copper mine in Michigan’s Upper Peninsula. The company operates multiple copper mines and projects across the Americas. The notice may affect near-term sentiment, but it provides no production or guidance details.

Copper prices surged on pre-buying ahead of tariff announcements, then collapsed after copper was exempted from import taxes. Analysts interpret the fall as a verdict on near-term economic prospects and slowing demand, particularly in China, rather than a copper-specific supply shock, pressuring the metal’s outlook. | Indigenous groups in Peru reportedly suspended a blockade of Glencore’s copper mine. The move likely eases near-term disruption risk to copper concentrate/production and could improve supply expectations over the coming days. That typically supports copper prices versus scenarios where the blockade continues or expands. | SMM says China’s copper foil operating rate rose in March to 71.82%, with both lithium-battery and electronic-circuit segments improving. Finished-product inventory/output fell as downstream orders and shipments picked up post–Chinese New Year. April is forecast to see higher operating rates, though weaker copper prices could delay stockpiling. | Barrick proposes renaming itself by dropping “gold” and emphasizing its pivot to copper. The company plans a US$7 billion copper mine in Pakistan and is expanding a major Zambian copper operation, aligning strategy with expected copper demand and potential future supply constraints. Shareholders vote in May. | Copper Fox updates on BLM permitting for drilling at its Mineral Mountain porphyry copper property in Arizona. The EA under NEPA has been published and a 30-day public comment period started April 7, 2025. The biological evaluation addresses ESA/other wildlife rules, with drilling deemed not likely adversely affecting an identified cactus. | Spain’s Civil Guard arrested 23 people in Operation Kupfer, dismantling a copper-theft ring that targeted PV plants in Andalusia and Extremadura (and nearby areas). Authorities recovered about 8.5 tons of stolen copper and opened further investigations as part of a wider effort to curb copper theft in solar installations. | Barrick said partners in Pakistan’s Reko Diq copper-gold joint venture approved an updated feasibility study and conditionally cleared phase-one development spending, pending up to $3 billion in limited recourse financing. The go-ahead allows major works this year and keeps first copper production on track for end-2028. | Antofagasta’s CEO said U.S. tariff turmoil and China retaliation have already sent copper prices lower, but he expects limited copper supply and resilience from AI-driven data-center and renewable demand. He noted no confirmed special copper tariffs yet and suggested mining investment could remain supported, keeping the market supported. | NRL says exploration in Chagai, Balochistan has identified copper-bearing zones, with initial drilling showing copper grades of 0.23%–0.48% and plans for advanced drilling in May 2025. It also has gold findings and further exploration licensing, but no near-term output changes. | Escalating U.S.-China tariffs and retaliatory Chinese sanctions have triggered a macro-driven commodities selloff, with copper futures plunging about 11% and the Bloomberg Commodity Index marking its worst week in over a year. Analysts warn trade-war recession risks could push copper further lower, including revised Chile price forecasts. | Codelco’s chairman said the company is not making short-term decisions despite US tariffs taking effect April 4, with copper currently exempt but under a Section 232 investigation. The article notes LME and CME copper prices have fallen sharply since tariffs, alongside record-low negative concentrate TC/RCs reflecting tight concentrate conditions. | Codelco CEO Ruben Alvarado said the Chilean miner targets the upper end of its 2025 copper output range, expecting 1.39 million tonnes, supported by improving operational performance. The firm is also weighing a return to bond financing and whether to ship additional spot copper to the U.S., factoring customer needs amid tariff expectations. | The piece argues that China’s manufacturing sector remains stable despite global uncertainty from US tariffs. It highlights that Chinese buyers have continued making copper purchases despite recent market volatility. This suggests resilient copper demand and supportive sentiment for industrial supply chains. | Comex copper futures settled 1% lower at $4.1275, signaling bearish near-term momentum for copper prices. The article is essentially a market “data talk” update, with no additional supply/demand or policy catalysts described, but the move itself indicates downside pressure.

New Zealand’s Commerce Commission seeks views on deregulating Chorus’ remaining copper phone and broadband services, allowing gradual withdrawal of a network still used by over 100,000 homes and businesses. Chorus argues copper is aging and less reliable than fibre, wireless, and satellite. Cabinet repurposing is mentioned, but no market-wide price effects are quantified. | Fresno County advanced an ordinance to criminalize possessing and transporting suspected stolen copper wire, allowing citations or arrests for amounts above 10 pounds. Officials cite over $3 million in copper-wire theft losses in 2023 and hope stronger deterrence. Possible legal challenges and state preemption concerns are noted. | Antofagasta forecasts strong demand for copper despite uncertainty around tariffs. The expectation of continued demand should underpin near-term copper market sentiment, even if trade policy adds some volatility. With no further details provided, the impact is assessed as supportive for copper prices in the coming days. | Aston Bay announced a strategic partnership with Ocean Partners, including up to 80% development financing and an offtake agreement covering 100% of Storm’s near-surface copper products, plus a royalty tranche advance from Taurus. Funds are earmarked for exploration, PFS, permitting, and long-lead equipment to advance copper development. | SMM data show copper pipe & tube enterprise operating rates at 85.23% in March, up sharply MoM, with large firms near 89.94% and April expected at 85.81%. Demand stayed supported by air-conditioner production and exports, though tariffs increased export uncertainty. Raw material inventory ratios were slightly lower MoM. | Central Asia Metals said it has completed the sale of its 76.1% stake in Copper Bay Ltd, which holds mineral rights to a copper tailings project in Chile. The USD 7.5m cash deal includes deferred payments tied to producing 7,500 and 15,000 tonnes of copper, with Guardian Metals taking full ownership. | EU member states approved retaliatory tariffs totaling $23bn against US 25% steel and aluminium tariffs. Measures take effect in stages from mid-April, late May and early December. The EU seeks a negotiated “zero-for-zero” industrial deal, but retaliation raises trade-friction risks that can curb aluminium demand and output. | The EU approved retaliatory tariffs on $23 billion of goods in response to Trump’s 25% US tariffs on imported steel and aluminum. Tariffs will be phased in from mid-April and May, with additional measures later in the year. EU leadership favors a negotiated, zero-for-zero industrial deal, but talks remain unsettled. | Rio Tinto says Trump’s administration could help unblock its Resolution Copper project in Arizona, citing plans to speed permitting and boost critical-mineral output. The article highlights potential upside for U.S. copper supply and notes another U.S. project (Twin Metals) that includes nickel, though details are secondary. | The EU member states approved retaliatory tariffs worth $23 billion in response to Trump’s 25% tariffs on imported US steel and aluminium. Retaliation will enter in stages starting April 15, May 15, and December 1. The EU prefers a negotiated “zero-for-zero” deal, but talks remain uncertain. | Sen. Smith introduced a bill to ban copper mining near the Boundary Waters. The proposal would restrict new and/or ongoing copper extraction in that area, potentially reducing supply growth and affecting investor expectations for copper project development and output timelines over the coming weeks. | The EU has approved retaliatory tariffs of $23 billion against US 25% steel and aluminium tariffs. Measures will be phased in from April 15, May 15 and December 1, with goods unspecified. The EU cites economic harm and seeks negotiated resolution, but the tit-for-tat risk is bearish for industrial metals. | The EU member states approved retaliatory tariffs totaling $23 billion against US President Trump’s 25% tariffs on imported steel and aluminium. Retaliation will be implemented in stages starting April 15, May 15 and December 1. The EU seeks negotiations, but trade conflict risks near-term demand disruption, pressuring aluminium sentiment. | EU member states approved retaliatory tariffs worth $23 billion against US 25% steel and aluminium tariffs. Measures take effect in stages (April 15, May 15, and Dec 1). The EU says the US tariffs harm the economy and seeks a negotiated “zero-for-zero” deal, but talks remain uncertain. | The EU member states approved retaliatory tariffs worth $23 billion in response to Trump’s 25% US tariffs on imported steel and aluminium. Retaliation is scheduled to start in stages (April 15, May 15, and Dec. 1). The EU seeks a negotiated settlement, but the measures add trade uncertainty. | EU member states approved retaliatory tariffs worth $23bn in goods in response to Trump’s 25% US tariffs on imported steel and aluminium. The measures enter in stages from mid-April to December. The EU prefers negotiation, but the tariff escalation raises near-term trade and demand uncertainty for aluminium and industrial metals including copper. | Chile’s mining minister said the U.S. tariff framework could be favorable for Chile if copper tariffs are applied, as Chile faces the baseline 10% while other countries received higher rates. The U.S. is still probing copper tariffs, and uncertainty could weigh on short-term demand. | Nickel prices have fallen sharply to levels last seen since 2020, driven by reciprocal tariff announcements that spooked growth expectations and triggered a broad sell-off in base metals. The article warns that renewed recession fears could add volatility. It also notes lower nickel and copper would reduce the 304 stainless surcharge. | Mexican exporters report aluminum and steel shipments to the U.S. have fallen about 25% after Trump imposed tariffs on steel, aluminum, and vehicles. Officials are seeking clarity from U.S. Customs and Border Protection, noting many goods are stranded in Mexico. Reduced aluminum export flow may pressure prices lower in coming days.

Koryx Copper announced completion of a dual listing on Namibia’s NSX under symbol KYX, with trading starting April 10, 2025. The company emphasized improved liquidity and access for Namibian investors. No new project, production, or supply-related information was provided, implying limited immediate impact on copper fundamentals. | SMM data show copper plate/sheet and strip industry operating rates rose to 69.14% in March, beating expectations and indicating improving production momentum. April is forecast to rise further to 74.46% as end-use demand recovers (NEV, electronics, power). However, copper price volatility could suppress orders. | A Canadian company received approvals to begin exploratory drilling for copper (and gold) at the Bend Deposit in northern Wisconsin. The project is early-stage (eight holes, years before development). It may support longer-run domestic supply expectations, but no short-term production or trade impact is indicated. | Aurubis’ CEO says copper may be exempt from reciprocal tariffs, so direct impact is limited, but uncertainty from possible US Section 232 copper tariffs could delay customer investments and reduce future copper demand. He also argues demand is stable in energy and electrification, though trade risks remain. | Aurubis says copper smelting capacity is moving closer to mines as countries pursue supply-chain independence amid tariffs and geopolitics. It warns against overcapacity, while noting Europe and the US will need additional smelting capacity. Meanwhile, copper concentrate TCs/RCs are at record lows due to tight concentrate supply. | Viscount Mining reports deep drilling at its Passiflora project, identifying continuous sulphide mineralization over ~1,100m and finding chalcopyrite as a copper indicator within a copper-porphyry-style alteration setting. The announcement is exploratory, with assay work pending and no direct near-term supply impact. | Adani Enterprises says its Kutch Copper facility will start refining copper concentrate and produce anodes within four weeks, with capacity expansion approvals. The move comes as Asian smelters face negative TC/RCs due to tight concentrate supply. Copper prices rebounded sharply on tariff pause, but the new smelter adds supply expectations. | Cochilco data show Chile’s Codelco copper output fell 6% year-over-year in February to 98,100 metric tons. Output at Collahuasi dropped 62% to 17,000 tons. While BHP’s Escondida rose 16%, the overall Chile supply picture is skewed toward lower copper volumes.

Hudbay Minerals and several unions signed a letter of commitment to provide union contractors an opportunity in building the Copper World open-pit copper mine near Tucson. While permits are in place, lawsuits could still challenge approvals. The agreement signals progress toward construction workforce planning and potential copper supply continuity. | Ivanhoe Mines says Kamoa-Kakula has entered a new growth phase after starting its on-site copper smelter. Additional imported hydropower (rising from 70MW to 100MW) supported record March copper production and higher concentrator throughput, enabling more anode output and improved cost performance. | Three men in Cantonment, Florida were charged with stealing and selling nearly two tons of phone cables and uninsulated copper utility wire. The wire, tied to AT&T and Pike Electric, was burned to remove insulation and sold as scrap to a yard in Alabama. Additional fiber lines were reportedly damaged. | Anglo American’s CEO says the world needs around 80 large copper mines by 2040 and highlights Chile’s copper supply outlook. Los Bronces production fell in 2024 due to care and maintenance, while a joint mine plan with Codelco is expected to improve output mainly from 2030, subject to permitting delays. | Freeport CEO Kathleen Quirk said US tariffs and possible Section 232 measures could slow economic growth and keep copper prices weak, putting US production growth at risk. She noted higher costs likely pass through the supply chain and that demand impacts are already appearing, despite long-term copper fundamentals remaining intact. | The alert details new U.S. Section 232 tariffs effective March 12, 2025 that raise and expand duties on imported steel, aluminum, and certain downstream derivative products, including removal of most exclusion processes and added documentation requirements. It targets steel/aluminum supply chains, with no stated copper linkage.

CNBC discusses whether Trump’s reciprocal tariffs could trigger a commodities “super-cycle.” It cites higher copper front-month prices but warns tariffs may raise slowdown risks that pressure copper short term. It says nickel is unlikely to surge, while aluminium is among materials expected to be repriced upward as trade tensions persist. | South Korea is reportedly requesting favorable treatment because copper products may face potential U.S. tariffs. If implemented, the tariffs would likely raise end-user costs and curb near-term demand expectations for copper, creating downward pressure on copper prices in the coming days to weeks. | South Korea urged the US to avoid imposing Section 232 tariffs on Korean copper products, arguing the imports are small and not a national-security threat. It warned tariffs could raise US domestic copper prices and disrupt copper foil supply for battery makers, threatening investments and jobs amid an ongoing Commerce review. | SMM reports refined copper rod operating rates rose in March but missed expectations, with consumption suppressed by secondary copper rod and some destocking-related maintenance. Copper prices fell sharply after trade-war tariff concerns. However, new orders and on-hand orders increased, supporting improved consumption and an April operating-rate forecast of 71.44%. | SMM reports March 2025 copper anode producer operating rates rose MoM overall, but expects April to fall to 48.80%. Ore-based rates are projected to edge up, while scrap-based rates drop sharply to 38.35% due to reduced scrap supply after copper’s mid-April price fall, import gaps, and producer losses. | The piece outlines U.S. reinstatement of full tariffs on steel (25%) and aluminum (25%) with a higher China rate and a 90-day pause that excludes steel and aluminum. It emphasizes continued elevated tariff burdens until at least July 9, 2025, likely weighing on aluminum-linked demand and prices near term.

Copper prices rose about 0.6% as markets benefited from U.S. tariff exemptions on some electronics, hopes for additional Chinese stimulus, and a weaker dollar. The piece also cites technical support after copper’s prior drop and notes China’s loan growth and TSF acceleration, boosting near-term demand expectations. | Copper producers in Chile discuss tariff “chaos” after reciprocal US tariffs that temporarily exempt copper, while a Section 232 investigation could impose future copper duties. The piece links tariff uncertainty to falling copper prices, rising Comex inventories near 112,000 tons, weaker arbitrage, and tight concentrate-driven record-low TC/RCs. | Rio Tinto’s copper CEO said US tariffs are driving copper market volatility but viewed as edge adjustments rather than a fundamental demand/supply shift. She cited copper exemption from a continuing 10% tariff, potential tailwinds for domestic US operations, and challenges because tariffs also affect Rio Tinto’s US-bound aluminium business. | Kincora Copper said it will hold an investor webinar after amending an earn-in and joint venture agreement with AngloGold Ashanti to pursue a second joint venture in Australia’s Northern Junee-Narromine Belt. The partnership supports total exploration expenditures up to $100 million, but remains exploration-stage and not an immediate production change. | U.S. GoldMining plans to start an initial economic assessment for its Whistler gold-copper project in Alaska. Management cites strong metal prices and recent drilling results as justification for moving forward, including use of a higher gold price assumption. No production timing or supply changes are announced. | Talon Metals said drill hole 25TK0561 at its Tamarack nickel-copper project intersected 16.09 meters of mixed massive sulphide with 10%–80% sulphide content, potentially extending mineralization at depth. Management links the result to earlier BHEM modeling success and suggests potential tonnage addition as feasibility work progresses. | Talon Metals announced a mixed massive sulphide intercept (16.09m) at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project, with sulphide content indicating higher nickel grades. The company frames results as extending the CGO East Zone below the current resource and supporting potential early tonnage, but it remains feasibility-stage exploration news. | Kincora Copper announced an expanded earn-in/JV with AngloGold Ashanti to broaden drilling across the Nyngan, Nevertire and Nevertire South projects in NSW, targeting copper-gold porphyry systems. The update notes ongoing scout drilling and prior anomalous copper results, but remains exploration-focused with no production or logistics changes. | Chile, Canada and Peru told the U.S. not to apply Section 232 copper tariffs, arguing copper imports support U.S. supply-chain and defense needs. The probe was launched in February but has no detailed findings yet. The suppliers and Freeport-McMoRan oppose duties, warning tariffs could benefit China. | President Trump issued expanded Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum, closing tariff loopholes and terminating exclusion processes. Aluminum tariffs rise from 10% to 25% and apply to additional aluminum derivatives, with no new or renewed product exclusions considered. The change is intended to counter excess capacity and national security risks. | Chile, Canada and Peru told the U.S. Commerce Department that copper imports do not threaten U.S. security and should be excluded from potential Section 232 tariffs. The probe follows Trump’s earlier steel/aluminium tariffs and could affect U.S. buying demand and pricing, while suppliers warn it may benefit China. | Citi said copper prices should decline more gradually over the next three months after Trump eased tariffs in the past week, while China bought on dips and U.S. scrap supply stayed tight due to stockpiling. Citi lifted its three‑month copper forecast and also raised its aluminum forecast on expected pricing support. | Destiny Copper announced BMI Group purchased a minority stake to help expand operations at Bioveld Niagara, aiming to scale its copper recovery to copper powder (CR-to-CP) production. The financing supports commercialization and potential new uses, increasing expectations for incremental copper supply and processing capacity in Canada. | Seven trade partners (Chile, Canada, Peru, Indonesia, South Korea, China and the EU) filed comments against a U.S. national security review of copper imports, urging the Commerce Department not to impose tariffs or quotas. Their pushback highlights the risk of restrictive policy that could disrupt copper trade flows.

SMM reports that frequent tariff-policy changes in April have driven large swings in copper prices, while enamelled copper wire remains in peak season. Many firms cite strong April orders and little immediate tariff impact, though concerns persist that US-bound home appliance and power tool demand may be delayed or canceled. | Prospect Resources raised about A$15.2m ($9.5m) from First Quantum Minerals via an equity placement for its Mumbezhi copper project in Zambia. FQM becomes a 15% shareholder and a technical partner, supporting exploration and phase-two drilling. Total proceeds aim to strengthen working capital; no immediate production changes are outlined. | Chile, Canada and Peru—supplying most US refined copper—oppose a US Commerce probe that could impose Section 232 national-security tariffs on copper imports. Industry and traders warn tariffs could damage the global economy and potentially benefit China, while alternatives like export controls are discussed. | Kincora Copper said it will host a briefing webinar after amending its earn-in and joint venture agreement with a subsidiary of AngloGold Ashanti for a second copper-gold exploration joint venture in the Northern Junee-Narromine Belt. Total expenditures could reach up to $100 million, supported by renewed partner funding. | Zambia’s consumer advocacy group warns proposed U.S. tariffs—including a 25% tariff on copper imports—could reduce the competitiveness of Zambian copper exports to the U.S. It expects greater global copper price volatility, worsening foreign-exchange shortages, and added economic strain as the kwacha weakens and costs rise. | BHP said its third-quarter copper output rose 10% year on year to 513,200 tonnes, boosted by 16% higher production at Escondida in northern Chile. Iron ore output was flat as operational improvements offset earlier tropical cyclone disruptions. The copper rise points to near-term supply support.

BHP said escalating U.S.-China trade tensions could hurt the economy, but its direct tariff impact was limited. It reported a slight iron ore output dip from cyclones, while copper production rose 10% to 513,200 tonnes, boosted by improved Escondida operations. The company expects Chile copper output in the upper half of guidance for 2025. | BHP says Chile’s Escondida mine lifted copper output by 20%, delivering a record nine-month production total of 1.5 million tonnes. BHP also increased copper production in South Australia. The company additionally outlined a major investment to expand copper operations over the next decade. | BHP cautioned that a tariff war could slow global growth and fracture trade flows, but it reported higher copper output. Third-quarter copper volumes rose 10%, driven by increased Escondida mine production in Chile. BHP also signaled plans to extend Escondida’s life, keeping output in the upper part of guidance. | BHP said third-quarter copper output at Escondida rose 10% versus a year earlier, driven by stronger material mined and higher concentrator feed grade. Guidance for FY25 copper remains unchanged but is expected to land in the upper half of the range. BHP also plans extensions to the Los Colorados concentrator beyond FY29. | BHP said Escondida copper output rose 10% year on year, aided by stronger mined material and higher concentrator feed grade. FY25 guidance remains 1.18–1.30 million tonnes, expected in the upper half due to operational strength offsetting strikes, sea swells and a Chile power outage. BHP also plans growth beyond FY29. | BHP said US tariffs have a limited direct impact on its business but may weigh on demand via slower growth and fragmented trade. It reported record copper output for the first nine months and reiterated 2025 copper production guidance of 1.84–2.04 million tonnes, implying mixed near-term effects for copper. | Sinomine Resource Group says it has started stripping at its Kitumba copper mine in Zambia and targets copper production to begin in September 2026. The company plans to invest about $560 million in the project, according to a WeChat post cited by Reuters. Near-term effects on copper prices appear limited due to the long timeline. | Camino Minerals completed its acquisition of Cuprum Resources Chile, which owns the construction-ready Puquios copper project in Chile, co-developed with Nittetsu in a 50/50 JV. The release discusses permitting, project economics and next financing steps, which are copper-specific but not linked to near-term production changes. | The EBRD plans to lend up to €20 million to Uzbekistan’s Artikul Aziya Kabel to expand production of copper cable and wire, including specialist solar cables, and to boost exports. The project supports grid enhancements and renewable deployment, potentially lifting near-term expectations for copper demand. | A Rio Rancho business says thieves broke into transformers twice in three months, stealing copper wiring and causing outages and losses. Owners estimate major damage in January and smaller losses during the second theft. The story is about local theft with no reported impact on broader copper supply or pricing. | Realtor.com reports that Trump’s 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports could raise construction costs for newly built residences. Builders may pass higher costs to consumers or delay finishing construction, reducing housing supply. This could push rents higher in multiple US metros as tariffs dampen development plans. | The U.S. Forest Service issued a 60-day notice to republish the Final Environmental Impact Statement for the Resolution Copper project in Arizona. The company frames the step as progress toward permitting for a major potential underground copper mine, improving near-term expectations for copper supply.

The article links volatile tariff- and trade-policy-driven copper price swings to downstream impacts and battery inventory management. It then highlights AMTech’s €composite current collector project using metal–polymer–metal structures that reduce copper/aluminium usage via “one-step” vacuum evaporation, aiming to lower costs and improve battery safety. | China’s copper plate/sheet and strip exports rose to 10,543 mt in March (+40.5% MoM, +5.2% YoY), with cumulative growth of 2.65%. Exports to the US fell 57% YoY, but SMM says tariffs haven’t directly hit orders. April exports may stay high, though downstream demand is suppressed. | Copper rose to a two-week high as a weaker U.S. dollar encouraged CTA/fund buying and traders covered shorts, breaching key moving averages. However, sentiment remains cautious because Trump’s import tariffs threaten growth and industrial metal demand. Aluminium and nickel were also up, but driven largely by macro flows. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. said Phase 1 construction is complete on an all-season restricted access road to its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project, replacing floatplane/ATV logistics with year-round road access. The company is starting Phase 2 and plans further upgrades and 2025 next steps to expand resources, potentially improving future supply prospects. | Glenstar Minerals announced it has hired Midnight Sun Drilling to conduct the first-ever drill program at its Green Monster nickel-copper-cobalt property in Nevada. The company plans to define the extent of mineralization after permitting and expects updates soon, citing earlier sampling with meaningful Cu and Ni values. | Glenstar Minerals hired Midnight Sun Drilling to run the first-ever drill program on its Green Monster nickel-copper-cobalt property in Nevada. The project is undrilled to date and targets shallow RC drilling based on prior surface sampling that included copper and nickel grades. Results are expected in coming days. | CEO.CA’s “Inside the Boardroom” interviews Vizsla Copper’s CEO on its exploration strategy for a major copper-gold discovery in B.C. The piece argues global demand may support copper prices and could drive upside for Vizsla. It is largely sentiment-focused, with no quantified supply or inventory details. | CEO.CA interviews Yukon Metals’ CEO about upcoming drilling plans at Star River and AZ targets, highlighting accessibility and high-grade surface samples. The piece specifically references AZ as copper targets. As an exploration program with no production or quantified resource changes, any near-term effect on copper prices is likely minimal.

Peru’s Antamina copper mine launched a full security shutdown after an operations manager was killed and another senior employee was injured at a mining camp. The company said it is investigating the accident’s cause. The stoppage raises near-term copper supply concerns, likely weighing on copper prices. | Canada One Mining received a 5-year exploration drilling permit for its Copper Dome Project in British Columbia, including plans for a 2025 field season to define drill targets. The release highlights historical copper intercepts and proximity to Hudbay’s Copper Mountain Mine, but provides no near-term supply impact. | A trade-war-driven IMF downgrade is expected to pressure US growth and global industrial output. The report notes tight physical copper supply in China and strong domestic demand, but warns declining Chinese exports and weaker industrial production could tip the market into surplus, with positioning risks adding downside pressure. | Talon Metals said feasibility study drilling at its Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project found massive sulphide mineralization extending laterally east of the prior southern resource boundary, including a 1.9m intercept logged at ~70% sulphides. The release continues drilling in Minnesota and references planned rigs in Michigan. | Vedanta is weighing a US listing to raise roughly $1 billion, with proceeds intended for its Zambian copper assets. The funding plan may strengthen market expectations for continued investment in copper production capacity, offering mild upward sentiment for copper prices over the coming weeks. | The article headline says copper is extending its advance, citing U.S.-China optimism and a broader risk-on tone. That framing suggests improving macro trade expectations that can lift near-term demand sentiment for copper and support prices in the first days of trading. | Antamina, Peru’s largest copper mine, shut down operations under a full safety stop after a fatal accident at its Yanacancha camp. The incident is under investigation, with another worker injured. As a major copper producer, the stoppage can reduce near-term copper supply. | U.S. copper imports are running ahead of expectations even as tariff concerns weigh on growth forecasts. Goldman notes tight Chinese physical supply and resilient domestic demand, but flags trade-war-driven export and industrial weakness that could swing copper toward surplus. Positioning is near max short on LME, adding uncertainty to near-term direction. | Two men were arrested in Topeka, Kansas, after police found copper wire stripped from a downtown building during a burglary call. Suspects face charges including theft and intent to steal scrap metal. The report is limited to this local incident, with no indication of wider copper supply disruptions.

The broker wrap highlights Morgan Stanley’s note that Resolution Copper, a joint project of Rio Tinto and BHP, received US Fast-41 status, with a key land-swap approval expected soon. The project could become a major US copper supply source, supporting near-term copper market sentiment. | Teck Resources reported Q1 results beating expectations, attributing improved profitability to higher base metal prices and higher copper sales volumes. Copper sales rose to 106,200 tonnes (+11% y/y), with revenue also ahead of estimates. The company maintained its 2025 forecast, supporting a constructive near-term copper outlook. | SMM reports March 2025 China customs data showing sizable MoM/YoY increases in exports of copper cathode rods (>6mm) and copper cathode wire (≤6mm). Growth is mainly driven by processing-trade categories, which helped stabilize volumes. Exports are near April 2024 highs and remain optimistic for 2025. | The piece is a technical analysis of copper futures (HG), claiming the April 7, 2025 pullback completed a corrective wave and that a final motive leg (wave (5)) is underway. It suggests pullbacks staying above 4.03 imply more upside, with a potential near-term 3-wave pullback thereafter. | Teck Resources reported stronger-than-expected quarterly profit and higher revenue, attributing improved profitability to higher commodity prices and increased copper sales volumes. The firm also detailed copper output performance, including disruptions at its Quebrada Blanca mine in Chile, and reiterated guidance while monitoring potential U.S. tariff impacts. | Gunnison Copper announced completion of a second amendment to its Nebari credit agreement, including suspension of principal amortization until Jan 2026 and potential partial conversion to equity. The financing flexibility and extension mechanisms are conditional on 48C tax-credit proceeds and refinancing, supporting continued development at its copper projects. | China’s customs data show copper foil imports rising in March 2025 (up 8.26% YoY, 22.40% MoM) and exports rebounding sharply (up 6.97% YoY, 42.83% MoM). The increase is linked to higher demand from AI servers, PV and electronics, implying stronger near-term copper foil demand. | Teck says its QB2 copper mine in northern Chile is still ramping up poorly, with first-quarter output down 30% due to an 18-day maintenance outage and other disruptions. It now expects 2025 production at the lower end and costs at the higher end. | Copper cathode premiums are volatile as traders monitor potential US tariff developments under Section 232. Spot activity in the US is largely absent due to extreme LME/COMEX arbitrage swings, while oversupply from past tariff-response behavior and arbitrage-driven inflows are cited as pressuring premiums. Europe remains tight, but US weakness dominates. | NIU Group’s acquisition of Chile’s Minera Tres Valles (MTV) follows a plan to renew and scale up operations. MTV produces Grade-A 99.999% electrolytic copper cathodes and management targets much higher output after ramp-up, supported by an integrated SX/EW plant and processing expansion. The move suggests improved copper supply outlook. | Teck says ramp-up issues persist at its QB2 copper mine in northern Chile, citing maintenance downtime, power outages, and weather that reduced Q1 output to 60% of nameplate. The firm expects 2025 production at the low end and higher costs, suggesting weaker copper supply near term. | Vedanta is exploring a US IPO for its 80% owned Konkola Copper Mines in Zambia to raise about $1bn for mine development. The plan follows regained control after provisional liquidation and financing to restart operations, with production targets rising. More funding prospects can lift short-term copper supply expectations. | Teck CEO Jonathan Price said tariffs and macro uncertainty may raise volatility but expects higher demand for copper and zinc tied to industrial policy, defence spending, and electricity grid upgrades. The company also reported higher realized copper prices and sales volumes, while a shutdown and outages at Quebrada Blanca constrained copper output. | Peru’s Antamina copper mine, controlled by Glencore, BHP, Teck and Mitsubishi, is slowly restarting after an accident earlier in the week that killed one employee and injured another. Operations were paused on Tuesday during an investigation at the Yanacancha camp in Ancash, suggesting reduced near-term copper supply pressure as output resumes.

The Trump administration is pushing the Resolution Copper Mine in Arizona by accelerating the U.S. Forest Service’s timeline for a Final Environmental Impact Statement and Draft Record of Decision tied to a critical land exchange. The project, led by Rio Tinto and BHP, is aimed at large-scale domestic copper production. | Southern Copper Corporation announced details for its 1Q25 conference call to discuss first-quarter 2025 results and host a Q&A. Southern Copper is a major integrated copper producer operating in Mexico and Peru. However, the article contains no new production, guidance, or policy information affecting near-term copper prices. | Southern Copper reported 1Q25 results, including stable copper production and higher copper sales volumes, with revenues benefiting from higher copper prices (LME mentioned +1.9% vs 4Q24) and lower cash costs. The release also references general copper-market instability, but no new acute supply shock. | Southern Copper Corp. reported first-quarter net income of $945.9 million and revenue of $3.12 billion. The report is from an earnings snapshot and does not detail comparisons to analyst forecasts or guidance. As a copper producer, the update may affect short-term copper sentiment despite limited directional information. | California CHP arrested a man with about 1,000 lbs. of copper theft, and the article also cites Chile’s Operation Red Gold, which arrested a network allegedly stealing, storing, and exporting copper to produce cables. Large seizures and arrests may disrupt specific illicit flows, but likely won’t materially move global copper prices.

GreenX Metals expanded its Tannenberg Kupferschiefer-style copper project in Germany from 272km2 to 1,900km2, adding new exploration licence ground and citing additional historic drill intercepts with copper grades. The work is focused on relogging and geophysics; no near-term production or supply change is indicated. | The piece highlights a rebound case for commodities tied to infrastructure spending and electrification, claiming electrification will require sharply higher copper production and that materials demand will rise with supply-chain realignment. It also mentions nickel as part of electrification-related minerals and suggests pro-growth policies will support commodity prices. | Barrick Gold plans to rename itself Barrick Mining Corp. to better reflect its focus on growing gold and copper operations. The change is subject to shareholder approval in May and involves a NYSE ticker update. The news is supportive for copper sentiment but lacks specific near-term supply changes. | The piece attributes copper’s early-2025 volatility to tariff-related uncertainty, then highlights signs of stabilization: copper inventories are pressured, China’s refined output rose in March, and smelters are leveraging higher by-product prices. It argues copper may have found a floor and could resume upward movement soon.

SMM says China’s copper ore/concentrate shortages are pushing smelters to seek supplements. Scrap-derived anode copper supply is expanding, with higher scrap flows into smelting. It also notes concentrate TCs weakness and reduced anode imports, plus operational factors like maintenance, shaping feedstock availability. | SMM’s outlook for global copper foil highlights rising overcapacity in lithium battery copper foil, cut-throat competition, and falling processing fees. It forecasts operating rates recovering in April/May, but US reciprocal tariffs are expected to impact end-use demand and prolong inventory buildup, likely weighing on copper foil-related copper consumption. | Hydro signed a long-term, ~1 billion euro deal to supply low-carbon aluminium wire rod to NKT for power cable production supporting Europe’s grid upgrades. The contract runs 2026–2033 (option for more) and follows Hydro’s planned Karmøy smelter expansion, reinforcing aluminium demand and supply expectations; aluminium can substitute copper in grids. | Star Copper Corp. appointed a new chairman and announced a $6 million non-brokered private placement, including flow-through proceeds earmarked for exploration, drilling, permitting, and related work on its British Columbian copper projects (Star and Indata). The funds support asset development but do not signal immediate supply changes for markets. | SMM’s CCIE 2025 analysis expects China’s copper cathode rod capacity to keep rising to about 17.67Mt by end-2025, worsening overcapacity. Supply surplus is pressuring copper rod processing fees, contributing to lower operating rates amid weak consumption. It also notes uncertain export rebate effects and a shift toward anodes. | MAC Copper Limited reported Q1 2025 output of 8,644 tonnes with a 4.1% Cu grade from its CSA mine, noting the quarter as seasonally lowest. C1 increased to $1.91/lb due to lower volumes, while 2025 TC/RC benchmarks are lower, improving outlook. Overall, it’s company-specific without major new supply shocks.

Codelco said April copper output rose 22% year-on-year to about 105,000 tonnes, after recovering from a prior production low. Chairman Maximo Pacheco expects strong demand across Asia, especially China, and the US, noting tariff-driven shipment shifts and renewed smelter investment plans. | Glencore reported a 30% drop in first-quarter copper production. The reduction signals weaker near-term mine output versus the prior period, tightening supply expectations and typically weighing on copper prices in the short term as markets reassess available inventories and production timelines. | Power Metallic reported drill results from its Lion Zone at the Nisk polymetallic project, including copper-dominant massive sulphides and assays containing nickel. While the high-grade results may improve investor sentiment for future Cu/Ni resource potential, the article does not imply immediate production changes, so near-term price impact is likely limited. | Glencore kept most 2025 production guidance unchanged, but copper operations had a slow start. Q1 copper output fell to 167,900 tonnes, down 30% from a year earlier, due to lower ore mining rates, grades, and recoveries at Collahuasi, Antapaccay and KCC. The firm expects stronger performance later in 2025. | Erongored says it loses nearly N$5 million a year from copper theft, citing repeated incidents including theft of power poles at Swakopmund and Henties Bay. The vandalism disrupts electricity and affects water supply facilities. The utility warns perpetrators and works with police to prosecute. | Record gold prices and valuable smelting byproducts are offsetting losses from deeply negative copper concentrate treatment/refining charges in China. Despite tighter concentrate availability and worsening profitability, smelters are unlikely to cut major output, helping refined copper production growth this year. | Zambia’s Mines Minister said copper production in Q1 2025 rose about 30% year on year to roughly 224,000 tonnes, up from around 173,000 tonnes in Q1 2024. The increase was attributed to higher output from Konkola Copper Mines and Mopani Copper Mines. | Jubilee Metals says it is in the final stages of trials at its Roan copper concentrator in Zambia. Initial results suggest the plant can boost copper production rates from high-grade ore, and delays were due to process adjustments. Jubilee also expects its Munkoyo open-pit ramp-up to reach 8,500 tonnes/month. | Police in Basildon issued an appeal in an investigation into the theft of copper pipes from a property in Alcotes last May. The theft reportedly caused damage to homes and neighboring properties. Officers are seeking information on a suspect’s whereabouts; no broader supply or market impact is mentioned. | MarketsandMarkets forecasts North America’s copper tubes market will rise from USD 3.18 billion in 2024 to USD 3.44 billion by 2030, citing construction, HVACR demand, and energy-efficient/green building trends. It attributes growth to copper’s thermal conductivity, durability, and recyclability, but provides no near-term market disruption details. | Glencore reported first-quarter copper output down 30% to 167,900 tonnes, despite keeping full-year 2025 production guidance unchanged. Nickel production fell 21% as well. The company cited tariff-driven uncertainty and potential trade-flow dislocation, while analysts reacted to the weaker quarterly physical supply. | Hudbay completed its acquisition of Mitsubishi Materials’ 25% interest in the Copper Mountain Mine in British Columbia, bringing ownership to 100%. The company said the added attributable production supports its strategy to increase copper output in tier-1 jurisdictions, reinforcing its position as a major Canadian copper producer. | Metals and raw material producers fell in tandem with copper as fears of economic fallout from the U.S.-China trade war intensified. Copper futures tumbled 5.5% on weak U.S. Q1 data and a drop in Chinese export orders in April, ending May delivery at $4.56/lb and down 9.2% for the month.

Sibanye-Stillwater’s Mount Lyell Mine in Tasmania has been out of production since 2014, but resource mapping and updated mineral resource estimates indicate large remaining copper resources. The company is continuing a feasibility study and may build a new concentrator plant if it restarts mining, potentially improving future supply outlook. | Copper rose on the LME after a sharp sell-off as traders gained confidence that U.S. trade talks with China could progress and ease tariff uncertainty. The move followed weak prior demand signals and was supported by continuing inventory outflows, with COMEX and China premium metrics indicating tighter near-term supply conditions. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. says it has started a 4-week airborne geophysical survey and a greenfields exploration program at its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project in northwestern Ontario. The work aims to refine subsurface targets for future prospecting, sampling, and drilling. No production or supply change is announced. | The piece argues copper’s downturn is accelerating, citing a drop of more than 6% in the U.S. session and technical moves below key retracement levels. It links the move to weaker economic indicators (ADP employment and GDP) and risk-off sentiment, suggesting the “big slide” in copper may resume soon. | Versamet completed a US$35 million copper stream acquisition on the Huachocolpa Uno (Kolpa) mine following Endeavour Silver’s acquisition of the mine operator. Versamet also upsized its secured revolving credit facility to US$60 million, lowering interest costs. The deal adds near-term copper exposure and can modestly bolster copper sentiment.

The piece focuses on MAC Copper’s March quarter performance, noting copper production progress (including 4kt in March), improving costs with higher volumes, and ongoing ventilation and Merrin development milestones. Analysts cite stronger metrics and assign Buy ratings, supporting constructive near-term sentiment for copper-linked supply outlooks. | Ivanhoe Mines says its Kamoa-Kakula complex produced a record 50,176 tonnes of copper in concentrate in April, with Phase 3 recovery and throughput exceeding design. The company also reports rising imported hydropower to support operations and smelter commissioning aimed at first anode production in July, supporting copper output growth. | NewRange Copper Nickel announced the NorthMet project was designated a “Transparency Project” by a federal council aimed at expanding domestic critical-mineral supply. The move signals prioritized permitting progress for a major undeveloped copper and nickel resource. The company reports most state permits secured and plans to update Section 404 filings. | NewRange Copper Nickel said the federal NEDC designated its NorthMet project as a “Transparency Project,” prioritizing permitting for critical minerals. The firm holds most required permits and plans to update its Clean Water Act Section 404 application. The designation is intended to speed development of domestic copper and nickel supply with environmental safeguards. | Michigan Representative Markkanen requests earmarked 2026 state budget funding to support the Copperwood Mine development in Gogebic County. The funds would upgrade roads, power, and telecommunications to improve site readiness, helping the project move toward production. The article notes prior funding was deferred and an air permit was approved with conditions.

Copper wiring thefts in Carroll County, Maryland have damaged light structures at athletic fields, requiring costly repairs and field downtime. Officials estimate replacement costs of about $18,000 in one case and consider measures like burying components and adding cameras. The theft reduces local copper availability but is unlikely to affect broader copper markets materially. | Lundin Mining, with BHP, announced what it calls South America’s largest copper discovery in 30 years at the undeveloped Filo del Sol project on the Chile-Argentina border. Estimated copper resources are about 13 million tons, with gold and silver. Development will likely take years, limiting immediate price effects. | Xanadu Mines let its exclusivity agreement with Zijin lapse and triggered a vote on a put option to sell its remaining 25% stake in Mongolia’s Kharmagtai copper-gold project. The move may draw third-party suitors and alter JV funding plans, but it does not change near-term output immediately. | Spain has seen a sharp rise in railway copper cable thefts, including on the Madrid–Seville high-speed line, disrupting sensors and forcing trains into safety mode. Theft is driven by scrap economics and reported flows to global buyers. Preventive measures may include replacing some copper with aluminium or fibre optics. | SMM reports blister copper and copper anode refining charges (RCs) in China fell sharply into May, with spot transactions largely absent due to unfavorable producer margins and tighter scrap flows from reduced US scrap imports. While supply-demand may tighten if copper prices don’t rebound, the immediate RC trend is bearish for copper sentiment. | Copper cable thefts disrupted Spain’s high-speed rail signalling, stranding thousands of passengers. Authorities said stolen copper cables were taken at multiple sites, and the transport minister expects repairs to restore schedules by late afternoon. The report notes copper prices are high, boosting scrap value incentives for theft. | Faraday Copper reported results from five drill holes at its Copper Creek Project in Arizona, including discovery of the blind Winchester breccia and multiple near-surface copper intercepts in the American Eagle area. The company also expects an updated MRE/PEA near end-Q3 2025 based on expanded drilling, supporting longer-term supply potential. | Brixton Metals announced 2025 exploration plans at its Thorn copper-gold project, including shallow drilling for gold mineralization with reported copper values and planned drilling of new copper porphyry targets Catalyst and Tempest. The news is exploration-focused with no production or supply disruption, so near-term copper impact is likely limited. | The article says Vedanta is trying to raise cash after President Hakainde Hichilema settled the Konkola dispute. With Konkola Copper Mines being a major Zambia copper operation, the settlement lowers operational risk and supports expectations for copper output and financing in the near term. | World Copper Ltd. said it has terminated a February 12 letter agreement to sell its interest in the Zonia copper project in Arizona. No funds were advanced and no break fee was paid. The release is a corporate transaction update without any stated near-term operational or production impact. | Capstone Copper amended and extended its revolving credit facility to May 2029, increasing commitments to $1 billion, and plans to repay Mantoverde project financing debt using proceeds from newly issued senior unsecured notes. The update improves balance-sheet flexibility but does not indicate changes to copper output, supply, or demand.

UNCTAD highlights long-term copper supply shortfalls and the need for major new mine investment, but near-term prices face tariff uncertainty. Trump’s investigation and expectations of at least a 10% import levy are cited, while recession fears from tariff-driven growth drag are described as a headwind for copper near term. | A US judge will rule by May 14 on Rio Tinto’s copper project, which Native Americans oppose. The decision could affect the project’s timeline and permitting status, influencing expectations for copper supply. Higher perceived supply risk or delays would likely weigh on copper prices in the near term.

Apache Stronghold is asking a federal judge to pause actions related to a copper mine until a high court makes a ruling. If the halt is granted, it could reduce near-term copper output and supply expectations, likely supporting copper prices over the following days to weeks. | Argentina’s Los Azules project in San Juan—aiming for ~180,000 tons of copper annually—could boost future supply, but it remains undeveloped pending large infrastructure and financing. The story highlights rising copper demand and stagnant production elsewhere, supporting expectations of a near-term supply shortage despite delays. | Viscaria announced an updated Mineral Resource and feasibility study for the Viscaria mine in Kiruna, Sweden. The resource increases to 108 Mt with a higher copper grade of 0.90%, containing about 967,000 tonnes of copper. The feasibility study supports reopening and ~26,000 tonnes copper annual output. | The piece says copper tried to rally but reversed, trading near $4.50 after US-China talks were scheduled to start in Switzerland. It argues the market is uncertain about near-term China demand benefits. It notes support around the $4.50 area/200-day EMA and upcoming Fed rates could sway prices. | Ivanhoe Electric’s subsidiary Cordoba Minerals signed an agreement to sell its remaining 50% stake in Colombia’s Alacrán copper project. JCHX’s consortium will pay $88 million on closing plus $12 million deferred and up to $28 million contingent based on copper price at commercial production, subject to regulatory approvals. | Ivanhoe Mines said crews have entered the Platreef Flatreef orebody underground for the first time, with the first blast on May 7, 2025. The polymetallic deposit includes high-grade nickel and copper, and ore is being prepared for Phase 1 concentrator feed in Q4 2025. | Ivanhoe Mines said crews have entered the high-grade Flatreef orebody for the first time and completed initial underground development, including the first blast of ore on May 7, 2025. The polymetallic deposit includes nickel and copper as payable by-products, with stored ore ahead of Phase 1 concentrator feed in Q4 2025. | Aurubis, Europe’s largest copper producer, plans to increase sales to the U.S. since copper is not included in current reciprocal tariffs. The company also expects U.S. recycling smelter ramp-ups. U.S. copper import investigations remain ongoing, but near-term market impact appears limited. | Marmota signed a binding agreement to sell its West Melton copper exploration licence to G4 Metals for A$1m in G4 shares, plus potential performance payments tied to future JORC copper resource discoveries. The deal depends on exploration and a planned IPO and requires ministerial approval. | Armenia’s copper concentrate output fell 23.7% year-on-year to 79,596.4 tons in Q1 2025, according to the national statistics committee. The decline signals reduced copper concentrate supply in the near term. Zinc and molybdenum trends are mixed, but only copper concentrate directly affects copper pricing expectations. | The U.S. and UK plan a trade deal that cuts tariffs on U.K. imports of steel and aluminium to zero and reduces auto tariffs to 10% for a quota. The agreement signals reduced trade friction, likely supporting aluminium demand and pricing expectations. Copper is not directly addressed. | Trump agreed to a planned trade deal with the U.K. that would cut tariffs on U.K. autos to 10% (from 27.5% on a vehicle quota) and eliminate U.S. import taxes on steel and aluminum (from 25% to zero). This likely supports demand and pricing for aluminium in the near term. | Green Bridge Metals reports a non-binding LOI to option the Serpentine copper-nickel sulphide project in Minnesota, with planned future option funding and continued drilling/permitting progress. The company highlights strong historic recoveries and infrastructure access. However, the LOI is not definitive and implies no immediate production impact for copper or nickel. | Green Bridge Metals entered a non-binding LOI to option the Serpentine copper-nickel sulfide project in Minnesota. The release links the plan to a U.S. executive order aimed at boosting American mineral production and cites permitting progress. It supports bullish sentiment for copper and nickel, though impact is likely limited near-term.

SMM reports Q1 2025 copper output from the top 15 miners at 2,967 kt, down 0.8% y/y and 12.8% m/m. Multiple companies cite operational disruptions (Chile power outages, smelter maintenance in Indonesia, lower ore grades/recovery), leading SMM to say concentrate supply is not optimistic. | Trump’s trade framework keeps a 10% baseline tariff on U.K. goods but cuts the U.K. auto tariff to 10% on a 100,000-vehicle quota and eliminates tariffs on steel and aluminum imports. This should improve near-term metal trade flows and demand expectations, with copper only indirectly affected via broader industrial activity. | Residents in Los Angeles’ Silver Lake report repeated vandalism of street lights as thieves rip out copper wiring, leaving blocks without lighting. The city cites repairs delayed by budget constraints and mentions a Heavy Metal Task Force, with prior arrests and 2,000 pounds of stolen copper wire seized. | Aptiv’s Plant 10 in Howland, Ohio, producing copper wiring, will close and outsource work, affecting 63 employees by July 14. The union says the decision was planned after outsourcing began in August 2022. The article also references Aptiv’s separation of its Electrical Distribution Systems business. | Trump announced a U.S.-U.K. trade deal that cuts U.K. tariffs on steel and aluminum to zero and reduces auto tariffs under a quota. The framework keeps a 10% baseline for British goods and hints at higher tariffs elsewhere. Overall, easing aluminium trade frictions may support nearby aluminium sentiment. | Ivanhoe Mines says Kamoa-Kakula in the DRC produced a combined April record of 50,176 tonnes of copper in concentrate, aided by Phase 3 exceeding its feed and recovery targets. Increased imported hydroelectric power via the Zambia-DRC interconnector supports higher milling rates and final commissioning of an on-site smelter. | SMM reports stable April copper foil operating rates (71.51%) with no major inventory disruptions. May is forecast for a slight decline. Tariff policy uncertainty is said to delay transmission of impacts, with some downstream frontloading exports supporting electronic circuit foil demand, while lithium battery foil orders weaken due to reduced US-bound ESS exports. | A U.S. federal judge temporarily blocked the Trump administration from transferring land to Rio Tinto and BHP for the Resolution Copper project in Arizona. The dispute centers on protecting Oak Flat, with the reserve holding more than 40 billion pounds of copper. The delay reduces near-term development certainty, potentially tightening future copper supply.

China’s copper concentrate imports hit record highs as smelters expand capacity and stockpile ahead of tariff uncertainty. Trade tensions divert unwrought copper toward the U.S., pushing COMEX inventories higher while SHFE stocks fall sharply. Backwardation widens and Yangshan premiums surge, signaling tight physical conditions and near-term bullish pressure for copper. | SMM reports copper cathode rod enterprises’ operating rate rose in April but is expected to fall in May to 71.01% due to post-Labour-Day demand weakness and a widening copper futures spread. Producers are reducing inventory to cut risk as copper remains volatile, raising concern operating rates may miss expectations. | UNCTAD warns that copper supply is falling behind rapidly growing demand from clean energy and digital infrastructure. Declining ore grades, long mine timelines and geopolitical tensions could lead to a looming shortfall. Meeting demand would require many new mines and investment, supporting expectations of tighter copper markets. | April copper billet operations were steady, but tariff disruptions and high copper raw material costs reduced downstream ordering and inventory days. SMM expects May operating rates for copper billet producers to fall to about 49.92% (down 5.05% MoM) amid tighter overseas raw supply and seasonal/weaker demand. | Rio Tinto and Sumitomo Metal Mining signed final JV agreements to develop the Winu copper-gold project in Western Australia. Rio continues operating while SMM will pay up to $430.4 million for a 30% stake. Development milestones and regulatory approvals are expected through 2025, implying limited near-term copper supply impact. | Sumitomo Metal Mining agreed to buy a 30% stake in Rio Tinto’s Winu copper-gold project in Western Australia, paying $195 million upfront plus deferred milestone-linked consideration. Rio is advancing pre-feasibility for a 10 mtpa mill and environmental permitting, but production timing remains unclear. | Codelco and Rio Tinto will create a Chile copper “mining district” around their Nuevo Cobre project in the Atacama region. They will form a joint committee and fund preliminary studies for 12 months, possibly extending, to expand exploration and development options, without any immediate production increase mentioned. | Faraday Copper reported Q1 2025 results, including ongoing drilling at its Copper Creek Project in Arizona, with multiple copper assay highlights and positive metallurgical test work. Phase III drilling was completed in mid-April and further assays are expected. The company remains exploration-stage, reliant on financing, with no immediate production impact.

India proposes retaliatory tariffs at the WTO after U.S. safeguard duties on steel and aluminium. India says the measures cover about $7.6B of imports and plans to suspend concessions/obligations on revised tariffs taking effect in 2025. The U.S. argues the duties are for national security and not WTO safeguards. | India informed the WTO it will impose retaliatory tariffs on US imports after the United States extended safeguard duties on steel, aluminium and related products. The action raises the likelihood of disrupted aluminium trade flows and near-term market repricing, while broader retaliation may weakly affect copper via macro sentiment. | SunDrive Solar says it has signed a China pact with Maxwell Technologies and Jiangsu Vistar Equipment to co-develop and distribute direct-copper plating tools for high-efficiency HJT solar cells. The tech uses copper for PV metallization instead of silver, but no near-term production volumes are provided, limiting immediate copper price impact. | UNCTAD’s Global Trade Update says copper is becoming a strategic bottleneck as demand rises sharply, while new mine development faces long lead times and high costs. It highlights trade/tariff barriers for refined products and urges permitting and recycling investment, supporting tighter supply expectations for copper. | India is preparing a legal response to a court challenge to its December quality-control curbs on copper cathode imports. Officials argue domestic supply and sufficient certified suppliers exist, while trade bodies claim a de facto monopoly and import-driven shortages. Enforcement and potential supply constraints could tighten near-term copper availability. | India filed a WTO notice saying it may impose retaliatory tariffs on selected U.S. goods to offset U.S. 25% steel and aluminum import duties. The proposal targets $1.91 billion in duties and is meant to counter the February tariffs, amid ongoing India-U.S. trade talks. | India has notified the WTO it may impose retaliatory duties against the US after the US revised and extended safeguard tariffs on steel and aluminium (including 25% and 10% ad valorem measures, effective March 12, 2025). India’s proposed suspension would mirror duty collected on US-origin imports, potentially disrupting aluminium trade flows. | A report states that a large copper wire manufacturer plans to come to Fairfield County, South Carolina. The development implies higher future production and copper consumption for wire applications. While the article doesn’t quantify capacity or timing, the announcement can still modestly support copper demand expectations. | Mazoon Mining secured OMR104 million in financing and signed construction and service contracts to start Oman’s largest integrated copper concentrate project in Yanqul. The project targets about 115,000 tonnes of copper concentrate annually, with equipment and EPCM appointments made. The execution milestone supports positive copper supply expectations. | Mega Metal, a Turkish-based copper wire producer, selected Fairfield County, South Carolina for its first U.S. facility. The $34 million, 91,000-square-foot plant will create 135 jobs and is expected to make about 55 million pounds of superfine electrolytic oxygen-free copper wire annually, starting in September 2025. | Green Bridge Metals announces the acquisition of the Copper–Nickel–PGE Serpentine project in Minnesota, USA. The deal is a mining-stage development move that may influence longer-term supply expectations for copper and nickel, but the article provides no near-term operational changes that would likely drive spot price shifts. | Green Bridge Metals says it has acquired the Copper-Nickel-PGE serpentine project in Minnesota. The deal links the company to copper and nickel resources, potentially shaping longer-term supply expectations, but there is no immediate production, permitting, or shipping change cited that would likely move near-term copper or nickel prices.

Trump’s administration fast-tracked the Resolution Copper project tied to the Oak Flat land swap, potentially enabling a major new copper mine that the company says could supply up to a quarter of U.S. demand. Apache Stronghold secured an injunction, so timing is uncertain, but near-term sentiment favors more copper supply. | Amarc Resources reported late-2024 drilling and re-assaying results from the Empress Deposit in its IKE copper-gold district in BC, including multiple long intersections graded using CuEQ with reported contained copper. The findings confirm high Cu-Au potential but do not indicate near-term production or supply changes. | Amman Mineral Nusa Tenggara has begun Phase 8 at the Batu Hijau copper-gold mine, extending operations through 2030, with stockpiles potentially processed until 2033. Phase 8 starts from lower-grade areas and ramps up toward richer sections, increasing copper production volumes gradually over the next few years. | Glenstar Minerals says it will begin a 10–14 day reverse-circulation drilling program at its Green Monster Ni-Cu-Co property in Nevada. The target is within a district known for copper and also nickel anomalies. Sampling has shown high Cu and Ni grades, but the release contains no new assay results. | Glenstar Minerals says drilling will start May 14 on its Green Monster Ni-Cu-Co property in Nevada, using a reverse-circulation rig over 10–14 days with five drill holes. Prior sampling reported elevated copper and nickel values, which may modestly support sentiment for copper and nickel. | Endolith, with BHP’s TAD and Unearthed, says it has achieved higher copper recovery from low-grade sulphide ores using microbes instead of conventional heap leaching. Column tests showed faster and more complete copper recovery across temperatures. The technology could improve economics of complex ores, supporting longer-term copper supply expectations. | Mega Metals will invest $34 million to build its first South Carolina plant in Fairfield County to produce superfine electrolytic oxygen-free copper wire. The 91,000-square-foot facility is expected to operate in September and produce 55 million pounds annually, expanding U.S. copper wire capacity and linked procurement.

Los Angeles authorities report a surge in theft of copper wire, including streetlight and telecom lines. Police say thieves strip and process copper reels and sell them to scrap yards, recovering thousands of pounds. Enforcement efforts target scrapyards’ intake of stolen metals and trace wires back to theft victims. | Faraday Copper reported results from seven Phase III drill holes at its Copper Creek Project in Arizona, including multiple near-surface oxide and enrichment-zone copper intercepts (e.g., 10.08 m at 3.62% Cu). Management said the findings expand oxide mineralization potential for earlier heap-leach cathode production. | North Dakota’s DEQ is updating residents on compliance with the EPA’s revised Lead and Copper Rule. Public water systems must inventory service lines and plan replacements of lead service lines. The state has hosted a webinar on the rule’s effects and provides guidance on inventory procedures and where to seek help. | Horizon Copper reported additional high-grade drilling results from the Hugo North Extension at Oyu Tolgoi, with multiple intercepts above ~1% and up to ~3.29% copper. It also notes Lift 1 underground ramp-up progress, reinforcing longer-life copper potential for the JV deposit and related equity exposure. | The article headline says scrap copper had been piling up in the US but can now finally move. Easing scrap logistics bottlenecks generally increases prompt scrap availability for processing/refining, which can add to near-term copper supply expectations and put downward pressure on copper prices.

The San Carlos Apache Tribe is seeking a court order to halt a land deal related to the Oak Flat copper mine. A legal block or delay to project permitting could reduce expected copper output and tighten supply expectations, potentially providing short-term support for copper prices as markets price in execution risk. | After Sino-US talks, tariffs were mutually suspended for 90 days, but SMM says the direct impact on copper rod exports to the US is minimal. Copper cathode rod trade relies more on Southeast Asia entrepot processing. Even with relief, new export orders have fallen ~30% since May as buyers wait for further tariff outcomes. | SMM launched the CIF Southeast Asia EQ copper cathode B/L Premium assessment, starting June 2, 2025, to improve transparency and price discovery for LME Grade A copper cathode shipments to Southeast Asian ports. The premium is quoted versus LME 3-month plus QP adjustments and will be published daily on SMM’s website. | Antofagasta’s upcoming mid-year concentrate negotiations approach amid weak buyer leverage. Spot treatment charges are falling as smelters “scramble” for limited copper concentrate, while global sulfide ore supply is expected to undershoot. Smelter capacity growth adds to concentrate demand, pressuring the market and supporting copper economics. | SMM will launch two new daily benchmark assessments—EXW Dar es Salaam and EXW Durban equalization copper cathode premiums—for LME Grade A copper cathode delivered at those ports. Premiums will be quoted versus the LME 3-month price, with publication each Chinese trading day. This formalizes trading-premium data, not a new physical flow. | Starr Peak Mining acquired the Copper Clive project in Quebec, securing 100% interest in 29 claims by paying C$40,000. The property shows a 2023 geophysics anomaly and is adjacent to known copper mineralisation. Planned airborne EM, prospecting and drilling aim to define the anomaly, supporting potential future copper exploration.

Traders are racing to import refined copper into the U.S. ahead of a potential Section 232 tariff. CME inventories have surged to an eight-year high, LME stocks have fallen, and the CME-LME cash premium has collapsed from about $1,600 to $600/ton. The resulting redistribution narrows the price gap amid uncertainty. | The piece expects SHFE copper spot premiums to move through three stages after rollover: a temporary spike due to warrant restrictions, a pullback as SHFE 2505 delivery warrants are released, then a rebound as downstream operations improve while social warehouse inflows remain limited and destocking resumes. | Mazoon Mining secured USD 270 million in financing and signed construction and service contracts to start developing Oman’s largest copper concentrate project, targeting ~115,000 tonnes annually. The financing and EPCM/equipment arrangements (e.g., Metso) support project execution, but no immediate production or supply shock is indicated. | An initial resource estimate for Argentina’s Filo del Sol finds up to about 13 million tonnes of copper, roughly five times more than previously believed, based on additional drilling. The results validate a BHEM-targeted zone and may improve long-run supply expectations, but no production timing is given. | Talon Metals reported record drill assays from drill hole 25TK0563 at the Tamarack nickel-copper project, including 34.9m grading 28.88% NiEq (and 57.76% CuEq). The company frames results as world-class and will guide further drilling, but there is no immediate production change. | President Trump raised Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum articles and derivative products from 25% to 50%, effective June 4, 2025 with strict customs declaration requirements. The change affects aluminum import competitiveness and can tighten near-term supply, supporting aluminum prices. The notice does not directly reference copper or nickel. | Metals Creek Resources reported additional diamond-drilling results from its Tillex Copper Project in Ontario, including 1.52% Cu over 28.8m (and 2.61% Cu over 13.27m).
