Domestic producers praised the move, while Canada and the EU condemned it and promised countermeasures. Economists warn tariff-driven price hikes and demand pressure could follow. | Trump doubled U.S. steel and aluminum import tariffs to 50% (UK at 25%), citing national security and China-linked dumping. With the U.S. relying heavily on imports for aluminum, higher landed costs are expected to strain consumers and support higher aluminum prices. Retaliation risk exists, but near-term tariff effects are bullish. | An executive order effective June 4, 2025 doubles Section 232 tariffs on imported aluminum and steel to 50% (except UK at 25%), applying to aluminum/steel content within products and even retroactively for certain foreign-trade-zone entries. Strict enforcement and tariff stacking/IEEPA rules add compliance costs. | President Trump signed an order doubling tariffs on all imported steel and aluminum, lifting rates from 25% to 50%. Automakers warn higher costs will disrupt vehicle production because cars use large amounts of aluminum and steel. The immediate tariff effect is most direct for aluminum input pricing and related metal markets. | Trump’s proclamation doubles U.S. Section 232 tariffs on most steel and aluminum imports, effective June 4, 2025, raising the rate to 50% (with specific carve-outs like the UK at 25% until July 9). Trump’s administration raised aluminum and steel import tariffs to 50%. A Mid-Michigan scrap firm expects tariffs to boost domestic production, tighten substitutes, and increase demand for recycled aluminum and steel scrap used in metalmaking. The CEO says recycled aluminum pricing is already strong and could rise if tariffs persist and are enforced. | Louisville’s Metro Council is advancing an ordinance to curb copper wire theft by requiring detailed seller documentation for smelted/burned/melted infrastructure wire, restricting purchases without proof of ownership, and adding local licensing and penalties. Impoundment and added enforcement aim to reduce profitable stolen copper sales, potentially easing near-term copper flows. | California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced efforts to crack down on copper wire thefts, including a law-enforcement bulletin on existing statutes and recyclers’ reporting duties. The state says thefts are rising and have caused outages and facility damage in places like Vallejo and Oakland, prompting calls for stricter enforcement. | U.S. President Trump doubled Section 232 tariffs on imported steel and aluminum articles from 25% to 50% effective June 4, reversing prior “tariff stacking” treatment for Canada/Mexico and adjusting reciprocal tariff calculations. UK gets a temporary exemption, while FTZ withdrawals for consumption will face the 50% duty rate. | California Attorney General Rob Bonta joins local efforts to curb rising copper wire theft in Kern County and statewide. AT&T reports thousands of theft and vandalism incidents affecting customers and emergency services, and says the theft rate is the highest in the U.S. Rewards and enforcement measures are being expanded to reduce losses. | The U.S. issued a presidential proclamation doubling Section 232 tariffs on imported steel and aluminum articles from 25% to 50%, effective June 4. It reverses prior tariff “stacking” for Canada/Mexico, limits exemptions, and clarifies tariff assessment for FTZ withdrawals. Importers face higher costs and supply-chain adjustments. | China’s Commerce Ministry criticizes US Section 232 tariffs raised on imported steel and aluminium (and derivatives) from 25% to 50%, effective Wednesday. China urges dialogue and market-based solutions, warning about broader trade instability. | Queensland’s treasurer has allocated an additional $1.4 billion to the CopperString 2032 transmission project, bringing committed funding to $2.4 billion, after earlier construction-scope changes. Work is expected to start before year-end, with officials aiming to keep costs below $13.9 billion—supporting prospective copper and aluminium usage for grid buildout. | SMM data show copper billet producer operating rates fell to 50.61% in May and are expected to drop further to 47.11% in June. Weak demand, slow finished-inventory drawdown, intensified competition, and price cuts with limited effect are cited, with high copper prices suppressing downstream procurement. | Armenia’s copper concentrate production fell 27.6% to 76,124.5 tons in January–April 2025 versus the same period in 2024, per the National Statistical Committee. This reduction in concentrate supply is likely to tighten copper availability and pressure prices lower in the near term. | Sinomine paused copper smelting at its Tsumeb plant in Namibia, blaming a shortage of copper concentrate stemming from rapid global smelter capacity expansion and “overcapacity.” The pause affects a key smelter site and implies weaker near-term concentrate processing, adding short-term downside risk for copper prices. | Kefi Gold & Copper reported a narrower 2024 pretax loss as it nears completion of its Tulu Kapi project in Ethiopia. The company still needs a USD320 million financing package to develop the mine. The report focuses on project progress and investment plans, with no clear immediate copper market supply figures or pricing targets. | Chinese smelters face very difficult concentrate negotiations with Antofagasta (offered price -$15 vs leaked $0), while multiple supply shocks hit copper mines. SMM updates sulfide concentrate balance accordingly. | SMM says copper foil enterprise operating rates rose in May and are forecast to increase further in June, driven by stable terminal orders—especially lithium battery copper foil from power/ESS sectors and mild electronic circuit demand. Inventories edged up slightly and processing fees stayed stable, supported by some stockpiling amid a higher copper price center. | Falling LME copper stocks in registered warehouses have tightened prompt supply, flipping spreads into backwardation: cash LME copper has traded at a $75/mt premium over three-month futures, after jumping to $93. Recent cancellations, mine disruptions, and diversion to the U.S. amid tariff concerns support the move. | President Trump signed a proclamation doubling Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum (and derivative products) from 25% to 50%, effective around midnight June 4, with no exemption for goods on water. Earlier steps revoked exclusions and country exemptions, removing relief. Manufacturing groups urged for tariff-free “zero-for-zero” outcomes. | Koryx Copper confirmed filing its Annual Information Form (AIF) on SEDAR+. The release mainly restates background on its Haib copper project in Namibia, including resource estimates and technical work underway for future low-cost open-pit development. No operational or supply-impact news is announced.

China’s rapid smelting capacity expansion has pushed copper treatment charges deeply negative, with spot minus-$60/t and even proposed negative terms for contracted supplies. Local leaders want faster rollout, but overall the spending supports anticipated demand for electrical conductor metals. | The article argues that the Supreme Court’s decision allows the Oak Flat land transfer to Resolution Copper, a mining project described as tied to Chinese Communist Party-linked ownership through Rio Tinto and Chinalco. It contends the deal will enable extraction of copper that could ultimately be controlled by Beijing. | The piece presents a bullish thesis for Northern Dynasty Minerals’ Pebble project, citing Trump-era executive orders prioritizing domestic critical minerals, including copper, and faster permitting. It also claims a growing copper supply deficit. Overall, it frames improved prospects for copper supply as supportive for prices. | The Trump administration doubled tariffs on imported steel and aluminum from 25% to 50%, raising the cost of foreign-sourced inputs for the Damon Company. The firm reports increased spending on aluminum and steel and delays/derails export deals, contributing to uncertainty and potential pricing pressure for metal-intensive manufacturers. | Terra Metals and Lunda Resources report a metallurgical breakthrough at Zambia’s Kalaba Copper Project, with independent testing confirming 96.5% cumulative copper recovery and high concentrate grades. The result is enabled by new flotation chemistry addressing high talc content, improving leaching economics and supporting future copper output plans. | A U.S. judge denied a halt to the federal land exchange for the Oak Flat copper project but barred the Forest Service from proceeding for 60 days pending an environmental review. Native American and environmental groups seek to stop the transfer, while Resolution Cooper says the project complies and has local support.

Bakersfield and California officials say copper wire theft is still causing damage and repair costs. The city has replaced numerous utility fixtures and is shifting some wiring to aluminium to deter theft. State and law-enforcement efforts highlight ongoing scrap-driven theft, supporting sustained copper scrap demand. | RNZ reports concerns in remote New Zealand communities if Chorus phases out its copper broadband network and replaces it with fibre. The Commerce Commission has closed submissions on draft recommendations to deregulate the rural copper network. The prospect of copper substitution points to weaker copper demand expectations near term. | BLM is seeking public comment on a proposal by Antler Operations Inc. to redevelop the historic Antler copper deposit near Yucca. The plan would build a new underground mine and related support facilities, including a powerline, with most work on private land and a small portion on public land. | Copper thefts are rising in West Texas amid a heatwave, increasing losses of physical copper and tightening local availability of copper scrap/materials. That potential near-term supply disruption could lift expectations for copper prices over coming days as buyers compete for limited inventory. | Pathfinder Tonopah said the U.S. EXIM Bank sent a non-binding indicative Letter of Interest for $896 million to finance development of its Nevada copper (and molybdenum) mine. The funding proposal would accelerate construction of processing facilities, improving the investment and supply outlook for copper in the U.S. timeline. | Cascadia Minerals agreed to acquire Granite Creek Copper to expand its copper-gold exploration portfolio, combining the Carmacks Project in Yukon with additional projects. The deal includes a premium share exchange, a C$2.5m cash position, and a private placement/bridge loan to fund near-term drilling, subject to approvals. | International Wire Group agreed to purchase Hussey Copper, a U.S. copper products supplier of busbars and electrical components. The deal aims to expand presence in electrical infrastructure, data centers, power generation, EVs and renewables, with closing expected in Q3 subject to approvals. Impact on near-term copper prices appears limited. | Indonesia’s ministry is working to attract more downstream investment in the high-value copper sector. The country already produces copper cathodes and plans to expand into derivative products such as copper foil and slabs. Incentives include tax holidays and allowances, aiming to move beyond limited tier-one processing. | Star Copper Corp. begins an upsized 2025 drill program at its Star Project in British Columbia’s Golden Triangle, targeting expansion of a near-surface oxide zone and deeper porphyry copper-gold mineralization. The program covers ~4,000 meters across six holes to test modeled magnetic/chargeability anomalies and fault-controlled structures. | Ausenco was selected to run a preliminary economic assessment for U.S. GoldMining’s Whistler gold-copper project in Alaska, including updated resources and metallurgical testing. The study targets an open-pit mine and processing facility, but no near-term production or supply disruption is indicated, limiting immediate copper price impact. | Copper prices fell as investors avoided the market amid uncertainty over U.S.-China trade talks and the trade war’s impact on demand. Comex futures dropped too, while LME stocks continued to fall, and aluminium and nickel also eased. | CNBC reports that copper is being pulled by tariff uncertainty: U.S. companies may “front-load” purchases ahead of potential new levies, while LME inventories deplete quickly as copper is drawn to the U.S., lifting prices. Offsetting factors include weakening China signals, continued exports, and possible slower solar/U.S. demand. | Glencore’s Mount Isa copper smelter faces potential earlier closure if government support isn’t secured, with experts warning Australian copper processing could shift offshore. Queensland officials cite sovereign risk and ongoing efforts to fund continued operations. Replacement capacity would be costly and slow, raising concerns about near-term refining tightness.

Copper prices are edging toward an annual high, boosted by revived US-China trade dialogue and expectations for improved growth, while LME stocks are falling. The piece also links the move to tariff-related uncertainty following a trade truce, implying near-term demand/supply tightness supportive of copper. | Pathfinder Tonopah said the US EXIM Bank issued a non-binding $896m letter of interest to help finance development of its Tonopah, Nevada copper mine. The support is aimed at strengthening domestic critical-minerals supply chains. The plan includes modern processing to produce copper cathode and molybdenum concentrate, but LOI is not final. | Trump’s June 4, 2025 proclamation doubles Section 232 tariffs on steel and aluminum (and covered derivatives) from 25% to 50%, tightening US import economics and potentially boosting near-term aluminium prices. It also clarifies tariff treatment rules and stacking priority versus IEEPA tariffs, with refunds for some retroactive overpayments. | The article argues the EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism will start generating liabilities within months and impose large compliance costs on importers of carbon-intensive goods, with aluminium facing meaningful added costs. It emphasizes certificate-price and policy-design uncertainty, which is already dampening forward contracting and could pressure prices via cost pass-through and demand shifts. | Zeus North America Mining plans to acquire a 90% stake in Nevada’s Delker and Bulls Eye copper-gold properties from Nedeel, with milestone-based cash/share payments and JV arrangements. The move targets future resource definition and drilling, with no immediate production impact cited, limiting short-term copper price effects. | Niagara Regional Police said thieves broke into electricity distributor yards in St. Catharines and Thorold to steal copper sold to scrap. The report is copper-specific but the volume appears limited and local. | The piece says copper prices have been stable in early June after prior tariff-driven volatility. A US copper trade group filed with the Bureau of Industry and Security asking for tariffs on imported semi-fabricated copper and copper alloys, while exempting certain feedstocks like copper cathodes and scrap. Supporters cite environmental safeguards and domestic critical-minerals supply; critics warn of acid drainage and plan legal challenges.

RBC highlights copper’s strong YTD performance near $4.37/lb, attributing price support to US-bound inflows ahead of a potential copper tariff and tightening conditions as demand and supply weaken. Supply disruption from Ivanhoe’s Kamoa-Kakula outage and reduced Chile 2025 growth estimates raise the odds of a deficit in H2 2025. | IFC and the World Bank approved a concessional $700 million loan for Pakistan’s Reko Diq copper-gold project in Balochistan. If the review clears the way for development, it would likely lift expected copper supply over the coming weeks, putting downward pressure on copper prices. | Ivanhoe Mines said the Kamoa-Kakula copper smelter in DR Congo will start operations in September 2025, with first anode production in October, at 50% capacity initially. The project adds 500,000 t/year processing capacity, backed by additional power, despite a lower 2025 mine output forecast after a seismic event. | IFC will provide a $400 million subordinated loan, adding to prior commitments, to help finance Pakistan’s Reko Diq copper-gold mine. Pumping infrastructure damage required emergency dewatering, with production guidance for 2025 revised significantly downward, signaling reduced copper supply.

Perth metro areas face nighttime blackouts as copper thieves strip wiring from the power grid, leaving about 65 km of streetlights and 14 km of pathway lights non-operational. Authorities say theft has risen since 2018, costing millions annually, and scrap-licensing laws have fueled an underground resale market for copper. | Police report that thieves stole over 750 meters of copper cable from a Hawke’s Bay railway bridge and removed four crossing bells. The event signals copper scrap theft but is unlikely to affect copper prices materially soon. | Two Minot men were sentenced for participating in a copper-wire theft scheme at Falkirk Mining Company near Underwood. The theft involved multiple incidents totaling about 980 pounds of copper tubing/wire, with proceeds from selling scrap in Minot and reported losses exceeding $500,000 to replace and repair materials. | SMM reports copper wire and cable enterprise operating rates fell to 70.18% (down 3.08 pp MoM, 12.66 pp YoY) and finished-product inventories rose after weak end-use demand followed April’s price volatility. Copper prices stay above 80,000 yuan/mt, but operators reduce rates to curb inventory risk. | Faraday Copper said the U.S. Bureau of Land Management approved its Exploration Plan of Operations for the Copper Creek Project in Arizona, enabling up to 67 drill pads and a Phase IV program likely starting in Q3 2025. The company plans updated resource and economic studies later in 2025. | Intrepid Metals acquired additional state leases and a patented mining claim to expand its Corral Copper Property near Rio Tinto’s adjacent claims in Arizona. The release is an exploration and development positioning update with no production or supply impact stated. | Gladiator Metals reports gravity survey results at the Little Chief Trend within its Whitehorse Copper Project, identifying multiple undrilled copper-gold skarn anomalies and planning two diamond rigs to drill in the coming weeks. The news is exploration-focused with no production or reserve changes. | India has sent geologists to Zambia to explore potential copper and cobalt deposits under a large government allocation. The initiative spans three years and aims to inform potential future mining leases. Because exploration is early and not affecting near-term supply, any impact on copper prices is likely limited. | Amarc Resources says it has started a $10 million 2025 drilling program at its DUKE copper-gold district in central British Columbia, funded via Boliden earning-in. The work targets multiple porphyry Cu-Au targets (JO, C4, Svea), expanding on prior scout drilling that returned copper and gold mineralized intercepts. | India is launching copper exploration in Zambia by acquiring greenfield land and sending geologists to assess copper deposits. It may provide mild near-term support to copper sentiment, though production impact is not immediate. | Informal miners in Peru have blockaded parts of a key copper transport corridor in Chumbivilcas, disrupting trucking used by major producers including MMG’s Las Bambas, Glencore’s Antapaccay and Hudbay’s Constancia. Companies reportedly began to be affected after days of protest, adding near-term supply risk and weighing on copper prices.

The article argues copper will stay strong through 2025, citing tariffs that shift stocks into the US and tighten ex-US availability, lifting LME premiums and forecasts (e.g., Goldman’s higher December 2025 outlook). It also describes active M&A/offtake bidding for ASX copper projects, reinforcing bullish sentiment. | Austral Resources has agreed to acquire Queensland’s Rocklands copper mine near Cloncurry, which has been in care and maintenance since November and has been reopened multiple times since 2016. Residents are cautious due to prior unpaid wages and closures, but Austral expects upcoming production to support the town. | Erdene signed an option agreement to acquire up to 80% of the Tereg Uul copper-gold porphyry prospect near Mongolia’s Oyu Tolgoi. The deal includes staged exploration spending and payments, with exploration expected to start in late Q3. No production is mentioned, suggesting only a modest copper-supportive sentiment. | Ero Copper said its Tucumã Operation in Pará, Brazil reached commercial production effective July 1, after commissioning and plant modifications improved throughput to above 75% of design capacity in June. The mine produced about 6,400 tonnes of copper in Q2, including ~2,000 tonnes in mid-June, and expects throughput growth into year-end. | Erdene Resource Development agreed to an option to acquire up to 80% of Tereg Uul, a copper-gold porphyry prospect near Mongolia’s Oyu Tolgoi corridor. The deal requires milestone payments and about USD 3 million of exploration spending within 12 months, with potential later stake purchases. Near-term copper supply impact is minimal. | Ero Copper Corp said its Tucum operation in Brazil reached commercial production effective July 1, 2025. Commissioning of a third filter press and process plant modifications helped sustain throughput above 75% of design capacity in June. Q2 output totaled about 6,400 tonnes, including 2,000 tonnes in late June. | Ero Copper said its Tucumã operation in Pará, Brazil achieved commercial production effective July 1, 2025. After completing commissioning of a third filter press and plant modifications, throughput exceeded 75% of design capacity in June. The firm produced about 6,400 tonnes of copper in Q2 and expects throughput and sequential copper growth into year-end.

India unveiled steps to boost copper output, including encouraging foreign firms to build smelters and refineries in exchange for overseas mining investments. With domestic reserves limited, India expects to import most concentrates by 2047 and plans trade links and subsidies to expand 4–5 million tonnes of smelting/refining capacity, supporting copper supply expectations. | India unveiled steps to increase copper output, including encouraging foreign firms to build smelters and refineries in exchange for Indian state-owned companies investing overseas in mining. The document highlights reliance on imported concentrates and rising demand, supporting expectations of tighter copper supply and potential near-term bullish pressure. | Aldebaran reports infill diamond drilling results (11 holes) at the Altar copper-gold project in Argentina, focused on zones intended to upgrade inferred resources to measured/indicated. Drill intercepts include multiple long copper-bearing sections reported as CuEq, supporting continuity and potentially strengthening the project’s development outlook. | Mirasol reports a second drill hole at its Sobek (Vicuña, Chile) Potro SE target, extending a hydrothermal breccia system with gold, high silver, and zinc/lead plus copper indications. The company plans follow-up drilling to test the fault corridor southward, but no near-term production impact is given. | Bell reports copper theft incidents across Eastern Canada have risen 23% year-over-year, with over 500 cases reported in the first half of 2025 and concentration in Ontario, New Brunswick and Quebec. The company says theft damages telecom infrastructure and urges stronger penalties, implying greater replacement needs for copper wiring. | India’s coal and mines minister released Copper and Aluminium Vision Documents to support the energy transition. The copper plan targets added smelting/refining capacity, scaling secondary refining and recycling. The aluminium roadmap aims to expand bauxite and production capacity and increase recycling and low-carbon tech. This may affect sentiment, but details are long-term. | India’s coal and mines minister released “Copper Vision” and “Aluminium Vision” documents to support the energy transition. Plans include adding copper smelting/refining capacity and scaling secondary refining and recycling, and expanding aluminium production and bauxite capacity. These are long-term roadmaps focused on raw-material security. | India’s coal and mines minister released Copper and Aluminium Vision Documents to support the clean-energy transition. The copper plan targets added smelting/refining capacity by 2030 and more secondary refining and recycling, while aluminium aims to expand production and bauxite capacity and raise recycling. Impact is mainly long-term. | A Brantford man was sentenced after stealing copper from air-conditioning units, causing about $45,000 damage to one business and $2,500 to another while seeking copper to fund drug addiction. The piece highlights rising copper-theft impacts on firms’ insurance and finances, but remains localized with no clear market-wide copper supply shock.

NexMetals Mining said drilling at the Selebi North deposit in Botswana hit “rich ore,” including multiple intervals with copper grades up to 2.87% and nickel grades up to 1.85%, while extending the orebody down-dip by 315 meters beyond the modeled boundary. The update is generally supportive for copper and nickel sentiment. | NexMetals reported rich results from initial drill holes at its Selebi North copper-nickel deposit in Botswana, including 13.5 meters with 1.13% copper and 1.25% nickel and additional intercepts extending ore down-dip and beyond the modeled resource boundary. Improved resource potential supports bullish sentiment for copper and nickel. | Thieves have stolen millions of dollars of copper cabling from streetlights and road infrastructure in WA, leaving areas without lighting for months. Main Roads says about 800,000 metres of cable were stolen over five to six years and replacement costs are about $2–$3 million annually, prompting investigations and prosecutions. | Indonesia’s minister asked mining authorities to consider easing the copper concentrate export ban for Amman Mineral International until its smelter reaches full capacity. Amman holds about 200,000 mt of concentrate inventory and production exports are restricted since mid-2023. The smelter is running but not yet at full output, prompting policy flexibility. | The U.S. Forest Service posted the final environmental impact statement and draft record of decision for the Resolution Copper Mining Project. The step supports advancing a proposed U.S. copper mine, though federal land transfer is not expected until Aug. 19, 2025, limiting immediate supply timing effects. | SMM data show copper billet producers’ operating rate fell to 46.15% in June and is expected to drop to 43.82% in July. Demand is weak in the traditional off-season as high copper prices suppress downstream buying, pushing brass-billet buyers toward substitutes like stainless steel. | Bell Canada warns copper theft is surging in eastern Canada, with incidents disrupting phone/internet and 911 access. Copper theft is up 23% year-on-year and Bell reports 2,270+ incidents since 2022, prompting accelerated shift to fiber and calls for tougher penalties. The story centers on copper theft impacts. | The article argues copper faces a growing supply-demand gap driven by EVs, grids and renewables, while US tariff threats and potential import restrictions add uncertainty and could disrupt supply chains. It also notes smelter intake constraints and concentrate shortages, tightening the market and pointing to price support despite longer-term technological solutions. | India announced incentives to attract foreign firms to build copper smelters and refineries, aiming to cut refined copper import dependence. The plan includes subsidies and customs duty exemptions, concentrate procurement strategy via trade talks, and notes tighter concentrate supplies. It also outlines aluminium demand and production strategies, but mainly longer-term targets. | Bell warns copper theft is rising in Ontario, New Brunswick, and Quebec, with 500 cases in 2025 and a reported 23% year-on-year increase. The telco says thefts target copper telecom power wiring for resale, prompting calls for tougher penalties and added surveillance.

Gunnison Copper announced a non-brokered private placement raising up to C$5.0 million to fund follow-on work at its Gunnison Copper Project, including long-lead drilling and metallurgical testing for a pre-feasibility study, plus 12 months of U.S. general and administrative costs. Closing is expected mid-July 2025. | Gunnison Copper Corp. announced a C$5 million listed issuer financing exemption private placement to fund additional and follow-on work at the Gunnison Copper Project, including long-lead drilling and metallurgical testing for a pre-feasibility study, plus US head office G&A. Closing is expected mid-July 2025. | SMM reports June operating rates for copper plate/sheet and strip producers fell to 67.44% as off-season demand stayed sluggish. Orders dropped MoM and firms cut production, while finished inventories rose. With wait-and-see sentiment amid high copper prices, SMM expects further July rate decline to 63.44%. | PwC said 32% of global semiconductor production could face copper supply disruptions by 2035 as drought risk rises in key chip-supplying countries. It highlights water stress already affecting Chile and Peru copper output, estimating 25% of Chile’s production at risk today, rising sharply over coming decades. | SMM says China’s copper foil enterprises’ operating rate rose in June and is expected to climb in July, driven largely by lithium battery copper foil demand. Payment-term regulation improved cash flow, and finished/raw inventory ratios fell. The company also highlighted ongoing expansion and power project progress. | The US Commerce Department expanded Section 232 to add 428 steel/aluminium derivative HTS codes, effective Aug. 18, covering downstream products like fasteners, wire and tubular components. The duties increase costs for imported aluminium-containing derivatives with limited mitigation via FTZs, likely weighing on near-term aluminium and some copper-linked demand. | Kinetic, a fiber internet provider, is offering a $10,000 reward for information leading to arrests over theft or vandalism of its copper and fiber infrastructure in North Carolina. The company cites multiple cable-cut incidents since Aug. 10 disrupting 911 access and services, but provides no direct data on copper market volumes. | Falcon Copper announced its Blue Copper and Schell Creek projects were added to the Federal FAST-41 Transparency Dashboard, aimed at accelerating federal permitting for critical minerals development. The projects target copper among other minerals, but the release provides no near-term production changes, making likely copper price impact limited. | Antofagasta reported its highest profit margins since 2021, citing an 11% rise in copper output and lower operating costs alongside stronger copper pricing. Copper sales increased 17% to reflect both higher volumes and market prices. The company also declared a larger interim dividend, highlighting copper’s ongoing price support.

Cyprium Metals is preparing a $60m underwritten equity placement to restart its Western Australia copper mine and bolster its balance sheet. The term sheet names Tribeca as lead manager and other banks as advisers/co-managers. The fundraising is intended to support renewed copper production prospects. | New Mexico denied a water-rights transfer required to reopen the Copper Flat Mine near Hillsboro, closed since 1982. The decision cites risks to future water supply and public welfare, including obligations with Texas. The denial likely prevents a restart, reducing near-term copper supply expectations. | Southern Hemisphere and FMR Resources have finalised a 4,000-metre phase-one drilling program at Chile’s Llahuin project to test undrilled copper-gold porphyry targets. The campaign starts late September with directional drilling and lab results expected weeks after each hole, potentially clarifying the system’s size and economic potential. | The US Interior Department proposes adding six minerals, including copper, to the national critical minerals list. The move is aimed at incentivizing domestic production and processing of strategic materials. The list will be formally posted in the Federal Register on Aug. 26, potentially boosting near-term copper sentiment. | A policy notice standardizing investment incentives and implementing reverse invoicing changes the VAT/refund economics for scrap-based copper smelting and refining. Secondary copper rod and scrap anode plate producers in some provinces halt or cut output, reducing August supply. SMM expects higher copper cathode consumption in September, supporting copper demand sentiment. | Kincora Copper reported positive early drilling at its Nevertire and Nevertire South projects in NSW, including encouraging anomalous copper mineralisation and favorable alteration/veining consistent with porphyry Cu-Au settings. Assays are pending, and follow-up geophysics and drilling are planned with partner AngloGold Ashanti. | First Quantum Minerals will keep its full stakes in Zambia’s Sentinel and 80%-owned Kansanshi copper mines after dropping plans to sell minority interests. It raised $1 billion via a Royal Gold streaming deal on Kansanshi gold by-product output. Cobre mine exports resumed in Panama, but production remains halted. | Tanzania’s minister laid the foundation for a USD 15m nickel and copper refining and processing plant in Dodoma, built by Zhongzhou Mining. The plant will need over 280,000 tonnes of raw material annually and is expected to start operations in Oct-Nov, boosting local processing demand for nickel and copper feedstock. | Tanzania’s Minerals Minister laid the foundation for Zhongzhou Mining’s first nickel and copper refining and processing plant in Dodoma, valued at over USD 15 million. The plant is set to start operations between October and November, requiring over 280,000 tonnes of raw material annually and creating jobs, with demand expected to support local mining supply. | Solis Minerals reported its first drill hole at the Ilo Este project intersected long porphyry-style mineralisation with copper (and molybdenum) observed visually. Four mineralised zones totalling ~350 metres are described, but laboratory assay results are expected in September 2025. No production or shipment impacts are mentioned. | Trump issued a Section 232 proclamation targeting copper imports with a 50% tariff on the copper content of semi-finished and derivative copper products, and ordered DPA-linked domestic sales requirements to preserve feedstock for U.S. producers. DHS added copper to UFLPA enforcement priorities, raising detention risk for Xinjiang-linked copper. | Camino Minerals reports trench/channel sampling at its Los Chapitos project in Peru uncovered a high-grade Mirador copper zone with strong copper-silver results (including intervals above 3% Cu) and bornite showings. The company plans further sampling and drilling in 2025, boosting copper exploration sentiment despite early-stage status.

The piece argues that adding silver to the U.S. critical minerals list is bullish because it can boost official demand via strategic stockpiling, subsidies, and faster purchasing ahead of policy implementation. It also notes copper was recommended for inclusion, implying a potentially supportive demand narrative for copper despite limited direct copper-specific details. | Hayasa Metals announced an amended option/joint venture agreement for its Vardenis copper-gold project in Armenia, granting Teck a sole option up to 80% with funding obligations and specified exploration spending and drilling milestones. Teck would fund costs during the option period, supporting development expectations for copper. | Aterian PLC said two of three new Botswana prospecting licences show strong prospectivity for copper (and silver), citing historical world-class deposits in the D’Kar and Ngwaka formations. The company secured licences in August and is beginning further exploration planning. The update is exploration-stage and likely limited for near-term copper prices. | The article discusses global copper reserve depletion estimates and warns that copper shortages could trigger supply shocks, disruption to construction and technology, and “drastic” copper price spikes driven by supply-demand imbalance. It also suggests recycling expansion and wider aluminium use as partial substitutes during a copper-constrained period. | Lincoln police are investigating the theft of roughly $50,000 in copper wire from a construction site tied to Cargill. Authorities are still seeking information and suspects. The report is local and does not indicate any disruption to regional supply chains or trading markets for copper. | Police are investigating an ongoing theft of copper wire from a construction site in north Lincoln, Nebraska. Officers say someone has been entering silos and stealing copper wire, with estimated losses of about $50,000. Investigators found fence and door damage; the case remains under investigation.

Copper Fox reported completion of a preliminary geometallurgical model for its Van Dyke in-situ copper recovery project in Arizona, identifying mineralogical domains and metallurgical data gaps and recommending further leach testwork to support prefeasibility. The release does not indicate imminent production or supply changes, so near-term price impact is limited. | Troilus Gold signed a memorandum with Aurubis for long-term offtake of copper-gold concentrate from the Troilus Project in Québec. The MoA formalizes earlier indicative terms, covering a significant portion of planned concentrate output as the project advances toward financing and construction. | Koryx Copper reported assay results from seven drill holes at its Haib copper project in southern Namibia, extending mineralization depth and lateral continuity. The company is re-logging core for geo-metallurgical modeling and plans to publish a September 2025 PEA based on an older resource, excluding recent drilling. | Mercuria partnered with Zambia’s IDC to form a 50:50 joint venture, Industrial Resources, focused on copper concentrate trading. With concentrate volumes secured and the first shipment underway, the state will gain direct copper marketing participation. The firm highlights rising smelter demand and record-low TCs/RCs, supporting copper economics. | Channel 4 reports on a copper mine disaster in Zambia where a toxic chemical spill entered a river used by millions. The story cites an independent report alleging the government and industry suppressed the accident’s true scale at a Chinese-owned mine, raising concerns over environmental and production impacts.

The piece explains that rising US tariffs on imported steel and aluminum (25% then 50%, plus more tariffed product categories) lift construction input costs. Higher project costs can increase rents and delay investment, supporting near-term aluminum price sentiment. It also notes downstream effects on other metal components used in buildings. | Iowa DNR is investigating a copper release after a broken pipe at the Zinpro facility in Garner, Iowa. About 700 gallons of copper-containing liquid spilled into a storm drain and drainage ditch. Cleanup and damming are in place, and residents are warned to avoid contact with the water due to confirmed dead fish.

Capstone Copper says its Mantoverde Chile operation faces a four-week production interruption after failures of both ball mill electrical drive motors, with repairs expected to take about one month. The mine will run at roughly half capacity by bypassing the ball mill, with 3,000–4,000 tonnes of copper-in-concentrate impacted.

Chile’s central bank reported July mining shrank 3.3% YoY, with declines in copper production weighing on the world’s largest copper supply source. The market also notes weak downstream demand from high prices and ample supply, while inventories are only partly supportive. Copper futures/spot edged lower overnight. | Middle Island Resources jumped after announcing its acquisition of Konstantin Resources, which holds Serbian licences in the Western Tethyan copper-gold belt (including multiple copper prospects). The deal includes a $3.4m placement and plans for near-term exploration. No production or supply disruptions are described, so near-term copper price impact appears limited. | Orogen Royalties announced it created a 1% NSR royalty (with option for an additional 0.25%) on the Camelot copper-gold porphyry project in British Columbia, acquired by Prospect Ridge. The company cites historical copper/gold assays and plans drilling in 2025, but no near-term production changes are disclosed. | Sinomine agreed to acquire a 70% stake in Cobre Limited’s Okavango copper project in Botswana, funding exploration via an initial A$3m placement and an additional A$7m payment. The deal, pending approvals, would give Sinomine majority control of a copper-belt asset and expand its African copper footprint. | Capstone Copper said its Mantoverde sulphide copper mine in Chile faces a temporary production disruption after two ball mill electrical drive motors failed. With no additional spare on site, the operation will run at about 50% capacity while repairs take roughly four weeks, reducing output by an estimated 3,000–4,000 tonnes. | Comex copper futures settled 1.13% higher at $4.5695, indicating upward near-term price pressure for copper. The move reflects improved sentiment or support in the futures market versus the prior session, which can influence expectations for physical demand and positioning over the following days.

Gunnison Copper announced first copper cathode production from its Johnson Camp Mine, following successful SX/EW start-up in late August 2025. The company says the plant is fully operational, with ramp-up toward 25 million lbs of annual cathode capacity. It cites Nuton’s leaching technology support and U.S. incentives. | Gunnison Copper Corp. says first copper has been produced at its Johnson Camp project, positioning Gunnison as a new US producer. The company frames the outlook as extremely bullish on copper and notes incentives aimed at boosting domestic mining supply, which can improve short-term expectations for copper availability. | NexMetals reports improved metallurgical test results from its past-producing Selebi Cu-Ni-Co mines in Botswana, showing it can produce separate saleable copper and nickel concentrates and potentially avoid a full smelter/hydromet plant. The company sees lower energy needs and faster restart optionality, but no near-term output change is stated. | Prime Minister Mark Carney said he’s in talks with President Trump for bilateral trade relief covering sectors facing U.S. tariffs, including aluminum. Canada recently cut retaliatory tariffs and U.S. officials discussed Canadian aluminum tariff levels, despite a reported 50% tariff. Easing barriers could lift near-term aluminum price expectations. | Canada’s PM Mark Carney said talks with the U.S. aim to obtain relief from tariffs on steel and aluminum. Despite Canada cutting some retaliatory duties, the U.S. plans to keep a 50% tariff on Canadian steel and aluminum, sustaining cost pressure on imports and likely weighing on aluminum prices in the near term. | Critical Mineral Resources PLC has begun a 6,000-meter diamond drilling program at its Agadir Melloul copper-silver project in Morocco to test for 150,000–200,000 tons of contained copper. The work aims to define resources for potential future mining, but no production increase is reported. | Golden Sky Minerals and Boliden signed a definitive earn-in agreement to jointly develop the Rayfield-Gjoll copper-gold project in British Columbia, with Boliden funding up to C$20 million for an 80% interest over six years. The release highlights copper-gold porphyry targets and geology, but no near-term production impacts. | Hydro says copper-to-aluminium substitution is accelerating in the US HVAC and refrigeration market despite US tariffs. Customers still pay higher aluminium tariff costs because aluminium remains much cheaper than copper, with the LME copper/aluminium ratio supporting substitution. The piece also notes copper supply constraints increasing the incentive to switch. | Hydro says the copper-to-aluminium substitution trend in industrial applications has not reversed despite US tariffs, with faster switching in the US HVAC market where aluminium remains cheaper despite a 50% tariff. The piece cites a high copper/aluminium price ratio and ongoing copper supply constraints, implying weaker copper demand and stronger aluminium uptake. | US Copper Corp. hired metallurgical consultants to review prior testwork and design additional metallurgy needed for a planned PFS at its Moonlight-Superior copper project in California. The update includes oxide/sulfide testwork planning, potential additional drilling, and references to prior PEA results, with no near-term production impact described.

The White House signed a proclamation imposing 50% tariffs on imported semi-finished copper products and copper-intensive derivatives from August 1, excluding copper inputs and scrap. The article says spreads narrowed and copper rallied further with expectations of Fed rate cuts, tighter smelter feedstock, and upcoming peak-season demand. | Ivanhoe Mines and Pallas Resources report outcropping copper mineralization at the Merke licence in Kazakhstan’s Chu-Sarysu Basin, including visible malachite, azurite and chalcocite and samples up to 5% copper. Follow-up mapping and a planned 15,000-metre drilling campaign have begun, but the discovery is not yet economic. | Electric Royalties reports higher royalty revenues tied to the Punitaqui Copper Mine in Chile, citing concentrate shipments (including copper content calculations) and stating the operator is executing plans to increase production. It also notes environmental approval to extend operations at Los Mantos, implying steadier near-term copper supply. | Koryx Copper reported updated PEA results for its 100%-owned Haib copper project in southern Namibia, projecting ~92 ktpa payable copper and detailing C1/AISC costs, capital needs, and NPV/IRR at a long-term copper price. The update supports project potential but does not imply immediate production impact. | Core Silver reported 2025 surface rock assay results from its Laverdiere copper project, including copper grades of 0.62–0.74% from Valley Zone samples and additional copper-bearing veining at Copper Creek. The company also outlines continued diamond drilling plans targeting the newly defined structures. | First Quantum CEO Tristan Pascall discusses Kansanshi’s S3 copper expansion in Zambia, citing $1.25bn investment, life extension by 20 years, and a broader goal of 1.5mn tpy copper capacity over the next decade. The piece emphasizes stable policy/investment conditions and copper demand themes, but offers no near-term supply shock. | Police and Transnet recovered about R3m of stolen copper cable at an unregistered scrapyard and arrested additional suspects for critical infrastructure theft at a Transnet depot. Transnet also welcomed convictions for copper cable theft, suggesting tougher enforcement against copper theft syndicates, with limited evidence of broader supply impact. | Oscillate PLC signed an earn-in/joint venture with La Miniere de L'Elephant to advance the Duekoue copper prospect in Ivory Coast, committing up to USD 650,000 over two years and funding early exploration. La Miniere earns a per-pound copper equivalent royalty, but no near-term production is indicated. | Aurubis secured a partial offtake deal for 75,000 tpy Canadian copper concentrate from Troilus (about 10% grade, implying 8,000-12,000 tpy copper content). The agreement supports smelter throughput amid tight clean concentrate supply and deep-negative TC/RCs, with pricing to be finalized under long-term terms. | Highland Copper reported positive metallurgical test work for its Copperwood copper project, redesigning the process flowsheet with ultrafine flotation and an MF2 circuit. Results indicate higher expected copper recoveries (up to 87.6%) and lower power and reagent costs. This supports future project engineering, but offers limited immediate supply impact. | Marimaca Copper Corp. announced an A$80 million brokered placement, with net proceeds earmarked for detailed design and engineering at its Marimaca oxide copper deposit and for exploration at the Pampa Medina and Marimaca sulphide targets. The financing supports project advancement, which can be sentimentally bullish for copper. | Pecoy Copper announced the closing of its reverse takeover and completion of acquisitions consolidating 100% control of the Pecoy copper-gold-molybdenum project in southern Peru. The company raised C$63.48M to fund exploration, metallurgical and engineering studies, and eventual development work, with no near-term output guidance.

Prince George is seeing a 68% increase in copper cable theft, including wire taken from street lights. City and RCMP coordination, tighter recycler rules under the 2012 Metal Recycling Act, and improved yard inspections aim to reduce buying of stolen copper. These measures may curb scrap monetization and near-term copper pressure. | The editorial says copper theft in Prince George and across Canada is rising, damaging 911 access, landlines, wireless services, streetlights and potentially hospital/alarms. It cites a TELUS-reported 68% jump in copper cable thefts and argues penalties are too light. It frames copper as a profitable black-market target, implying tighter effective supply. | SMM reports copper billet operating rates fell in August to 43.87%, dropping MoM and YoY, with weak order flow—especially extruded billets—and refrigeration sectors still in off-season. Policy uncertainty on recycled copper prompts production cuts and tight inventories, while firm imported recycled raw material prices raise costs. September may only rise slightly MoM. | Hot Chili completed a fully subscribed $14.1M entitlement offer to fund further drilling and development at its Chile Costa Fuego copper-gold project. The company plans a follow-up drill program at La Verde and continues financing studies and partnering efforts, reinforcing expectations for progress toward near-term copper production. | Argentina’s President Javier Milei unveiled reforms (RIGI) to attract large investors to develop copper projects, with major firms like BHP and Glencore engaging on benefits for specific ventures. The news frames potential supply growth amid electrification demand, but highlights policy and social-licence risks, limiting immediate near-term price impact. | Zambia inaugurated First Quantum Minerals’ $1.25bn Kansanshi Sulphide-3 (S3) expansion, commissioning a new 25 mtpa processing plant that should lift Kansanshi output to about 280,000 tpa and increase total copper from Kansanshi/Sentinel toward 480,000 tpa by 2027. The project also extends mine life and highlights Zambia’s output ramp plans. | JX Advanced Metals, a major Japanese copper smelter, plans to slash electrolytic copper output by several tens of thousands of tonnes versus earlier projections because it can’t purchase enough concentrates under current terms. It also plans to reduce smelting capacity amid weak TC/RCs and tighter concentrate supply, supporting copper prices. | Copper Fox announced completion of an execution plan for a prefeasibility study on its Van Dyke in-situ copper recovery project in Arizona, including planned drilling, metallurgical testing, and permitting steps. The company notes prior economics sensitivity to copper prices but provides no near-term production or supply change guidance. | Marimaca Copper Corp. completed an A$80m bookbuild to fund exploration at the Pampa Medina project and work at its Marimaca Oxide and sulphide targets in Chile. The financing supports ongoing development but provides no immediate production, supply, or trade disruption information for near-term copper pricing. | Great Southern Copper will seek shareholder approval at its AGM for a Rule 9 waiver so its largest shareholder can exercise warrants without triggering a mandatory offer. The company expects 6.3 million new shares from warrant exercise and plans further Chile exploration drilling, funded by the exercise and other financing. | Koryx Copper released a preliminary economic assessment for its Haib copper project in Namibia, estimating initial investment of $1.56B to produce 88,000 tonnes of copper per year for 23 years. The work is still pending licensing, funding, and a DFS in 2H 2026 before a final investment decision.

SMM says China copper plate/sheet and strip producers’ operating rate fell in August to 65.87%, below expectations, due to off-season/weak end-demand and reduced buying from higher late-August copper prices. Tight recycled-copper scrap supply raised costs. September recovery is minimal, implying limited near-term upside for copper prices. | SMM says China’s copper foil enterprises’ operating rate rose to 78.44% in August and is forecast to climb to 79.05% in September. Growth is led by lithium battery copper foil and strong downstream orders, with lower finished inventories and added support from peak-season stockpiling and export demand momentum. | Pakistan’s Reko Diq project, a major prospective copper (and gold) mine, secured $6 billion in loan pledges after a Saudi investor withdrew. Barrick Mining is now seeking alternative funding from U.S. and other international lenders, which may influence project timelines and future copper supply expectations. | Copper pipe & tube producer operating rates fell to 65.70% in August, with lower production and inventories, largely due to off-season effects. A 50% tariff on imported semi-finished copper products and weak US copper-pipe export orders hurt some producers, though US processing demand may lift utilization abroad. Sep forecast rises modestly; H2 copper pipe rates grow mildly. | Noronex reported deeper drilling results at its Fiesta copper project in Namibia, including multiple copper-bearing intersections and plans for a 7,000m reverse-circulation program. The program is funded through an earn-in alliance with South32. Assays confirm copper continuity at depth, but impacts are largely exploratory. | Panama’s Ministry of the Environment is conducting a comprehensive audit of the Donoso copper mine to verify compliance with environmental conservation standards. A biologist says past audits and rehabilitation/monitoring measures support the mine’s environmental management. The audit could last months, and no immediate production impact is mentioned. | Armenia’s copper concentrate output fell 8% to 159,125.3 tons in January–July 2025 versus the same period in 2024. The National Statistical Committee of Armenia reported the decline. Zinc and molybdenum saw rises and declines respectively, but copper production weakened year-on-year. | Venerable Ventures announced commencement of a 50,000 m diamond drill program at the Minto copper-gold-silver mine in Yukon. Drilling began August 23 after land-use permit approval, and the first hole of the 2025 program intersected mineralization, suggesting expansion potential at Minto North.

National Bank Financial initiated coverage of Ivanhoe Electric with an Outperform rating, citing its Santa Cruz copper project in Arizona expected to deliver domestic copper cathode for U.S. supply-chain goals. The piece highlights its technology for locating copper deposits and frames the outlook as supportive for copper development sentiment. | Anglo American and Teck plan a multi-billion merger to form Anglo Teck, positioning the new group as a leading copper producer with major mining and processing assets. The companies cite booming copper demand from electrification, renewables, EVs, and AI/data centers, and shares rose on the news. | Anglo American and Teck announced a multi-billion merger forming a new copper-focused producer, expected to complete in 12–18 months. The deal positions the combined group as a top global copper producer and highlights strong copper-linked demand from energy transition and AI, supporting bullish sentiment for copper. | Anglo American and Teck agreed to merge, forming a major copper producer expected to raise annual copper output and make copper over 70% of the combined mix. The deal could reshape M&A expectations in copper, with analysts noting potential bidding activity, supporting near-term bullish sentiment for copper prices. | The article headline says Anglo and Teck are forging a $53 billion mining giant based on expectations of surging copper demand. Such a move can strengthen market expectations for copper demand and support prices over the next days to weeks. No material information on nickel or aluminium is provided. | Transition Metals reports completion of an 8.9 km IP survey and defines 17 drill-ready targets at its Pike Warden project in Yukon, including porphyry copper targets. Mapping and sampling show pending assay results, with historical highlights including up to 7.49% Cu. The company plans drilling this fall. | GreenX reports results from a 58km2 airborne magnetic/radiometric survey over its Tannenberg copper project in Germany. It identifies the Mid-European Crystalline Zone as the deep source beneath historic Richelsdorf mines and notes anomalies extending into Tannenberg 2, increasing exploration potential. No immediate production change is stated. | Viscount Mining outlines expanded exploration at its Colorado Silver Cliff project. For Passiflora, new deep drilling (PF-03A) confirms thick copper-gold porphyry mineralization with open depth and higher-grade intervals, and permits have started for follow-up drilling. This supports positive copper discovery sentiment, though supply impact is limited near term. | Anglo American and Teck Resources agreed to merge to create a large-scale copper miner (“Anglo Teck”), targeting about 1.2 million tonnes of annual copper output, rising to 1.35 million by 2027. With most revenues linked to copper and investor interest rising, the news supports bullish copper sentiment near term. | Anglo American and Teck agreed to merge, creating one of the world’s largest copper producers with about 1.2–1.35 million tons of annual output and copper as the dominant production mix. Analysts viewed the deal as a potential start of a new wave of mining M&A, supporting expectations for copper supply and demand. | Freeport Resources reported results from an optimization study for its Yandera copper-gold project, evaluating phased high-grade, reduced-throughput development and revised economics using updated commodity pricing models. The release cites current and historical copper prices and recommends further test work, but provides no near-term production or supply changes. | Copper Standard Resources issued a portfolio and corporate update, electing not to proceed with an option on copper-related claims at the Colpayoc project while retaining adjacent interests. It also reports exploration results mentioning enrichment values up to 3% copper at Capricho. The update is corporate and exploration-focused. | Anglo American and Teck agreed to merge into a $53 billion copper-focused producer. The article frames copper as in rising demand from electrification/AI while supply may not keep up, implying support for copper prices. A related market talk adds Canadian government review risk for critical-minerals deals. | Anglo American and Teck agreed to merge into Anglo Teck, a $53bn copper giant with over 70% copper exposure, amid surging AI-driven data-center electricity demand. Copper has risen about 15% this year but recently pulled back after Trump’s 50% import levies started in August. Deal completion is expected in 12–18 months. | Anglo American and Teck agreed to merge assets, a deal framed by Codelco’s chairman as reinforcing Chile’s role in the global copper market. The alliance is expected to boost productivity in extraction and processing and position Chile more strongly amid rising demand linked to technology and green energy. | Anglo American and Teck Resources announced a $50 billion merger to form Anglo Teck, combining large copper mine portfolios and targeting production scale of over 1.5 million tons annually. The deal is positioned against a tight copper market with rising electrification demand and supply constraints, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Proalu SA will break ground on an $88bn-CFA aluminium processing plant in Douala, targeting 30,000 tons of aluminium coils and pre-coated products annually and producing electric cables including several thousand tons of aluminium and copper. The plant will use raw materials from Alucam with guaranteed sales, supporting import substitution and boosting near-term demand expectations for aluminium and copper. | CEO.CA interviews Gunnison Copper CEO Craig Hallworth after Gunnison began copper cathode production at the Johnson Camp Mine in Arizona. The company says it is now producing “100% American copper” with an on-site SX/EW refinery and cites its 2024 PEA economics. The news is intended to bolster investor confidence in copper output. | Anglo American agreed a £40bn merger with Teck Resources, creating one of the world’s largest copper producers. The deal combines adjacent Chilean copper mines (Quebrada Blanca and Collahuasi) and includes cost savings. Analysts expect consolidation and possible counter-offers, boosting copper sentiment in the near term. | PwC urged the Philippines to move up the copper value chain by investing in processing, refining, and manufacturing rather than only exporting mined ore. The piece highlights large copper and some nickel reserves and mentions a need for regulation and infrastructure, but offers no immediate supply or demand change. | A man in Wodonga allegedly cut out and stole about 50 metres of copper cabling from railway signalling, causing signal faults. Police estimated repair damage at $102,540, including materials and labour. The court heard the theft could have created safety risks, but the incident seems localized. | Anglo American’s £40bn merger with Teck Resources is positioned as copper-focused, combining adjacent Chilean mines (Quebrada Blanca and Collahuasi) and making the group a top five copper producer. The piece notes potential efficiencies but also risk from heavy income exposure to a single, volatile metal source.

Anglo American’s $85bn merger with Teck is framed as changing acquisition dynamics for other majors considering miners’ copper assets. The deal reduces the ease with which BHP or Rio Tinto could move on such holdings, but the excerpt doesn’t indicate immediate production or supply disruptions for copper. | Opinion piece argues the ~$US50bn Anglo American–Teck Resources nil-premium merger could spur “copper wars” as major miners seek more copper exposure. It highlights Anglo and Teck’s large copper output, constrained new supply, and potential consolidation around related Chile assets, supportive for copper sentiment. | Anglo American and Teck’s $US50bn merger (nil-premium) would combine large copper producers (~1.2m tonnes/year) and put both firms in play for rival bidders. The piece frames copper demand as booming and new supply as constrained, suggesting the deal could trigger further copper-focused M&A and support prices. | Kingfisher Metals reported initial 2025 drilling results from the HWY 37 copper-gold project in British Columbia, including 557.8 meters at 0.29% copper and 0.64% CuEq, with a higher-grade 234.35-meter subinterval at 1.00% CuEq. The company says results de-risk targets and justify more drilling. | Penang Customs intercepted an attempt to smuggle 125 tonnes of copper scrap worth RM5.29 million arriving from Thailand. The goods were misdeclared as used electronic components and were detained as prohibited imports requiring a permit. The incident reduces immediate copper scrap availability while investigations continue. | Teck’s proposed merger with Anglo could move Teck toward a foreign listing while keeping Vancouver and copper operations central. The deal combines Chilean assets (including Collahuasi and QB2) and is pitched as offering about US$800 million/year in savings, subject to Investment Canada review, supporting copper-focused sentiment. | Green Tech Mining Services and Nuhas Oman agreed to study localising copper cathode procurement under Oman’s Industrial Strategy 2040, aiming to reduce copper cathode imports. Oman’s imports rose to OMR25.7 million by June 2025 versus OMR45.3 million in 2024, highlighting the import dependence to be addressed. | Serbia’s Timok district discovery Malka Golaja is highlighted by Zijin Mining with preliminary JORC-style resource figures for copper (2.81m tons at 1.87% grade) and gold, with resources open at the edges. Since it’s early-stage resources, near-term supply impact is limited, but sentiment for copper improves. | Green Tech Mining and Nuhas Oman plan to procure locally produced copper cathode under an MoU to reduce reliance on imports. Oman’s copper cathode import bill fell but remains sizable, and a planned Suhar processing plant is set to add capacity. The plan supports the local copper value chain and exports.

Dutch greenhouse builders report demand disruption after U.S. tariffs on imported steel and aluminum. Clients are canceling orders and builders are securing fewer U.S. contracts as greenhouse construction becomes about 25% more expensive, with supply-chain uncertainty stalling activity. The article does not mention copper directly. | SMM reports China’s August copper rod output rose slightly, driven by higher copper cathode rod operating rates, while secondary rod rates fell MoM. High copper prices suppress downstream procurement and keep demand weak, limiting upside for “Golden September.” September production is forecast to rise modestly on late-month stocking. | Alaska Energy Metals says senior EPA and FAST-41 officials toured its Nikolai nickel project and that Alaska signed an MOU with the Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council to streamline permitting. The company frames this as improving predictability for developing domestic critical minerals, supporting sentiment for nickel and, to a lesser extent, copper. | Dufferin OPP is investigating an increase in copper thefts across the region, targeting telephone wires, utility lines, and cable boxes, often overnight or in daylight by impersonating utility workers. Police warn against tampering with live infrastructure. Authorities urge residents to report suspicious activity immediately. | Teck’s CEO discusses its merger with Anglo American, saying the combined company will focus on copper and invest $4.5 billion in Canada’s critical minerals sector. The deal includes $2.4 billion to extend Highland Valley Copper mine life and aims to improve Quebrada Blanca performance, boosting bullish copper fundamentals. | Farm equipment makers say high U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminium are squeezing them. Demand stayed weak in Guangdong, but overall buildup remained constrained. | Zambia’s kwacha rose to a near two-year high as investors gained confidence in the country’s copper industry recovery following a drought. The article cites expectations for record copper production this year, which is driving the rally and suggests improving copper supply prospects. | The article argues copper volatility is outweighed by underlying supply-demand pressures. It cites potential mining disruptions causing a supply deficit, slightly revised 2025 output guidance at Kamoa-Kakula, and tariff-driven demand changes tied to electrification and data centers. Overall, it expects copper prices to remain supported. | Zambia’s kwacha surged as optimism returned to the country’s copper industry amid a broader global copper price boom. The article cites strong demand from EV and renewable energy, major mine expansion investments, and targets for higher output, reinforcing near-term expectations for copper. | Goldman Sachs said copper is likely to trade within $10,000–$11,000/t in 2026/27 due to a surplus, citing potential weaker Chinese buying and U.S. inventory effects. The cuts extend through 2028 and add to broader mine supply challenges, supporting expectations for tighter copper concentrate supply. | Copper wire theft has knocked out power at a Gilroy elementary school, highlighting ongoing thefts in California. The state attorney general and lawmakers are pushing a bill requiring junk dealers to document sellers and metal details, with Governor Newsom deciding soon. The measure targets incentives behind copper theft. | Rumble Resources announced an expansion of its Wilmac copper-gold project in British Columbia by adding Lamont Ridge and the Plume tenure application.
