The slump raises concerns about weaker near-term industrial demand, pressuring base metals exposure in Mexico (copper mining and aluminium components). It also highlights tariff/USMCA uncertainty, adding further downside risk to demand.

SMM reports post-holiday copper social inventories rose less than expected (+18,000 mt to 166,300 mt) and fell YoY, with Shanghai showing destocking (-3,000 mt) due to weaker imported arrivals and fewer cathode shipments. Elsewhere inventories rose, and high prices suppress demand, implying continued accumulation. | The article says copper theft in Australia is rising as copper prices climb. It quotes measures implemented through a regulated, monitored recycling/scrap-yard system aimed at discouraging theft, suggesting compliance with scrap-yard protocols helps keep theft under control. It does not indicate major sustained supply impacts. | Artemis executed a land access agreement for the Cassowary copper-gold exploration project in Western Australia, enabling planned heritage surveys and initial diamond drilling in December (subject to approvals). The target is interpreted as IOCG-type copper-gold, but the news contains no production, reserves, or immediate supply impact. | Copper prices neared a record near $11,104/t but slipped after Trump threatened higher tariffs on China, raising concerns about demand. Analysts say further upside likely requires stronger China-led stimulus and demand growth. Inventories are mixed, suggesting surplus pockets in the US and uncertainty over whether supply deficits are real. | Codelco said losses from an El Teniente mine accident are likely 45% higher than previously estimated, while output is still targeted to rise slightly overall despite a planned 48,000-ton reduction for 2025. Together with other disruptions, supply tightness is cited as pushing copper prices near record highs. | Codelco said a rock-burst at El Teniente, started in July, will reduce output and hit earnings, with an expanded estimate of lower production. The incident worsens global refined supply tightness alongside other disruptions, contributing to a shift toward a 2026 deficit per ICSG revisions. | Market participants are preparing for LME Week with major uncertainty over the 2026 copper concentrates TC/RC benchmark, with many expecting it to settle at zero or deeper negative levels due to persistent concentrate tightness after disruptions at major mines. Spot TC indices are far below the current benchmark, widening seller-buyer gaps and driving contract re-pricing discussions. | Copper futures gained 3.45%, nearing record highs around $10,775/ton, driven by a weaker dollar and tight supply alongside mining disruptions. Analysts say durability depends on Chinese demand and inventory levels, with UBS constructive on future tightness while Goldman flags potential oversupply and demand caps above $11,000/ton. | Openreach says it will activate Stop Sell in 94 more UK exchange locations covering about 960,000 premises, ending sales of legacy copper-based phone and broadband where ultrafast full fibre is available to most premises. Customers must upgrade to new full fibre services, reducing incremental copper-linked demand. | China’s 2025 import data show diverging commodity trends: copper imports jumped 24.4% year-on-year and overall imports rose to a 17-month high in September, indicating firmer Chinese industrial/infrastructure demand. Iron ore also rose, while coal imports fell sharply. The copper surge is the clearest near-term bullish signal for copper. | Copper is rallying near record levels on strong projected Chinese demand for 2025 and persistent global supply constraints, including flooding at Grasberg and production losses at Chile’s El Teniente. U.S.-China tariff threats add volatility, but recent tariff-related dips are followed by recovery signals, keeping near-term bias bullish. | Verity Resources said it gained 100% control of a copper-silver exploration portfolio in Botswana by buying out its JV partner’s 66% stake for $206,060. The acquired assets include multiple high-grade copper prospects and a deposit reporting copper, nickel and PGM occurrences, but no new near-term production plans were announced. | Britain’s EV charging network is being repeatedly vandalized as thieves target copper cables, disabling chargers and stranding drivers. The article links the theft wave to soaring global copper prices (cited at $11,000/tonne) and describes large replacement costs, suggesting stronger scrap/cabling demand for copper. | Capstone Copper said Orion will invest up to $360m for a 25% interest in the Santo Domingo and Sierra Norte projects, conditional on positive FID and followed by matching and contingent payments. Funds include exploration to advance copper resources and processing studies, de-risking development financing and supporting future copper output expectations.

Rio Tinto reported slightly lower Pilbara iron ore shipments but improved production. It upgraded full-year bauxite guidance, citing better utilisation at Weipa/Amrun, suggesting stronger aluminium supply expectations. The firm also said copper production remains on track for the higher end of guidance, supporting copper sentiment. | A nationwide power outage in Chile and several accidents—mine suspensions, mechanical failures, earthquakes, and tailings/processing disruptions—reduced output across major copper operations (Chuquicamata, El Teniente, Antamina, Kamoa-Kakula, Grasberg, Quebrada Blanca, and others). Guidance was repeatedly cut, worsening supply tightness for 2025 and likely lifting copper prices. | Capstone Copper said Orion Resource Partners will invest up to $360 million for a 25% interest in the Santo Domingo and Sierra Norte copper projects. Funds will support exploration and advance engineering/financing toward sanctioning. The transaction reduces Capstone’s funding burden and improves confidence in project execution, which can support copper sentiment. | Rio Tinto lifted its 2025 bauxite output guidance to 59–61 million tonnes and reported higher alumina and aluminium production. The company also said the Oyu Tolgoi underground ramp-up remains on track to boost copper output by more than 50% this year, supporting both aluminium and copper supply expectations. | Global copper mine output is projected to rise modestly in 2025, but declines in Australia and Indonesia constrain expansion. Indonesian export bans on copper concentrate/anode sludge from Jan 2025 and disruptions at Freeport’s Grasberg add near-term supply tightness, partially offset by gains in Zambia, Chile, Peru, and the DRC. | SMM says China’s copper foil enterprise operating rate rose to 82.17% in September and is expected to increase to 83.72% in October. Growth was driven by higher shipments, especially lithium battery copper foil and electronic circuit foil, supported by robust NEV/ESS demand and stockpiling ahead of holidays. | Copper prices rose sharply as Indonesia’s Grasberg mine suffered mudslide damage and fatal accidents, leading to force majeure and large output losses. The market shifted toward a deficit in 2026, while a weaker dollar boosted base metals sentiment. China’s solar subsidy rollback also caused short-term front-loaded copper demand. | BHP’s CEO said the company is reviewing whether to reopen defunct mines in Arizona’s US copper belt after a major shift in US government policy. The policy change has encouraged BHP to increase exploration in the US, prompting a reassessment of options for restarting copper production. | The piece projects a major expansion in the global copper market through 2034, driven by electrification, renewables, and EV adoption, while noting supply frictions such as declining ore grades and slow mine development. It frames expectations for ongoing price volatility but suggests a supportive demand floor for copper in the coming weeks. | Renewed U.S.-China tariff threats reignited volatility, but copper was firm and reportedly bounced above US$5 a pound, with follow-through into copper equities. The interview ties the move to renewed interest in domestically produced copper, making previously uneconomic U.S. mines viable at current prices.

Authorities charged a man caught inside a manhole attempting to steal copper wire in West Los Angeles. The piece links recent thefts to service outages and infrastructure damage, citing AB 476 signed into law to strengthen penalties for illegal dealers. The events are local; no direct market-wide copper supply impact is stated. | Wood Mackenzie forecasts copper demand rising sharply through electrification, data-centre growth, EVs, and emerging Asian industrialisation. It warns that insufficient mine investment and higher disruption assumptions could tighten supply, depleting inventories and increasing price volatility, with potential data-centre construction spikes of 15% or more. | Capstone Copper said Orion Resource Partners will buy a 25% stake in Chilean Santo Domingo and Sierra Norte projects for about $360m, with payments tied to a positive FID and project milestones. Orion also buys Capstone shares for $10m to fund exploration. The transaction supports copper project development but lacks immediate output impact. | Camino Minerals announced mobilization for discovery drilling at its Los Chapitos copper project in Peru, with MINEM naming the project among 15 priority exploration initiatives. The release details surface Cu-Ag trenching results and expects drilling to start in early November, but no production impact is indicated. | Taseko reports wellfield operations starting at its Florence Copper project with first copper cathode expected in about three months. It also cites strong Q3 Gibraltar operational results, including higher copper output and sales and improving recoveries. Company expects further production gains in Q4 and raised 2025 copper guidance. | NorthWest Copper reported assay results from drill hole K-25-272 at its Kwanika deposit, including 16.9 metres at 1.37% copper equivalent and additional near-surface mineralization suited for open pit. The company says the intercepts extend higher-grade zones 50 metres down dip and remain open for further growth, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Taseko Mines said it has received final regulatory approvals for its 100%-owned Florence Copper project in Arizona and is beginning wellfield operations, marking the start of commercial production. Construction is largely completed and commissioning will run in parallel with startup. The company also reported Gibraltar operations and copper sales of 26.3 million pounds in the quarter. | Malaysia’s SIRIM import supervision for copper scrap has tightened since 2021, raising inspection costs and driving trade diversion to Thailand, Vietnam, and the UAE. Concerns over constrained concentrate availability and negative treatment charges support a bullish tone. | Panelists said copper smelters are coping with very negative TC/RCs, but the concentrate market remains tightly supplied. The surge targets LME-monitored depots and bonded warehouses, potentially replenishing prompt supply and easing tightness, which should pressure near-term copper pricing. | The piece argues mining is in a “renaissance,” driven by energy-transition demand and tighter supply expectations. It specifically highlights the IEA view that countries need much more copper and says investors expect strong deal activity in gold and copper, reinforcing bullish sentiment toward copper prices. | Wood Mackenzie forecasts global refined copper demand rising 24% by 2035, with major needs for additional mine capacity and more recycling. The piece links bullish sentiment to rising benchmark copper prices as investors anticipate tighter availability from electrification and data centers. | FireFly Metals reports step-out drilling at its Green Bay (Ming) copper-gold project, extending known mineralisation by 430–650m beyond prior drilling and confirming a long DHEM anomaly. Cobre Limited began the environmental impact assessment for an in-situ copper recovery pilot plant at its Ngami project in Botswana, following ministry approval of its work plan. The study supports permitting for construction, after tests showed 82% average copper recovery, and could improve outlook for future copper production. | Saskatchewan’s McIlvenna Bay copper project is 60% complete and on track for commercial production by mid-2026, with a $1 billion investment. The mine will produce copper and other metals and relies on green power/hydrogen-related infrastructure and planned global shipping, but output timing is still future. | The US Department of Commerce issued 95 new requests to add additional steel and aluminum derivative products to Section 232 tariffs, potentially covering nearly $300 billion of imports. Public comments are due October 21. If approved, broader tariff coverage would likely increase aluminium-related costs and weigh on metal-intensive activity. | BHP is reportedly reviewing whether to reopen four dormant Arizona copper mines and reprocess tailings, citing higher copper prices and more supportive US government policy (tariffs and expedited permitting). The company is stepping up exploration and drilling in the Globe-Miami area, potentially boosting future US copper supply expectations. | Pacific Ridge Exploration announced drill hole RDP-25-011 intersected 112.2m of 1.35% copper equivalent at its RDP copper-gold project in British Columbia, including several higher-grade sub-intervals. A new U.S.-China trade flare-up is discussed as a reminder of supply-chain fragility for copper. The piece argues that copper demand should rise, supported by AI-driven electrical/compute buildout and increased European defense spending. It also notes renewed focus on diverse delivery and smelting reinvestment, affecting near-term supply expectations. | HSBC upgraded Freeport-McMoRan shares to Buy, citing higher metals price assumptions. The firm expects FCX to benefit from strength in copper (and gold), with copper up strongly year-to-date. | Bell Copper says Arizona Geological Survey research has started at its Big Sandy porphyry copper project, using company core samples to assess potential critical minerals and supergene profiles. The company also plans to resume drilling (BS-4) after a suspension, but no immediate production or supply changes are mentioned. | ExGen Resources and MTB Metals announced an arrangement to merge, creating a better-funded copper-gold and lithium exploration/development company. The story attributes the theft surge to copper’s higher price and cites a newly signed California bill tightening scrap/junk-dealer rules on penalties and identity verification, aiming to curb stolen-copper recycling. Reward offered for arrests. | The article says a deal has been reached to keep Glencore’s Mount Isa copper smelter and Townsville refinery operating, ending months of uncertainty for workers and local suppliers. Pakistan’s finance minister projects the Reko Diq copper and gold project’s first year of commercial operations could generate $2.8 billion in copper exports, raising the export base by about 10%. Critics argue funding for the western link to Mount Isa is vague, raising concerns about potential delays affecting grid connection and copper-linked investment timelines. | True North Copper appointed a new CEO to push development of its Queensland copper assets, including drill-out of the Mt Oxide/Aquila discovery and restart assessments for the Cloncurry project. The company cites strong copper assay intercepts and potential upgrades to restart economics, referencing a recent rise in copper prices. | China’s September copper enamelled wire exports rose 21.17% YoY to 12,248.48 mt, despite a 4.35% MoM drop. Cumulative exports for Jan–Sep were up 24.91% YoY. Total Jan–Sep exports increased 1.22% YoY, while Vietnam and the US declined. | Core Silver reports a new copper-bearing porphyry vein system at the Laverdiere Copper Project, with surface assays up to 1.35% Cu and accompanying silver and molybdenum. The company says the confirmed Cu-Mo-Ag mineralized footprint now spans about 4.5 km and remains open for expansion, supporting bullish copper sentiment. | Talon Metals said Kennecott Exploration granted a 12-month extension to deliver a feasibility study and make an additional US$10m payment to earn up to 60% of the Tamarack nickel-copper project. The company aims to align the study with Minnesota’s H1 2026 scoping environmental review, supporting permitting timelines. | Talon Metals says Kennecott Exploration granted a 12-month extension to deliver a feasibility study for the Tamarack nickel-copper project, plus a US$10 million payment for an additional 9% earn-in. The company aims to align the study release with Minnesota scoping environmental document timing in H1 2026.

BHP reported first-quarter copper production of 493,600 tons, up 4% from a year earlier, citing record concentrator throughput and improved recoveries at Escondida. BHP also noted resilient commodity demand, but the key market-relevant item for prices is the increase in copper output. | Solis Minerals announced a $5.9 million placement to fund acquisition and exploration of Peruvian copper projects, including drilling at Ilo Este and planned drilling at Cucho and Cinto. Proceeds will cover exploration and working capital. The release is primarily corporate financing, with no immediate production or supply disruption impact. | Solis Minerals announced a $5.9 million placement to fund acquisition and exploration of its Peruvian Cucho copper project, plus drilling at Ilo Este and Cinto. Proceeds are earmarked for drilling and working capital. The update is copper-specific but reflects company-level exploration financing rather than immediate market fundamentals. | Star Copper Corp. says drilling has started on its Copper Creek satellite target within the Golden Triangle, BC, with two ~400m first-pass holes to test copper-gold structures. The termination followed a long period of no drilling activity in 2025 and leaves Anglo American with a smaller African mining footprint. Anglo American cites broader restructuring toward copper exposure, but the Zambian exit dampens copper-linked activity sentiment. | KEFI says it has successfully closed US$240m of senior debt for Ethiopia’s Tulu Kapi project (70% of total debt), and is now moving to finalize an oversubscribed ~US$100m equity risk portion with shareholder meetings in November. Resettlement, access roads, and grid connection continue. | Lara Exploration reported results of a Preliminary Economic Assessment for its Planalto copper-gold project in Brazil’s Carajás district, estimating multi-decade copper concentrate production and project economics based on assumed copper prices. However, the project is not yet in production, limiting any immediate supply effect on copper markets. | Atalaya Mining reported third-quarter 2025 copper production up 1.9% to 12,123 tonnes and said output remains consistent with its full-year guidance of 49,000–52,000 tonnes. It cited strong plant availability and favourable cost trends, alongside higher realized copper prices, while maintaining optimism on growth projects. | China customs data show September 2025 copper foil imports at 6,508.93 mt (+7.28% YoY, -4.60% MoM) and exports at 5,174.88 mt (+44.69% YoY, -0.98% MoM). Export values also rose sharply. The report links stability/exports to AI server and 5G buildout and capacity expansion. | Romios Gold began a 10-day geological mapping and sampling program on its Kinkaid copper-gold-silver project in Nevada to advance PM Skarn and Bismark Hill targets toward drill readiness. Prior assays include high copper values in quartz veins; however, no production or supply changes are announced. | Gold Hart Copper Corp. launched additional geophysical surveying at its Tolita gold-copper asset in Chile and expanded drill pads for upcoming drilling. At its Nessa properties, new road construction enabled the discovery of additional visible copper-bearing surface showings and historic pits, with further surveys planned. | Los Angeles reports widespread copper wire theft that has disabled streetlights and increased repair backlogs. City and business leaders urge Gov. Newsom to sign AB 476, which adds verification and penalties for recyclers buying stolen scrap and restricts possession of certain public-infrastructure wire. A reward program is also approved. | MMG said Q3 total copper output rose 11% to just over 127,000 tonnes, mainly driven by Las Bambas. This implies higher near-term copper supply. While the company cited financial milestones, the production update is the main factor likely to weigh on copper prices over the next days to weeks. | PPX Mining reported assay results from four underground drill holes at its Igor Project in Peru, including strong copper grades in sulfide zones (up to 11.1% Cu) alongside high gold and silver values. The update is exploratory and does not indicate production changes, limiting immediate copper supply impact.

Citi says it remains bullish on copper and aluminium, projecting copper to reach $12,000/mt over the next 6–12 months and aluminium to hit $3,500/mt by 2027. The near-term discussion focuses on gold and the US shutdown, with no direct short-term catalyst for these metals. | BHP reported a strong start to the year, with group copper production up 4% to 494,000 tonnes due to record throughput at Escondida and stable output across other assets. It reaffirmed full-year copper guidance and cited competitor disruptions tightening supply, alongside higher average realized copper prices. | Sponsored article claims copper demand is set to soar as AI data centers, EVs, and power-grid modernization expand, citing expected higher copper intensity and data center buildouts. It also notes production curtailments from mine disruptions. Overall, it frames a bullish near-term copper demand/supply outlook. | Hercules Metals reported long copper-bearing drill intersections at its Leviathan porphyry target (e.g., 273 m of 0.60% Cu) indicating grade continuity and potential scale. The update supports exploration expectations but does not change near-term production. Aluminium is not addressed beyond unrelated context. | Amarc and Freeport report completion of a 2025 expansion-focused drill program at the AuRORA Cu-Au-Ag project in BC’s JOY District, including step-out and infill drilling and expectations of further assays. Freeport funds the 2025 program. The news is exploration-focused, with limited near-term implications for copper supply and prices. | Comex copper finished up 0.63% to $4.9610, according to the data talk. The move reflects modest short-term strength in copper futures, which can influence near-term expectations for the metal’s spot and related pricing, though the size suggests no major shock. | Teck reported Q3 profits aided by higher copper prices, even as its Chile Quebrada Blanca copper mine struggles after an expansion. Tailings drainage problems raised costs and delayed ramp-up, prompting a production guidance cut. An exclusive report covers protests around Sterlite’s Thoothukudi copper plant, where a feasibility study recommends recycled copper use, closing some polluting sections, and a CSR corpus. It also notes ongoing counter-protests, leaving uncertain timing and capacity changes that could influence near-term copper supply expectations. | DRC’s Gecamines and Zambia’s IDC are partnering with Mercuria to trade copper cathode and concentrates, using trading capacity to monetize rising production and manage smelting constraints. Gecamines traded ~88,000 tonnes via Mercuria in 2024 and expects higher volumes as output ramps up. Zambia’s IRL has sold 200,000+ tonnes of concentrates. | Phoenix police report copper theft cases more than doubling, with thieves targeting utility boxes, street lights and construction sites. Losses include cut internet/phone service and risks to hospital connectivity. Authorities cite rising copper prices as a driver, but the impact described is local infrastructure disruption. | SMM describes a restructuring of global copper scrap flows after China’s 2018 solid waste import ban and subsequent tightening by Southeast Asian countries. Compliance and traceability requirements reduce low-quality supply, potentially supporting higher copper prices. | Angola’s Mines Minister said the Tetelo copper mine will begin production soon. The first major large-scale copper venture is a $250m investment and is expected to yield about 25,000 tonnes per year of copper concentrate initially, potentially adding supply ahead of broader expansion. | Antofagasta cut 2025 guidance for cash costs and capex, expecting net cash costs of USD1.20–1.30/lb versus the prior USD1.45–1.65 range. Copper output is guided to the low end of 660,000–700,000 tonnes amid rising unit costs and weaker copper sales. | Magna Mining reported new assay results from drillhole FNX6083-W2 at the Levack Mine in Sudbury, including copper-rich chalcopyrite veins (up to 19.3% Cu) and nickel-rich pentlandite-pyrrhotite veins (up to 12.4% Ni), alongside PGM and silver. It is an exploration update with assays pending for other holes. | Fathom Nickel reported updated mapping and pXRF/geochemical results extending the Gochager Lake project’s Ni-Cu sulphide “container rock” along a 3.5 km strike length. The company highlights disseminated and vein-hosted pyrrhotite/pentlandite with potential for further high-grade discoveries, but provides no production or confirmed new reserves. | Freeport-McMoRan reported better-than-expected profit as higher copper prices outweighed lower output after a mudflow forced a shutdown at the Grasberg mine in Indonesia, leaving seven workers dead. Realized copper prices rose about 9% year-on-year, supporting results, while Q4 copper sales guidance declined. | Antofagasta reported Q3 output of 161,800 tons and revised its annual copper production outlook to the lower end of 660,000–700,000 tons. Nine-month production rose 2.8% year-on-year, but weaker annual expectations and reduced investment, amid tense supply conditions, pressured the stock and sentiment near-term. | A chief geologist was appointed for Sterling Metals’ Soo Copper Project north of Sault Ste. Marie. The company highlights the high-grade copper discovery and ongoing drilling at priority targets, plus a new exploration agreement with the Garden River First Nation covering related claims in the area. | Nornickel said its nickel output fell 4% to 140,000 tons in January–September 2025 and copper output fell 4% to 313,000 tons. The company cited changes in ore quality and the need to replenish work-in-progress inventories. Q3 nickel rose but Q3 copper declined to 100,000 tons. | Nornickel said its nickel output fell 4% to 140,000 tons and copper output fell 4% to 313,000 tons in Jan–Sep 2025. The company attributed declines to a higher share of disseminated ores, fewer rich ores, and replenishing work-in-progress inventories. Q3 nickel rose vs Q2, while copper fell slightly. | Copper wire theft is disrupting Los Angeles EV charging expansion as thieves cut and hack chargers’ copper cables. The story says rising copper prices are making theft more common, while repairs cost $650–$1,500 per incident. California’s AB 476 increases penalties and companies add anti-theft hardware and cable designs. | Angola’s Tetelo copper project, a $250 million mine led by China’s Shining Star Icarus, is expected to start production soon, Reuters reported. The move would add new supply from Angola alongside other African copper producers. Although no date is given, the announcement is supportive for copper prices in the near term. | Rural Women New Zealand urged that members be considered in a telecommunications sector review as a copper network phase-out is contemplated. While the piece focuses on inclusivity in services, it signals potential reduction in copper-based network use, which can weigh on copper demand expectations. | Canadian Critical Minerals Inc. said it earned USD $167,000 from a provisional payment for 942 dry metric tonnes of stockpiled mineralized material sold to New Gold’s New Afton operation. Noronex has begun a new 3,000-metre reverse-circulation drilling program at its Cgae Cgae copper project in Botswana, funded in part by South32. Copper and nickel impacts are not addressed. | CME reports December copper futures extended a three-session winning streak, gaining about 3% over three sessions and ending the week up 2.93%. The move is linked to optimism ahead of a US-China meeting and expectations of Fed rate cuts after CPI readings kept policy on a cut path, supporting industrial demand outlook.

Oyu Tolgoi LLC, controlled by Rio Tinto and jointly owned with Mongolia, said it is conducting an internal investigation and sought law enforcement help over allegations of corruption tied to procurement. Oyu Tolgoi is a key expanding copper mine, and disruptions could affect short-term copper supply expectations. | Rio Tinto’s Oyu Tolgoi, a major Mongolian copper operation, has asked law enforcement to support an internal investigation into allegations tied to procurement and unethical conduct. The probe adds near-term execution and supply-risk concerns for an expanding underground project expected to lift copper output. | Anglo American and Teck approved a merger of equals to create a large copper producer, combining major assets across multiple regions. The deal targets substantial synergies and projects an additional 175,000 tons of annual copper output, but remains subject to regulatory and competition approvals, limiting immediate near-term supply clarity. | Antofagasta reported a modest 1% increase in Q3 copper output and warned full-year production is likely to fall at the lower end of its guidance range. Despite the forecast trim, the incremental improvement in production is likely to bolster near-term expectations for copper supply and sentiment. | A Reuters poll shows analysts lifted 2026 copper forecasts after mine disruptions in Indonesia, Congo and Chile raised shortage and deficit worries, flipping consensus to a market deficit. Aluminium is also supported by concern that China’s output is near a government-imposed cap, reducing upside in the 2026 surplus outlook. | Solis Minerals is expanding its copper footprint in Peru, funding Cucho and further drilling after a A$5.9m raise. The report highlights a stronger US dollar as a macro headwind for dollar-denominated commodities, noting copper typically falls with USD strength, pressuring expected revenues and valuation sentiment. | Australia’s market fell as investors digested company-specific news, but it notes that copper steadied just below its record high as US-China tensions eased and trade-deal hopes rose. This suggests near-term improved sentiment for copper demand tied to reduced trade friction.

Javelin Minerals reports eight new magnetic and gravity targets at its Coogee copper-gold project near Kalgoorlie, highlighting historical grades including 2.31% copper over 3m and 2.54% copper over 7m. The company plans further air-core and diamond drilling, but no immediate copper supply changes are indicated. | Anglo American said copper output fell 9% in the first nine months of the year to 526,000 tonnes versus 575,000 in 2024. The company kept its 2025 copper guidance, but the lower production figure can weigh on near-term supply expectations and copper prices. | Anglo American reported third-quarter copper output broadly flat at 183,500 tonnes, with guidance unchanged, despite some site-level grade and recovery offsets. Nickel production rose 2% to 10,100 tonnes. The major mine-fire disruption was to the company’s steelmaking coal business, not tracked metals. | Anglo American said its Q3 copper production rose to 184,000 tonnes and reiterated/kept 2025 copper guidance in a 690,000–750,000 tonne range. It also reported changes in iron ore output and updated iron ore guidance, but copper was the only directly tied tracked-metal input. Overall impact on copper prices appears modest. | EPA approved an aquifer exemption in Utah’s Burro Canyon aquifer, allowing Lisbon Valley Mining Company to expand underground recovery operations at the Lisbon Valley Copper Mine. The decision follows prior BLM approval and permits Class III injection for commercially recoverable copper. The expanded plan is set to boost total production. | Kodiak Copper Corp. begins further metallurgical testwork for its MPD copper-gold porphyry project in southern British Columbia. The program targets higher copper and gold recoveries by optimizing rougher/cleaner flotation and gold characterization. Results are expected in Q1 2026 to support mineral resource definition and future development guidance. | Copper Fox Metals says it has started a maiden diamond drilling program at its 100% owned Mineral Mountain porphyry copper project in Arizona. The 2025 drilling will test depth extensions of copper-molybdenum mineralization and a chargeability anomaly. The release provides geology and drill targets but no production or supply changes. | Tinka Resources announced the start of its first diamond-drill program at the 100%-owned Silvia gold-copper project in Peru. First drill results are expected before late December 2025, with assays pending. | Australia is considering further taxpayer support for power-intensive metals producers, highlighted by Rio Tinto’s Tomago aluminium smelter warning it may close without an affordable long-term energy contract. The article also cites a recent Glencore copper smelter bailout, indicating governments are easing near-term supply risks. | The piece argues electronics makers face rising copper costs as forecasts point to higher copper prices driven by limited supply. It cites flooding at Indonesia’s Grasberg mine and reduced mining activity in Chile and Peru, projecting market deficits in 2025-26 that can tighten supply versus demand. | Koryx Copper reported assay results from 17 drill holes at its Haib copper project in southern Namibia. Results show continued positive copper intersections across four targets, refinement of the geological model, and an expected updated MRE before year-end. The company expects potential upside in copper (and molybdenum) resource estimates. | Anglo American said copper production fell 9% year-on-year in the first nine months of 2025, mainly due to lower ore grades and recovery issues at Chile’s Collahuasi mine. With demand rising for energy transition and AI, the shortfall is expected to tighten supply and add upward pressure to copper prices.

True North Copper reports early drilling results from maiden holes at its Salebury prospects near the Ernest Henry IOCG operation in Queensland, identifying copper sulphide and IOCG alteration indicators. The company plans infill and deeper drilling and potential restart of oxide operations, but results are still preliminary. | Glencore said its own-sourced copper production fell 17% in January–September to 583,500 tonnes and it tightened 2025 copper guidance on lower ore grades at some mines. While third-quarter output rose, the reduced supply outlook is likely to weigh on near-term copper market sentiment. | Police allege two men forced entry to a communications depot in East Maitland and stole copper cabling valued at more than $15,000. Officers found cable in a vehicle inside the compound after reports of power tools. Both were charged and refused bail pending court proceedings. | Glencore cut the upper end of its 2025 copper production guidance to 850,000–875,000 tons after copper output fell in the first nine months due to lower grades. It produced 583,500 tons year-to-date, down 17%. Guidance reduction signals tighter near-term copper supply expectations. | Glencore’s copper output dropped in the first nine months as mined grades and recoveries fell, leading the company to trim the upper end of its full-year guidance. The shortfall is linked to ore sequencing impacts at Collahuasi, Antamina, Antapaccay and KCC. Nickel and aluminium are not discussed for price relevance. | Police in Malawi’s Nkhata Bay are investigating the theft of 23 metres of copper wire worth over K3 million from an ESCOM transformer. The theft is linked to a reported power outage caused by damaged utility equipment. Authorities seek suspects and encourage public tips to recover the stolen copper wire. | India’s copper demand rose 9.3% in FY25 to 1,878 kt, supported by faster infrastructure and building construction, clean-energy additions, and stronger consumer durables and EV-linked segments. The International Copper Association India links policy momentum to higher copper usage, implying improved demand outlook that can support copper prices. | Northstar Gold Corp. secured non-brokered private placement funding to advance the Cam Copper Project, including permitting, engineering, and initiation of a Zone 2 surgical mining pilot. The financing supports a high-grade copper extraction program and a forthcoming NI 43-101 technical report, aiming to accelerate timelines toward production. | Copper jumped to record highs on the LME as mine accidents in Chile and Indonesia reduced output and tightened physical supply. Traders also cited a thaw in U.S.-China trade improving growth and demand expectations, plus expectations of a Fed rate cut. Tariff uncertainty still prompted U.S. copper stockpiling. | Copper hit a record high on the LME, driven by hopes of a U.S.-China trade truce and escalating supply concerns. A major Indonesian mine remains shut after a deadly mudslide, tightening global availability. The article also highlights strong structural demand from energy transition and AI/data centers. | India’s copper demand is reported to have risen 9.3% to 1,878 kt in FY25, driven by fast growth in infrastructure and building construction, plus renewable energy capacity additions, consumer durables, and broader mobility including EV-related segments. The data implies stronger demand expectations for copper markets. | Glencore’s third-quarter update shows a 17% decline in copper production over nine months and trims full-year guidance, reinforcing fears of tighter global supplies. The report also highlights disruptions at major Chilean and Indonesian mines and an ICSG cut to 2025 supply growth, implying supply will lag demand and price pressure to the upside. | Market talk highlights Glencore’s results, saying the firm delivered a solid quarter and mostly narrowed guidance. Copper production rose 36% on-quarter on higher grades, though analysts warn production must rise sharply in the last six months to hit guidance. Overall, the update is viewed as supportive for copper. | London Metal Exchange copper prices hit record highs, driven by supply worries. LME three-month copper rose to around $11,173/ton as mine disruptions were reported and producer forecasts weakened. Glencore trimmed the upper end of its copper output guidance after lower production, while Anglo American flagged reduced output from Collahuasi. | Southern Copper reported Q3 and nine-month production results. Supportive macro factors (weaker USD, US-China trade optimism) further bolster near-term demand expectations, keeping upward price pressure. | Morgan Stanley signals a major shift toward commodities, highlighting a bullish view on copper. It projects the copper market could face its largest deficit in over two decades by 2026, citing tight physical supply, dwindling inventories, and strong demand. The strategy may steer institutional flows toward copper-linked assets. | Copper surged to a record high as renewed mine-supply concerns and expectations of a US-China trade deal boosted sentiment. Glencore cut copper production guidance and the ICSG sees a refined deficit next year. Analysts warn gains may cool if demand doesn’t strengthen and much of the macro catalyst is priced in. | Policy Notice No. 770 standardizes investment/tax incentives and removes irregular refund/subsidy practices for secondary copper rod makers, increasing effective tax burden. Enterprises in key Jiangxi clusters suspended production and August output fell ~30% (≈48,800 mt supply impact). A US 50% tariff on unrefined copper adds local volatility, supporting expectations of higher copper prices and tightness.

ICSG says several large copper mines suffered accidents (Kakula seismic flooding, El Teniente collapse, Grasberg mud inflow), cutting 2025 mine supply growth and tightening mined concentrate availability. It projects a 2026 refined deficit risk (150,000 tonnes short) versus earlier surplus expectations, supporting copper price firmness. | First Quantum reported Q3 2025 copper output of 104,626 tonnes, down 9% year-on-year, largely due to weaker performance at its key Zambian mines Sentinel and Kansanshi. The deal requires due diligence before final share issuance in January 2026, with no near-term output impact. | Abitibi Metals announced drill results showing a 150-metre step-out extension of the high-grade Western Copper-Gold zone at B26 in Quebec, including multiple copper-rich CuEq intercepts and a new copper-gold discovery to the west. The update supports continued expansion potential but does not indicate immediate supply impacts. | Abitibi Metals announced strong drill results from its B26 Western Copper-Gold Zone, including 8.16% CuEq over 3.2 meters with reported copper grades and broader mineralized continuity. The step-out expansion de-risks the project and boosts expectations for future copper supply, likely supporting near-term copper sentiment. | Anglo American said Chile’s Collahuasi copper mine will recover toward full capacity by 2027, but output is currently constrained by lower-grade ore and operational issues. The company cautioned near-term production may be lower than expected, adding pressure to a tight market where supply disruption fears are already supporting record copper prices. | BlackRock and other strategists argue copper is surging due to a growing supply deficit (declining ore grades and aging mines) against resilient demand from electrification, EVs, renewables, and AI/data centers. Morgan Stanley’s deficit forecast and claims that copper must reach ~ $12,000/t support expectations of further price strength. | BlackRock says copper is indispensable for the energy transition and AI build-out but that mined ore supply is severely constrained. It argues copper prices may need to rise substantially (toward an incentive level) to fund new mines, expecting a demand-supportive backdrop despite localized construction softness. | Comex copper futures settled 3.04% lower at $5.0780. The move signals bearish near-term price pressure for copper, reflecting weaker sentiment or demand expectations driving settlement losses into the latest trading session. No comparable data is given for nickel or aluminium. | Capstone Copper reported Q3 2025 results showing record adjusted EBITDA driven by higher sulphide output and lower C1 cash costs, with copper production and sales exceeding prior year. The company also sanctioned its Mantoverde expansion and reiterated 2025 guidance, reinforcing positive near-term copper margin and production expectations. | Copper is presented as rallying to a new all-time high above $11,200/ton, with the Indonesian Grasberg mine disaster (mudslide) allegedly shutting ~70% of production indefinitely. The piece also argues ongoing demand growth (data centers, EVs) plus a supply deficit is reinforcing tightness and upside for copper prices.

Panama’s president says the government has strengthened oversight of the Cobre Panamá mining project, with the environment ministry starting an environmental audit while the company hires staff for authorized mine maintenance and partial reactivation. Tensions with First Quantum have eased through dialogue, reducing near-term disruption risk for copper supply. | Ivanhoe Mines said it will start a 6,400-meter copper drilling campaign in Angola by end-2025 on an exploration license acquired in 2023. The base case forecasts sustained copper output (about 108,579 t/year in the first 20 years) and positive economics, with NPV and IRR improving at higher spot copper prices. | China’s non-ferrous metals industry association recommends capping capacity for copper (and others) to curb overbuilding that has crushed smelters’ profitability and processing fees. The conveyor build will be repeated to complete 900 meters over time. | The EPA approved an exemption lifting drinking-water protections for part of the Burro Canyon aquifer, enabling Lisbon Valley Mining’s in-situ recovery expansion at its Utah copper mine. Together with prior BLM approval, the decision supports additional domestic copper supply, though operations still require further filings and monitoring. | The piece argues the US is pushing to build or restart primary copper smelters despite weak global smelting margins. Grupo México’s Hayden restart, Falcon’s new smelter, and expansions at Kennecott and Miami are cited. Policy is supportive: copper is a critical mineral and an EPA rule rollback adds permitting flexibility. | U.S. stock futures rose on strong earnings from Apple and Amazon, with AWS growth driven by AI. The piece argues AI infrastructure build-outs imply higher copper demand for wiring, cooling, and power transmission, alongside generally optimistic capex sentiment. It expects crude oil weakness but highlights copper as a potential beneficiary.

Japan Bank for International Cooperation and other Japanese entities are preparing to finance Pakistan’s Reko Diq copper mine. True North Copper extended the Aquila copper-silver-cobalt discovery at its Mt Oxide project, reporting thick, higher-grade drill intersections and more than 500 metres of strike along the Aquila trend. For aluminium, it highlights critical-minerals policy, tariffs and government funding for new smelting, suggesting firmer deficits and higher prices. | KJZZ reports on Resolution Copper’s stalled Oak Flat project and disputes over whether its future copper will be refined abroad, potentially in China, amid political concerns and tariff threats. The update is exploratory and is not linked to immediate supply changes. | Stuhini Exploration announced a new “Ruffner” copper-gold porphyry target at its Ruby Creek project in British Columbia. The news boosts copper sentiment but does not alter near-term supply. | Chile’s Codelco cut its 2025 copper output forecast to 1.31–1.34 million tonnes from 1.34–1.37 million, despite higher production through September. Lundin Mining reported Q3 2025 results and increased/tightened full-year copper production guidance to 319,000–337,000 tonnes while lowering consolidated copper cash cost guidance to $1.85–$2.00/lb. The update reflects stronger performance at Caserones and Chapada, supporting near-term copper market expectations. | Kenadyr/Algo Grande provides an operational update on the Adelita Cu-Au-Ag project, noting full permits and planned November exploration, alongside progress toward TSXV approval and financing tranches to reactivate the transaction. The article also notes bullish copper fundamentals, citing projected global deficits amid mine disruptions in DRC and Indonesia and rising LME copper prices. | Caravel Minerals and Kutch Copper (Adani subsidiary) signed a non-binding MoU to collaborate on the Caravel Copper Project in Western Australia, including engineering, joint procurement, and concentrate offtake feeding Kutch Copper’s $1.2 billion smelter in Gujarat. It targets an FID in 2026 and aims to accelerate development. | Adani Enterprises said its Kutch Copper Ltd signed a non-binding deal with Australia’s Caravel Minerals to accelerate a copper project in Western Australia, with initial capex of A$1.7 billion and options for equity participation. The announcement is not an immediate production change. | Caravel Minerals and Adani’s Kutch Copper signed a landmark (non-binding) MoU to fast-track Australia’s Caravel Copper Project, targeting an FID in 2026. The release is focused on planning and environmental/water/tailings design, not near-term output changes. | Vedanta launched CopperTech Metals, which will own and operate Zambia’s Konkola Copper Mines. While this can be supportive for sentiment around copper supply chains, the article provides no immediate production or inventory impact.

SMM reports enamelled wire industry operating rates fell in October, citing weak end-use demand and holidays. The deal supports development toward a 2026 FID, lifting copper sentiment despite being preliminary. | The US expanded its critical minerals list to include copper, enabling more federal incentives, permitting and stockpiling decisions tied to domestic supply resilience. CEO.CA interviews Midnight Sun Mining’s leadership, stating it has confirmed copper at the Dumbwa target within a defined anomaly zone and is fast-tracking drilling. EMX Royalty Corp. acquired a 1.25% NSR royalty over the Puquios development-stage copper project in Chile and subscribed to concurrent financing to advance it toward construction and a final investment decision. Much of the discussion instead covers thermal-coal mine prospects in Australia, but the merger narrative has potential implications for copper supply expectations. | China’s customs data show December 2025 copper scrap (HS 74040000) imports rose 14.81% MoM and 9.88% YoY to 239,000 mt, with full-year imports up 4.12% YoY to 2.34 million mt. The company cites a strengthening nickel market and plans a 3,000–4,000 meter program, but provides no immediate supply or inventory impact. | Abitibi Metals announced additional high-grade copper-gold intercepts from Phase 3 drilling at the B26 polymetallic deposit in Québec, including 12.83% CuEq over 5.0 metres. Full-year 2025 data show EQ copper cathode made up 67.54% of China’s total imports, rising year on year. Copper prices surged to an all-time high as investors anticipate AI-driven demand will tighten the market. Copper prices have climbed above $13,000/tonne, driven by sluggish mine output and strong electrification-linked demand, including EVs and renewable energy. Copper jumped to record highs on speculative buying, supported by a weaker dollar and geopolitical risk, though physical demand signals in China remain soft and inventories are high. Despite a stronger second half, the year-on-year decline is modestly bearish for copper sentiment. | Antofagasta said copper output rose 9.4% quarter-on-quarter to 177,000 tonnes in Q4 2025, with copper sales up 42% and full-year net cash costs reduced 27% to $1.19/lb. The release is promotional and lacks output, timing, or near-term supply data. | Shares of Hindustan Copper jumped 20% to a 52-week high, explicitly linked to record copper prices. It links the rise to tighter non-U.S. supply from tariff-driven disruptions and bullish demand expectations, despite weak downstream wire/cable demand and high inventories. | Copper surged above $14,000/ton on the LME, jumping 11% to a record $14,527.50, driven by speculators and short-covering. Alta Copper’s Cañariaco project development could influence future copper supply, but no production changes are stated.

Capstone Copper restarted operations at its Mantoverde copper/gold mine in Chile, while a labor strike continued. The decision followed a Chilean court ruling allowing forced removal of striking workers from the desalination plant that supplies mine water. Capstone expects production at 50%-75% during the strike, limiting shutdown risk. | UK gangs posing as contractors are stealing underground broadband cables, cutting communities’ internet and prompting large repair bills. A copper import ban would reduce supply in the near term and pressure prices lower. | Copper fell about 15% to ~$5.60/lb from a late-January high, reflecting risk-off sentiment. Hydro reports thieves stole nearly 4,000 kilos of copper cable from maintenance holes in Surrey, renewing warnings after similar thefts rose sharply since 2024. Hot Chili says new diamond drilling at its La Verde copper-gold discovery in Chile returned multiple long, high-grade intercepts near surface and indicates a thickening copper porphyry core at depth. Glencore reported swung to annual profit and reiterated momentum for a copper-led growth strategy. It targets annualised copper production of over 1 million tonnes by end-2028 and reached a DRC land-access agreement for Kamoto Copper Co. The operational performance and production plans are supportive for near-term copper market sentiment. | Glencore said it has finalised a land access agreement with Gécamines for Kamoto Copper Co. in the DRC, unlocking long-term mining titles and leases. The deal supports expansion of tailings storage and waste rock dump capacity, enabling an extension of KCC’s life of mine and higher recovery in existing areas. | Zambia’s ZCCM is discussing with Mercuria and others to directly market its share of copper output, shifting from dividend income to active trading and export control. The move parallels DRC’s Gécamines, amid rising demand and IEA warnings of a widening copper supply gap ahead. | Zambia says it is investing billions and securing funding for copper projects, with 2025 output up 8% year-on-year. The company says results strengthen its copper-gold exploration thesis next to Hudbay’s Copper Mountain Mine. The news is exploration-stage and preliminary. | Glencore reported lower adjusted earnings because coal and energy prices slumped, partially offset by stronger metals pricing during a copper boom. The piece notes copper trading near fresh highs, supported by safe-haven demand and a weaker dollar, and highlights Glencore’s new DRC copper lease deal. | Glencore said it reached a land access deal with DRC state firm Gecamines to unlock expanded leased areas for Kamoto Copper Company. The agreement extends KCC’s mine lifespan and allows increased efficiencies, targeting higher copper output to around 300,000 tonnes annually and longer operations into the mid-2040s. | Enduro Metals reports 2025 sampling that identified strongly mineralized gold skarn in bedrock at its October Dome project and expanded a gold-copper target corridor to 5.5 km, with soil copper anomalies up to 0.13%. The release is exploration-focused with no production impact, so near-term copper price effects are likely limited. | Copper surged over 8% to record levels, with LME copper futures peaking around $14,500/ton, driven by AI infrastructure demand and U.S. tariff-related stockpiling that tightened physical availability. The article projects a refined copper deficit into 2026, keeping prices elevated. Aluminium is mentioned mainly as a constrained substitute. | Anglo Asian Mining said it aims to triple copper production in 2026 to 20,000–25,000 tonnes, making copper its primary product, helped by Azerbaijani mines starting production last year. The firm also forecasts higher gold and silver output and provides copper cost guidance of $6,800–$7,800/t. | Glencore’s failed $260 billion Rio Tinto merger is off, but CEO Gary Nagle reiterates willingness to pursue large deals in a copper-hungry market. The firm also reports strong metals performance and progress on expanding output at Kamoto in the DRC, supporting a bullish copper narrative. | Viridian Metals reports MT and mapping results at its Sedna Project in Labrador, showing basin-scale plumbing systems and widespread copper-bearing reduced horizons hosting disseminated chalcopyrite. The company says the model de-risks an emerging sediment-hosted copper district and moves forward with permitting and follow-up exploration at targets. | The Trump DOJ defended the Biden EPA’s 2023 veto blocking the Pebble Mine in Alaska. Blocking it raises near-term copper supply uncertainty, contributing to a sharp drop in Northern Dynasty Minerals’ shares. | Release, a Scatec subsidiary, signed a seven-year lease for a 21 MW solar plant to power Botswana’s Motheo copper mine, supplying about 30% of the site’s electricity needs. Service is expected by end-2026. The move reflects mining’s shift to cleaner power, potentially easing production constraints over time. | DR Congo’s state miner Gecamines has reportedly secured rights to tender and market about half of output from Glencore’s Kamoto Copper Company for at least two years, and 30% thereafter. Reuters could not verify; Glencore didn’t comment. The arrangement affects copper supply flows from the DRC. | Taseko announced strong 2025 results and that Florence Copper’s SX/EW plant has started and is now plating copper cathode, with wellfield injection operating and cathode harvest expected in coming days. The company also guided higher 2026 Gibraltar copper output, supporting improved near-term copper supply expectations.

The piece says BHP’s first-half copper earnings surpassed its iron ore earnings for the first time, aided by a highlighted silver streaming deal and an unexpectedly strong dividend. While details are limited, better-than-expected results tied to copper are likely to support short-term copper sentiment. | Taseko says production of copper cathode has begun at its Florence Copper project in Arizona, with commissioning now complete and first cathode harvest expected soon. The firm also swung to profit on higher fourth-quarter revenue, alongside improved Gibraltar copper output and sales. | Lundin Mining reported updated mineral resource and reserve estimates effective Dec. 31, 2025, with contained copper resources and reserves detailed across its Chile/Brazil assets and the Vicuña project. Changes are driven by resource updates, reserve declassifications, and asset sales. This is a copper supply-sentiment update without immediate production disruption. | Rio Tinto reported flat full-year profit, with stronger copper prices and higher output offsetting weaker iron ore markets. The company’s underlying earnings matched expectations. The report signals copper price strength for Rio’s flagship operations, which can support near-term copper sentiment. | Teck Resources reported profit gains and works to close its Anglo deal. The headline notes that copper prices contributed to lifting its Q4 profit forecast, indicating better near-term copper price expectations that could support copper sentiment in the coming days. | Chile researchers found strains of the cherry bacterial canker pathogen have adapted locally and some populations show greater resistance to copper-based products. The findings suggest current copper controls may be less effective, supporting a shift toward integrated management and alternative measures, potentially softening expectations for copper-treated inputs. | Teck says QB2’s tailings-dam issues in Chile should stop constraining production by year-end, after mitigation work to improve drainage and dam height. The earnings call also reviews ongoing antitrust approvals for Anglo’s acquisition. The piece links the copper price backdrop to broader supply disruptions, implying near-term but mixed copper sentiment. | Selkirk Copper Mines reported new assay results from drilling at its Minto Mine in Yukon, including high-grade copper intervals (up to 3–4% Cu in broader zones and higher grades in short intercepts). The company also updated its Phase 1 drilling progress and restart planning, targeting a longer mine life. | Amarc Resources reported final assay results from 10 holes at the JOY District, including the TWINS discovery, described as a third porphyry copper-gold system with extensive IP-defined lithocap and anomalous Cu-Au intercepts. The company plans further 2026 drilling; no near-term production changes are announced. | Rio Tinto reported flat annual earnings as weak iron ore offset stronger copper results. The copper division doubled earnings, helped by higher realized 2025 copper prices and 11% higher output, including Oyu Tolgoi ramp-up. Aluminium is mentioned only as a smaller earnings contributor without specific drivers. | Glencore said it reached an agreement with Gécamines in the DRC granting land access and long-term mining titles for Kamoto Copper Company (KCC). The deal enables capacity expansions for tailings and waste rock, improves efficiencies, supports a ~300,000 tpa copper output target, and extends mine life into the mid-2040s, subject to cadastre registration. | Sterling Metals has begun a drilling program at the Soo Copper Project north of Sault Ste Marie, targeting at least 20,000 metres this year and aiming to expand on last year’s results. The company frames the project as potentially significant copper discovery, but there’s no immediate production or supply impact. | Rio Tinto reported flat profits as weaker iron ore prices were offset by higher copper prices and increased production. The company said copper realised prices rose 17% with output up 11%, supported by ramping Oyu Tolgoi. Analysts noted China demand concerns but copper-led growth remains in focus.

Newmont reported end-2025 mineral reserves of 118.2M ounces of gold and 12.5M tonnes of copper, with copper reserves slightly down from 2024 mainly due to reclassification of Yanacocha Sulfides from reserves to resources. The update does not mention operational changes that would materially affect near-term copper flows or prices. | Capstone Copper met 2025 targets but delivered weaker 2026 guidance: lower output (200–230kt vs consensus), higher cash costs, and higher capex, driven by grade shortfalls and a Mantoverde strike/shutdown. Brokers may adjust earnings down, even as copper price forecasts were upgraded to cushion the impact. | Delhi Metro Rail Corporation plans to replace 175 km of 33 kV copper wiring with aluminium cables to deter repeated thefts that have disrupted signalling and auxiliary power. The Rs 32.59 crore project replaces existing copper cables with aluminium over 18 months, targeting vulnerable lines and improving reliability. | Delhi Metro Rail Corporation plans to replace about 175 km of 33 kV copper signaling/power cables with aluminium over 18 months to reduce theft-driven outages. The project targets troublesome lines and follows repeated incidents causing signalling failures, speed restrictions, and delays. It reallocates cable demand from copper to aluminium. | Rio Tinto reported weaker overall results, but copper drove performance, with higher realised copper prices and sharply higher volumes after the Oyu Tolgoi ramp-up. BHP also confirmed copper is now its largest earnings driver and lifted copper guidance, reinforcing expectations of tight supply and firmer copper prices. | The piece argues that fast-growing AI data center demand is colliding with a tightening refined copper market, including a predicted 150,000 mt 2026 deficit. It notes rising copper quantities per GW, potential delays in copper-based electrical equipment, and measures like reserves and smelter relief—yet says shortages remain a risk. | Hudbay reported record 2025 revenue and EBITDA and said it met its consolidated copper production guidance, with copper output of 118,188 tonnes. It also outlined 2026 copper guidance and cited temporary operational interruptions. The update may slightly affect near-term copper supply expectations, but likely not move the wider market meaningfully. | Faraday Copper signed a non-binding LOI to acquire BHP’s San Manuel copper property in Arizona, adjacent to its Copper Creek project. The deal would consolidate the assets into a district-scale copper initiative and potentially support expedited cathode production, subject to definitive agreements and approvals. | Faraday Copper signed a non-binding LOI to acquire BHP’s San Manuel copper property in Arizona, including mine deposits and processing sites. The company aims to consolidate adjacent assets to potentially accelerate copper cathode production and strengthen U.S. copper supply. Deal timing is toward Q3 2026, but sentiment is supportive. | Copper has hit record highs, and the interview argues the move may not be temporary. It attributes strength to electrification, EV adoption, renewable expansion, AI data centers, and grid upgrades, suggesting growing expectations of a substantial structural copper shortage ahead, which could keep prices firm. | Copper prices are pulling back from late-January record highs, with the LME three-month contract retreating about 12% to the $12,600–$12,800/t range. The decline is linked to a stronger U.S. dollar, post-tariff profit booking, and China’s Lunar New Year trading slowdown, easing near-term demand. | Hudbay says it has received amended Mines Act and Environmental Management Act permits for the New Ingerbelle expansion at its Copper Mountain mine in British Columbia. The approvals enable advancement of the project, extend mine life, and support higher copper output, improving near-term copper supply expectations and reducing permitting risk. | BHP signed a letter of intent with Faraday Copper to explore selling its San Manuel property in Arizona in exchange for a 30% interest and rights in Faraday. The parties will explore restarting the mine and building a copper hub. Restart expectations are supportive, but the agreement is non-binding. | Lundin Mining reported a record quarter and year, attributing results to realized copper prices near record highs. It is advancing the Vicuña joint venture with BHP, whose staged development could eventually produce large volumes of copper, with a potential sanction decision before year-end. The company says it can fund its share. | Rio Tinto, a major supplier to China, is expected to increase its copper volumes. The piece also discusses weakening demand after the China boom, especially for iron ore, but the explicit near-term operational change is directed at copper, which can influence expectations for copper supply and price. | Anquiro Ventures and Black Pine Resources begin a winter drill program at the Sugarloaf copper property near Tyrone, New Mexico. Permits are secured and up to 11 holes will test shallow oxide secondary copper, with planned Cu/Mo/Ag assays. The release is exploratory and does not affect current supply immediately. | The article discusses Michigan’s proposed Copperwood mine in Gogebic County, intended to produce mostly copper, with permits already secured and potential $250 million Exim financing under consideration. It also notes opposition concerns and failed prior state grants, but emphasizes copper prices are at record highs due to strong demand from renewables and data centers. | Western Copper and Gold filed a final short-form prospectus for a bought deal equity offering to fund its business, including the Casino copper-gold project in Yukon. The note mainly covers financing mechanics, approvals, and prospectus availability, with only general mention that company results depend on copper prices.

Sound Transit reports copper wire thefts disrupting Link light rail service twice in nine days, prompting repair efforts and increased security. Local lawmakers propose HB 2213 to require recyclers to document and hold copper for ten days before selling. The article frames theft as a broader market demand issue, but with no market-wide copper pricing implications. | The episode discusses Zambia’s Copperbelt mining landscape, including regulatory and security comparisons versus the DRC, and highlights key companies and projects. It also mentions Midnight Sun Mining and prior interest by Barrick. The content is informational rather than reporting concrete near-term production or policy shifts impacting copper pricing. | New Frontier Minerals reported updated JORC exploration targets across 14 prospects at its NWQ copper project in Queensland. The targets are conceptual (no defined JORC resource), but include a mining lease review for the Big One deposit and plans to process copper ore via Austral Resources’ Mt Kelly facility. Overall impact on near-term copper prices is limited. | NKT, the power cable maker, has renewed a €6 billion copper supply agreement with KGHM Polska Miedź to secure European-produced copper for 2028–2036. KGHM covers mining and refining/smelting, while NKT expands capacity for cable demand. The long-dated deal supports supply security more than immediate price pressure. | Communities in Zambia’s Copperbelt say renewed mining has brought pollution and social harm, after a February 2025 acid spill at a Chinese-owned leaching operation contaminated the Kafue River watershed. While copper demand from renewables is rising, activists and unions warn environmental risks could disrupt supply and raise regulatory pressure. | Faraday Copper is negotiating a non-binding deal to acquire BHP’s San Manuel property in Arizona, with plans to study restarting the copper mine and creating an integrated Arizona copper hub using existing infrastructure. If approvals and due diligence progress, it could boost expectations for US copper supply, supporting prices. | British Columbia accepted Surge Copper’s Berg Project into its Critical Minerals Office, providing coordinated provincial support to streamline regulation. The Berg Project contains a copper-molybdenum-silver deposit and is expected to primarily produce copper with byproducts. This improves sentiment around future copper supply and regulatory progress, supporting copper prices modestly. | Queensland has allocated A$15m via QIC to refurbish Austral Resources’ Rocklands copper sulphide processing facility near Cloncurry. The project targets restoring production capabilities after the 2024 care-and-maintenance period, with output resuming later as part of a broader Queensland resources reinvigoration strategy. | Faraday Copper announced a non-brokered private placement raising up to C$100m, with participation by the Lundin Family Trusts and BHP. Proceeds will be used to advance its Arizona copper projects, including integration of the San Manuel property from BHP, subject to regulatory approvals. | Koryx Copper reported assay results from 13 infill/expansion drill holes at the Haib copper project in Namibia, highlighting generally stronger-than-expected copper intercepts and continued advancement toward an updated MRE and a PFS in late 2026. The news is supportive for project fundamentals but unlikely to shift near-term copper markets materially. | Copper surged to a record $14,268/t on Jan 29, then reversed to about $12,850/t by late February. Drivers cited include U.S. tariff front-loading and speculative long positioning unwind, plus stronger dollar and profit-taking after mining disruptions. Goldman warns of a deeper mid-2026 correction toward $11,000–$11,200/t. | British Columbia approved permits for the New Ingerbelle expansion at the Copper Mountain copper-gold mine near Princeton, extending operations by 12 years. The province projects about 750,000 tonnes of copper over the extended lifespan, supporting jobs and local activity, which adds expected supply pressure to copper markets.

British Columbia has approved Hudbay Minerals’ Copper Mountain (New Ingerbelle) expansion, extending mine operations to at least 2040 and potentially increasing annual copper production by up to 90% over a three-stage project. Environmental groups opposed the plan, but permits were issued after the proposal met assessment thresholds. | B.C. approved Hudbay Minerals’ Copper Mountain “New Ingerbelle” expansion in southern Interior, extending mine operations to at least 2040 and potentially increasing annual copper production by up to 90% over a three-stage plan. The decision follows regulatory reviews and environmental concerns about tailings. | SMM notes China’s copper concentrate import mix is shifting toward land-transported supplies from nearby Central/Southeast Asian countries, with land share rising from ~11.5% (2019) to 15% (2025). The diversification and shorter logistics are expected to improve concentrate access for Chinese smelters, supporting copper supply outlook. | True North Copper secured a $400,000 Queensland grant to sample and metallurgically test legacy waste at its Mt Oxide copper project.
