Results are uncertain, but sentiment is supportive for copper. | Fathom Nickel reported completion of a winter access trail and mobilization for a 3,000–4,000 m drilling program at the Gochager Lake Ni-Cu project, expected to start in early March. The release outlines multiple high-priority targets and geochemical results, but provides no production or finalized resource changes. | Barrick Gold approved preparations for an IPO/spinoff of its North American assets while increasing focus on copper. The article says copper is ~30% of Barrick profits and points to expansions/financing for projects like Lumwana and Reko Diq. Such updates can support near-term copper demand and sentiment. | Copper prices surged to record levels near $14,500 as AI data-center demand collided with supply shocks: a 35-day Chile mine strike and force majeure/output cuts from Indonesia’s Grasberg. Prices remain highly volatile after trade-related tariff developments, with potential aluminum substitution easing some downstream demand. | EU officials expect the U.S. to scale back tariffs on goods containing steel and aluminum within weeks, easing transatlantic trade friction. Tariffs on commodity-grade metal forms are expected to remain. The development could improve near-term aluminum demand and trade expectations, though copper is not directly referenced. | Ero Copper shares rose after a preliminary economic assessment for Vale-owned Furnas in Brazil, outlining a large-scale mine with estimated early-life copper production and multi-year development milestones. While supportive for future copper supply expectations, it does not indicate imminent output changes, limiting near-term price pressure. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reported assay results from its Kenbridge nickel-copper-cobalt project, including 3.0m grading 2.17% nickel and 1.45% copper. The company is continuing an infill/drill program to expand and improve deposit continuity, with additional assays pending. No production or supply changes were announced. | The letter urges Michigan regulators not to grant permits for the proposed Copperwood Mine by Highland Copper, citing risks of clear-cutting forests, damaging wetlands, rerouting streams, and drawing water from Lake Superior. It frames the project as environmentally harmful and implies potential permitting delays or denial, which could tighten copper supply expectations. | The piece frames copper’s outlook as increasingly driven by fear, geopolitics, and policy-driven trade barriers. It cites tariffs and deglobalization prompting sourcing uncertainty and strategic stockpiling, alongside AI/electrification demand growth. It also points to e-waste recycling as a future supply source but emphasizes immediate anxiety shaping prices. | The podcast transcript discusses what’s driving copper, emphasizing that global uncertainty can translate into investor flows and stockpiling. It also notes US policy ambiguity around copper tariffs and potential stockpile actions (Project Vault) that can tighten available metal and keep premiums elevated, supporting firmer copper price expectations despite near-term supply comfort. | Comex Copper front-month February contract settled 2.60% higher at $5.9230, gaining 15 cents per pound. The move is the largest one-day dollar and percentage gain since Feb. 18 and follows mixed recent sessions. It remains below key recent highs but above prior settlements and lows. | Jack Lundin discusses conditions he says are lifting copper demand, despite heightened market volatility. He argues the drivers supporting copper prices remain intact and highlights Lundin Mining’s Vicuña project in South America as potentially making it a top producer. Overall, the interview is framed as copper-positive.

Kazakhstan produced 471,000 tons of refined copper in 2025 (+1.5% y/y) but exports fell 8.3% in Jan–Nov, driven by sharply lower shipments to China. Türkiye and other markets gained share. The Eurasian Commission approved a temporary duty exemption on copper intermediates, lowering input costs for domestic producers. | The article argues India’s copper demand from EVs and chargers could surge while supply lags, as India imports most of its copper and domestic output is stagnant. It also cites US Section 232 tariffs and inventory build-ups, lifting copper prices, and warns shortages could raise battery costs and slow EV targets. | FTTH Council Europe’s 2026 update tracks progress across 27 European countries in switching off copper broadband networks in favor of full-fibre FTTP/H/B. Results vary widely, with some countries far ahead and others lacking data. The copper phase-out is gradual, so near-term copper price effects are likely limited. | Copper prices have fallen from January records as visible exchange inventories surge to a 23-year high (~1.1m tonnes across LME/SHFE/COMEX). The buildup is attributed to U.S. tariff-driven front-loading and early-2026 inflows to LME warehouses, reducing the tightness premium and cooling the physical bid. | Base metals rallied after Chinese markets reopened and a U.S. Supreme Court decision limited emergency powers for broad tariffs. Morgan Stanley projected U.S. levies on Chinese goods falling from 32% to ~24%. Copper surged 2.8% with a 60% rise in Yangshan premium; aluminum rose ~1% as trade uncertainty eased. | B.C. issued Mines Act and Environmental Management Act permits for the New Ingerbelle expansion of the Copper Mountain Mine near Princeton in smǝlqmíx territory. The project revives the century-old Ingerbelle open pit gold and copper site and extends operations until 2047, pending Indigenous concerns. | First Quantum plans to invest about $5.25 billion to develop the Taca Taca copper mine in Argentina under the RIGI incentive regime, targeting 291,000 tonnes of copper annually in the first decade. The project is progressing through environmental assessment and incentives, but near-term production changes are not immediate. | Copper surged to all-time highs above $13,000/t on the LME, driven by a “perfect storm” of AI-driven demand and tightened supply. The article cites mine outages (e.g., Grasberg mudrush), labor strikes at Escondida, tariff-driven inventory pull-forward, and a deficit near 400,000 tons. | Prospect Ridge reports assay results from its 100% owned Camelot copper-gold project in B.C., highlighting intersections such as CAM25-009 averaging ~0.08% Cu over 156.6 m, plus other holes with weaker Cu-Au mineralization. The release discusses geology and targeting criteria, with CuEq estimates based on assumed copper prices. | World Copper Ltd. signed a definitive property option to acquire a 100% interest in the Brassie Creek porphyry-skarn copper-gold project in British Columbia, subject to payments, exploration expenditures, and a 2% net smelter returns royalty. The company plans steps toward project evaluation, supporting copper-linked sentiment.

Gunnison Copper Corp. released an updated NI 43-101 PEA for its Gunnison oxide copper heap-leach/SX-EW project in Arizona. The study highlights 174 mln lbs/year of 99.999% copper cathode for 15 years, lower costs, and higher NPV8% at a $4.60/lb copper deck, with project optimizations driving the increase. | Kincora Copper Limited requests a trading halt on ASX while it prepares an announcement about a planned capital raising. The halt is to help manage continuous disclosure obligations. The release provides no details on production impacts or supply changes, indicating limited direct linkage to copper price fundamentals. | The Autonomous Bougainville Government increased its control of Bougainville Copper Limited to 72.9% and says it will ensure redevelopment of the Panguna copper project proceeds lawfully and transparently. The government lacks technical and financial capacity to advance mining independently, but greater control may improve supply outlook. | Press Metal Aluminium reported a 32% jump in FY25 4Q profit and expects steady FY26 performance, citing strengthening aluminium prices and easing alumina input costs. Management attributes resilience to stable demand, low inventories, supply constraints, and a favourable copper-to-aluminium ratio amid evolving trade policies. | The article discusses Bell Potter’s bullish view on Aeris Resources, an ASX copper-dominant producer. It cites a financial update showing earnings leverage to rising copper (and gold) prices and expects production growth. While it’s stock-focused, the stated outlook is tied directly to near-term copper-price expectations. | Eskay Mining announced its 2026 exploration plans at the Consolidated Eskay Project in British Columbia, including drilling to test targets with porphyry copper/gold potential near the Kyba Line. The company secured camp and drilling capacity and is refining borehole locations and interpreting historical datasets to guide fieldwork, with drilling expected in 2026. | Tinka reported initial diamond drilling results from its Silvia copper-gold project, highlighting copper-bearing intervals and planning follow-up geophysics over a wider target area. The copper exploration news may sway sentiment, but no immediate supply impact is indicated. | Questcorp Mining begins a 2026 induced polarization (IP) survey at its North Island Copper Project to confirm and refine historical targets in the Marisa Zone porphyry copper system. The program aims to improve subsurface modeling ahead of a potential future drill program; no production or sales changes are cited. | Police in Coldwater, Michigan are investigating a copper heist at American Copper and Brass. Nearly $100,000 in copper coils and scrap were stolen, using a forklift to load materials onto a stolen U-Haul trailer. Officials noted higher copper prices around $6 per pound make tracking harder after melting. | Kutcho Copper announced the closing of a C$3.0 million flow-through financing to fund exploration on its Kutcho copper-zinc property in British Columbia. Proceeds are intended for Canadian exploration and flow-through critical mineral expenditures. The release includes standard financing and regulatory language, with no direct near-term production or supply change. | Comex copper front-month (March delivery) settled 0.64% lower at $5.9470, down 3.85 cents per pound and ending a two-session winning streak. Prices are also slightly below recent highs, with the contract off 3.7% from a 2026 high earlier in the month. | A UEW lecturer warns Ghana’s EPA copper-nanoliquid pilot for the polluted Birim River could unintentionally introduce or increase copper toxicity, citing unpredictable behavior in sediment-laden waters and risks of exceeding the 2.0 mg/L drinking-water standard. He calls for rigorous real-world monitoring and precautionary evaluation.

Kincora Copper Limited raised A$4 million via a placement at A$1.05 per CDI to fund expanded near-term drilling and geophysics at its Condobolin, Fairholme, Trundle, and Jemalong projects. The company’s exploration focus includes copper-containing porphyry and lens targets, but no production or supply change is described. | Sound Transit is asking King County prosecutors to pursue copper wire thieves after repeated thefts disrupted light rail, including incidents affecting the new 1 Line extension. Officials seek tougher scrap-yard controls, including a proposed photo-database requirement that would have enabled police seizures. | King Copper Discovery Corp. reported appointments to its Peruvian Colquemayo project team and participation at PDAC, alongside plans to reprocess historical core and pursue a fully financed 35,000-metre drill program. The project targets copper-gold-silver mineralization with past high-grade copper intercepts, but no production or supply change is announced.

Hudbay Minerals agreed to acquire the remaining shares of Arizona Sonoran Copper in a $1.48 billion all-share deal, closing in Q2 2026. The transaction expands Hudbay’s Arizona copper assets, strengthening its U.S. growth pipeline, but it is unlikely to change copper supply tightness within days. | Visionary Copper and Gold Mines reported Phase I drilling results at the Pt. Leamington VMS project, including discovery of the Kraken copper-rich zone. Highlights include 75.8m grading 0.45% Cu and additional 23.5m grading 0.43% Cu, plus broader higher-grade copper intersections elsewhere. Step-out drilling continues to define the zone. | Copper prices have reversed sharply after an early-January peak driven by supply constraints and U.S.-tariff-related pre-emptive stockpiling. The article cites collapsing TC/RC, demand destruction, and a Goldman forecast of a deeper mid-2026 correction toward $11,000–$11,200, with some buyers switching toward aluminum. | Capstone Copper reported record Q4 2025 results, with higher copper output and record-low C1 cash costs, plus 2026 guidance of 200,000–230,000 tonnes. It also said a Mantoverde union strike that began January 2, 2026 ended after a new three-year agreement was signed February 5.

Aluminium ended lower as a stronger dollar and a widening Middle East conflict against Iran pressured commodity prices. The closure of Hormuz earlier had boosted aluminium, but risk persisted and producers paused premiums. Copper and nickel also weakened, reflecting broad base-metal pressure from higher oil/gas inflation fears. | Taruga Minerals is reprocessing old aeromagnetic data to build a refined 3D geologic framework for its Kol Mountain porphyry copper-gold project in Papua New Guinea, aiming to rank drill targets and constrain drilling. Historic results showed copper grades and supergene enrichment, supporting mildly bullish copper sentiment. | A US-Israel strike on Iran raises geopolitical disruption risk and may increase freight and insurance costs affecting global copper concentrate sentiment. But Iran is a minor supplier (about 1.8–2.0% share globally) and provides only ~0.1% of China’s imports. With diversified sourcing and limited mine disruption, copper impact is expected limited. | Capstone Copper’s announcement (as indicated by the title) points to record production and firmer guidance, improving confidence in its operating outlook and growth prospects. Stronger-than-expected output can lift expectations for near-term copper supply and generally supports copper price sentiment over coming days. | Taseko Mines reports the first copper cathodes from its Florence operation in Arizona, describing it as the first new U.S. copper output from a greenfield project since 2008. The milestone adds supply expectations for the near term, generally supportive for copper market sentiment.

An alleged thief cut an 11,000-volt cable at an Ergon Energy substation near Brisbane and suffered serious burns. The report says Ergon had replaced copper with aluminium at the site due to copper theft, and around 800 customers lost power briefly. The event is localized and mainly operational. | Copper’s historic rally abruptly reversed on March 4, with LME prices falling nearly 10% as investors de-risk amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. The blockade boosts energy costs and interrupts sulfur supply for SX-EW, raising costs while geopolitics lifts war-risk premiums. Demand-growth fears and de-grossing deepen the bearish move. | Debswana approved plans to diversify beyond diamonds amid a demand slump, including expanding copper and platinum projects via Naledi Mining Services and developing a solar farm near the Jwaneng mine. MainPower in North Canterbury says copper thefts from power poles have exceeded 200 incidents this year, prompting a $5,000 reward for information. Abitibi Metals secured 80% ownership of the B26 high-grade copper-gold polymetallic project in Quebec through early option exercise with SOQUEM. Police in Mulund arrested nine people for allegedly posing as MTNL workers and stealing underground copper cables using a crane and labourers. Power Metal Resources plans to invest US$1 million (2.6% stake) in Chile’s Next Minerals to fund development of the mine-ready Comahue underground copper project in Antofagasta. Directors cite phased work programmes and optionality. The announcement supports copper exploration activity, but is unlikely to affect short-term copper prices materially. | The piece cites analysis from The Conversation that copper demand is rising with new technologies while supply struggles to keep up due to financing risk, permitting complexity, and labor constraints. If investment proceeds or signals strong near-term copper consumption, it can boost expectations for copper demand and prices over the coming days to weeks. The rest of the provided content is cookie/privacy text.

Scotiabank argues copper fundamentals are bending but not broken, maintaining a sizable deficit outlook while updating for weaker near-term demand and higher fuel-cost impacts from the escalating Middle East crisis. Despite valuation discounts in copper miners, the note cautions copper prices may not have bottomed, suggesting near-term bearish pressure. | Copper prices have surged above $14,500 on the LME amid a cited 330,000 tonne global supply deficit, tight warehouse inventories, and production disruption from Indonesia’s Grasberg mine. The piece expects demand to remain largely inelastic short term due to electrification and AI data-center buildouts. | The piece argues that copper prices are high while smelter processing economics have broken: treatment charges have fallen to near $0 and China smelters plan a 10% output cut. A potential continued concentrate deficit threatens refined cathode supply, supporting a bullish outlook for copper in coming weeks. | NGEx reported high-grade drill results from its Lunahuasi copper-gold-silver project in Argentina, including 335 metres grading 2.25% copper and standout intervals such as 6.83% copper and a 21.65% copper bonanza sample. The update supports expectations of resource upside, boosting copper-focused sentiment. | Freeport-McMoRan CEO Kathleen Quirk said copper demand for electrification, data centers and high-tech uses should remain resilient despite market jitters linked to the Iran conflict. She argued “Dr. Copper” drivers are largely secular. Copper has dropped nearly 10% since late-Feb strikes, but outlook stays constructive. | Tulsa County deputies arrested two men for allegedly cutting wire from a telephone pole and charging them with larceny of copper and conspiracy. The report is limited to a local theft probe with no indication of wider supply loss, production impact, or market-wide effects on copper prices.

Faraday Copper reported results from Phase IV drilling at the Copper Creek Project in Arizona, highlighting multiple near-surface copper intercepts at the American Eagle area and an expanded northeast extension at Keel. Drilling is ongoing, aimed at improving open-pit potential and collecting hydrogeological and geotechnical data. | The piece claims China’s state-backed overcapacity in copper smelting and dumping has given it dominant fabrication control and recently pushed bulk copper prices to 16-year highs. It calls for U.S. measures—new tariffs, export controls, and scrap feedstock restrictions—plus reliance on foreign refined supply until U.S. smelting returns. | Commentary argues global refined copper supply is tightening, citing analysts’ forecasts for a 2026 refined deficit above 150,000 tonnes. It attributes demand growth to grid electrification, AI data centers and defense spending, plus sovereign “critical minerals” funding, and references copper-focused drilling updates. | Copper has entered a technical bear market after peaking in late January, with LME prices down nearly 20% to around $11,700. The sell-off is tied to China’s price rejection, swelling Shanghai/LME inventories, plummeting treatment charges to near zero, and mandated smelter production cuts—reinforcing bearish near-term sentiment. | Comex copper front-month futures for March delivery settled 0.31% lower at $5.4225 per pound, losing 1.70 cents. The contract fell eight of the past 10 sessions, placing today’s settlement among the lowest of the year, and remains down month-to-date and year-to-date. | The piece forecasts copper prices staying high as electrification, EVs, and AI data-center infrastructure raise demand while physical inventories remain low and a future production deficit is expected. It highlights investment momentum in Latin American copper projects and potential implications of Panama’s Cobre Panamá mine reopening and US-China trade uncertainty.

Portland Police arrested a key suspect in an organized copper theft ring that targeted communication wiring and other infrastructure since May 2025. Investigators with Lumen Technologies traced stolen copper into recycling facilities and payments. Subpoenas were issued to recycling businesses and the case remains active, disrupting local theft and scrap flows. | Portland Police report arrests in an organized copper theft operation that targeted communication lines and underground cabling for scrap. Investigators worked with Lumen Technologies to track stolen copper through recycling facilities. Authorities say the scheme processed over $487,000 in metal and are pursuing additional recycling-business involvement. | Portland police arrested a key suspect in a copper theft operation that cut communication lines and sold copper for scrap, disrupting emergency communications and local infrastructure. The probe involved recycling facilities and partners like Lumen Technologies. Authorities issued subpoenas to major recyclers, with the investigation ongoing. | Portland police arrested a key suspect in an organized copper theft ring that cut communication lines and sold copper wiring and underground cabling for scrap. Authorities worked with Lumen Technologies and recycling facilities. The investigation is ongoing and subpoenas target two recycling businesses, but impacts appear localized. | Portland police arrested five suspects tied to a copper wire theft operation, accusing them of stealing communications cabling and recycling the metal for profit. The case also targets a broker who allegedly processed ~$487,000 of copper and includes subpoenas for recycling businesses, potentially tightening illegal scrap flows. | Portland Police arrested a suspect tied to an organized copper theft operation that cut communication lines and sold stolen copper wiring to recycling facilities. Investigators worked with Lumen Technologies to track cables and dismantle the broker-led pipeline. Authorities say the theft disrupted 911 and critical infrastructure, and subpoenas target recycling businesses. | Clark County in Nevada is replacing stolen streetlight copper wire with aluminum and adding “Aluminum Wire Only” stickers, weld-capped poles, and additional electrician shifts. The effort targets copper wire theft for scrap resale. Local repairs face material shortages, but overall substitution impact appears limited for markets. | HC Wainwright reiterated a “buy” rating and $5.75 price objective for Western Copper and Gold, a Canada-based explorer focused on the Casino copper-gold deposit. The piece mainly reports stock and institutional ownership changes, without new production, project, or policy details that would directly affect near-term copper supply expectations. | Aftermath Silver released initial Phase 3 drilling assays from its Berenguela silver-copper-manganese project in Peru, reporting multiple high-grade, near-surface intervals with % copper (up to ~2.2% Cu over 48.5m) and strong silver-copper association. The results bolster confidence in copper-bearing resource potential. | Selkirk Copper reported new drill assay results from the Minto mine, extending high-grade mineralization at Minto North and Minto East and expanding near-surface potential at Ridgetop and Area 118. Copper grades include multi-percent Cu intercepts, supporting ongoing development and restart planning for the project. | Selkirk Copper reported additional assay results from its Phase 1 drilling program at the Minto copper-gold-silver mine in Yukon, including high-grade copper intersections (bornite/chalcopyrite lenses) and expanded drill-defined mineralization. The update supports potential resource growth and future redevelopment planning but does not indicate immediate production impact. | Takela Mining Tanzania, a Marula subsidiary, signed a purchase contract with Traxys to sell 100% of copper ore production from the Kinusi Copper Mine in Tanzania. Traxys holds exclusive rights until Dec 2029, with deliveries starting May 2026 and pricing tied to the LME copper price. | The Trump administration cleared legal steps for the Resolution Copper project at Oak Flat, ending a religious freedom dispute that challenged land transfer. The Justice Department said the move enables development of a major copper deposit, but the mine is not expected to open until the mid-2030s, limiting near-term price impact.

Copper Fox reported Q1 2026 financial results and progress on multiple copper projects, including advancing Van Dyke toward PFS with groundwater modeling and drilling/updated geophysics at Schaft Creek, Mineral Mountain, Eaglehead, and Sombrero Butte. It also noted a private placement, but no near-term production or supply shock. | Aton reports initial results from an Abu Marawat RC infill drill program in Egypt, highlighting polymetallic intersections that include copper grades around 0.36–0.82% along with gold and silver. The company says metallurgical work suggests recoverability of gold, silver, copper, and zinc, supporting improved project outlook. | Police arrested five alleged copper thieves after a speeding stop on South Africa’s N2. Officers found over 200kg of copper, later identified as part of Telkom and Eskom infrastructure. Authorities linked the arrests to copper theft disruptions, though the seized amount is small versus market scale, likely limiting price impact. | A Bulawayo man was granted bail after being arrested for allegedly possessing 32kg of copper cables cut into pieces, without permits, under Zimbabwe’s Electricity Act. Police seized the copper and vehicle, and the trial is set for March 27. The case highlights ongoing copper theft disrupting electricity infrastructure. | Short interest in the iShares Copper and Metals Mining ETF (ICOP) fell 55.2% in March, with the ETF trading down 4.8% on the day. The piece focuses on ETF positioning and trading metrics, without citing new fundamentals affecting copper, nickel, or aluminium markets directly. | Western Copper and Gold reported 2025 annual results and a post-year-end $92 million financing, alongside progress on the permitting sufficiency review for its Casino copper-gold project in Yukon. The Government of Yukon also announced measures to reduce mine permitting delays. The update is supportive but not a clear near-term copper supply shock.

Industrial metals prices are surging as Middle East geopolitical tensions disrupt energy and transport, driving higher smelting costs and forcing Aluminium Bahrain to declare force majeure on parts of its production. The piece also highlights AI data-center buildouts requiring large copper tonnages, reinforcing demand expectations alongside supply constraints. | Norfolk OPP is investigating after suspects knocked down utility poles in Simcoe and stole copper telecommunications and grounding wires. Residents lost phone and internet service while crews repaired damage. The report says copper theft is frequent nationally and similar incidents have occurred across Ontario, including prior arrests earlier this year. | Copper futures are retreating from record highs, trading around $12,305/ton on the LME. The piece highlights a persistent supply deficit but growing expectations of a surplus via inventory dynamics, scrap recycling, and possible demand destruction. It flags a June 30 U.S. refined-copper tariff review as a catalyst for further downside. | The piece contends global copper demand will rise sharply from electrification, grid expansion, AI data centers, and defense, while new mine supply is slow and constrained by limited discoveries, underinvestment, permitting delays, and long development timelines. It cites IEA and S&P projections of future shortfalls, implying a near-term bullish copper outlook. | A Wasco man was arrested in Kern County after allegedly operating an unlicensed recycling scheme and being found with about 175 pounds of stolen copper wire. Authorities said copper wire thefts from agricultural equipment have risen, and the suspect recycled over 3,200 pounds within two months.

Botswana Minerals says it has begun the first phase of work on its copper licences in Ngamiland, Botswana, using AI and integrated geophysical/satellite data to rank targets for future drilling. The company plans to refine survey priorities and mentions ongoing JV discussions, but no production or near-term supply impacts. | Metso signed a €100 million agreement with Southern Peru Copper for delivery of copper solvent extraction and electrowinning (SX-EW) technology for the Tia Maria project in Peru. The plant is planned to produce 120,000 tonnes of LME grade A copper cathodes annually, with orders booked for 2026 Q1. | Canada and Quebec are reportedly working with Glencore to prevent the shutdown of the Horne Smelter in Quebec, the country’s only copper smelter. Glencore threatened to close the facility over new pollution-control requirements, but governments are attempting to secure a deal to keep operations running. | Copper prices fell from an all-time intraday high near $14,500/t in January 2026 to about $12,250/t on the LME by late March as China buyers pause. Goldman forecasts an 18% correction toward ~$11,000 by year-end, citing cooling demand and a 2026 surplus. | Lundin Mining and BHP’s Filo del Sol project in northwest Argentina reportedly has a fivefold increase in estimated resources, including up to about 13 million tonnes of copper from additional exploration drilling. While extraction faces altitude and potential Glacier Law/environmental scrutiny, the copper resource upgrade is sentiment-positive. | Vale Base Metals reported 2025 increases in copper and nickel mineral reserves and resources and outlined 2026 exploration plans, including doubled drilling intensity in Brazil’s Carajás copper district. The update is supportive for long-term supply potential but contains no immediate production, export, or cost shock.

WTI rises above $100 amid renewed Middle East tensions, increasing fuel-cost and inflation concerns. Iranian strikes are reported as hitting major aluminium producers in the region, lifting aluminium about 6% toward $3,500/t. Conversely, high energy and rates are seen pressuring industrial demand; copper has fallen ~7% in the month. | The piece says copper spot treatment charges have plunged to historic lows (around -70 USD/dmt) as smelters pay miners, but sulfuric acid prices have surged due to Middle East shipping disruptions. The higher acid byproduct revenue helps maintain Chinese cathode output and may keep copper concentrate TC pressure from worsening quickly. | Datavault AI and Coppercore announced a definitive agreement to tokenize high-grade copper resources into “Coppercoin” digital tokens, with pricing tied to the COMEX copper benchmark. The initial program targets $100 million+ in tokens, representing pro-rata ownership in in-ground copper, with planned launch by Q2 2026. | Copper trades near $12,220/mt despite a 23-year high inventory, because investors treat stockpiles as earmarked while AI data centers drive exceptional copper intensity. It also references the Grasberg mine shock, government “Project Vault” strategic reserve planning, and an upcoming deficit forecast, supporting bullish near-term pricing. | Comex Copper April front-month settled at $5.5875, down 0.75% over the quarter and down 6.94% for the month, snapping a seven-month winning streak. However, it gained 2.04% on the day after posting the largest one-day gain since Feb. 24, reaching the highest settlement since March 17. | Ivanhoe Mines reported updated Kamoa-Kakula mineral reserves/resources supporting long-life copper production. Mine plan updates revise 2026 guidance to 290,000–330,000 tonnes of copper anodes and 2027 to 380,000–420,000 tonnes, alongside lower 2026-27 C1 cash cost ranges. Ramp above 500,000 tpa is targeted from 2028. | Austin police arrested a man accused of stealing copper wire from streetlights, prompting Austin Energy to warn the public about ongoing copper theft. The utility says repairs divert staff and supplies and can be costly, with thieves sometimes targeting transformers and energized equipment. The story is localized and doesn’t cite market-wide impacts. | Glencore is seeking Canadian federal funding to modernize its Quebec copper smelter (Horne) and Montreal refinery (CCR). The firm paused major investments after failing to reach provincial terms and warned it could shut the plants. A federal response could reduce shutdown risk and support copper processing capacity.

Police in Montgomery County arrested three suspects in a copper wire theft case, saying thieves stole and scrapped about 15,000 pounds of copper wire valued around $400,000 from a utility contractor over months. Police attribute the copper thefts to burglary and trespassing and highlight scrapping proceeds of nearly $50,000. | Spot copper treatment charges in China have fallen to historic lows (about -70 USD/dmt), threatening smelter profitability. Smelters are still running high output as surging sulfuric acid prices offset TC losses. Geopolitical risks around Hormuz/Red Sea may keep acid prices high, supporting copper supply and near-term price tone. | The podcast episode focuses on copper and how potential sulphur supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz could constrain copper production, particularly in the DRC and Zambia’s Copperbelt. It also highlights how higher energy prices may pressure producers and how macro slowdown risks could weigh on prices. | Glenstar Minerals received BLM approval for a 13-hole reverse circulation drill program and 2,200 feet of road construction at its Wildhorse copper-rich, polymetallic project in Nevada. The company highlights prior anomalous nickel-copper mineralization and copper oxide/sulfide zones, but no new assay results are provided. | Mongoose Mining reported 2025 drill results from the Mount Thom IOCG project, confirming multiple hydrothermal copper systems with breccia-hosted zones and broad disseminated copper intervals, including a 45-metre enriched section and a 4.98% Cu intercept. The update is exploration-focused with no immediate supply impact.
